source: It is the custom on the stage, in all good murderous melodramas, to present the tragic and the comic scenes, in as regular alternation, as the layers of red and white in a side of streaky bacon. The hero sinks upon his straw bed, weighed down by fetters and misfortunes; in the next scene, his faithful but unconscious squire regales the audience with a comic song. We behold, with throbbing bosoms, the heroine in the grasp of a proud and ruthless baron: her virtue and her life alike in danger, drawing forth her dagger to preserve the one at the cost of the other; and just as our expectations are wrought up to the highest pitch, a whistle is heard, and we are straightway transported to the great hall of the castle; where a grey-headed seneschal sings a funny chorus with a funnier body of vassals, who are free of all sorts of places, from church vaults to palaces, and roam about in company, carolling perpetually.
domestication: 在舞台上，所有优秀的杀人剧里，悲剧和喜剧场景的交替就像五花肉上红白相间的层次一样规律。英雄被铁链和不幸压得倒在草垫上；下一幕，他忠诚但无知的随从却用一首滑稽的歌曲逗乐观众。我们心跳加速地看着女主角落入一个傲慢无情的领主之手，她的贞操和生命都危在旦夕，她拔出匕首，准备以生命换贞操；就当我们紧张到极点时，一阵口哨声响起，转眼间我们就被带到城堡的大厅，看到一位白发总管正和一群更搞笑的家臣唱着欢快的歌。这些家臣从教堂地下室到宫殿都来去自如，成群结队地四处游荡，唱个不停。
foreignization: 在所有优秀的凶杀剧中，舞台上有一种惯例，那就是像一块五花肉的红白层一样规律地交替呈现悲剧和喜剧场景。英雄因戴着镣铐和遭受不幸而倒在他的稻草床上；在下一幕中，他忠诚但未察觉的随从以一首滑稽的歌曲娱乐观众。我们怀着怦怦跳的心看到女主角被一个骄傲无情的男爵抓住：她的贞操和生命同样受到威胁，抽出匕首以牺牲一个来保全另一个；正当我们的期待被推至最高点时，一声哨响，我们立刻被带到了城堡的大厅；在那里，一位灰发的管家与一群更为滑稽的家臣一起唱着有趣的合唱，这些家臣自由出入各种场所，从教堂地窖到宫殿，成群结队地四处游荡，不断欢歌。

source: Strokes, thick and heavy, rattled upon the door and lower window-shutters as he ceased to speak, and a loud huzzah burst from the crowd; giving the listener, for the first time, some adequate idea of its immense extent.
domestication: 他话音刚落，门和窗户上就响起了沉重的敲击声，人群爆发出震耳欲聋的欢呼声，这才让听者第一次真切感受到这人群的浩大声势。
foreignization: 粗重的敲门声和拍打低窗板的声音在他停止说话时响起，人群中爆发出一阵响亮的欢呼声；这让听众第一次对人群的庞大程度有了些充分的认识。

source: But his heart was heavy, notwithstanding; and he wished, as he crept into his narrow bed, that that were his coffin, and that he could be lain in a calm and lasting sleep in the churchyard ground, with the tall grass waving gently above his head, and the sound of the old deep bell to soothe him in his sleep.
domestication: 尽管如此，他心里还是沉甸甸的。钻进窄小的床时，他想，要是这张床就是他的棺材该多好，能够安安静静地长眠在教堂的墓地里，高高的草在他头顶轻轻摇曳，古老的钟声缓缓响起，伴着他进入梦乡。
foreignization: 但是他的心情依然沉重；当他钻进狭窄的床时，他希望那张床就是他的棺材，希望自己能够躺在教堂墓地的土地下，沉睡在平静而永恒的睡眠中，高高的草在他的头上轻轻摇曳，古老的深钟声在他 sleeps 时安慰他。

source: “Let him alone!” said Noah. “Why everybody lets him alone enough, for the matter of that. Neither his father nor his mother will ever interfere with him. All his relations let him have his own way pretty well. Eh, Charlotte? He! he! he!”
domestication: “别理他！”诺亚说道，“其实大家早就够不搭理他的了。他爹妈从来不管他，亲戚们也都随他去。是不是啊，夏洛特？呵呵呵！”
foreignization: “让他去吧！”诺亚说。“其实大家都已经够让他自由的了。他的父亲和母亲永远不会干涉他。所有的亲戚也都随他去。是吧，夏洛特？嘿！嘿！嘿！”

“Let him alone!” said Noah. “Why everybody lets him alone enough, for the matter of that. Neither his father nor his mother will ever interfere with him. All his relations let him have his own way pretty well. Eh, Charlotte? He! he! he!”
“让他去吧！”诺亚说。“其实大家都已经够让他自由的了。他的父亲和母亲永远不会干涉他。所有的亲戚也都随他去。是吧，夏洛特？嘿！嘿！嘿！”

source: The old gentleman was a very respectable-looking personage, with a powdered head and gold spectacles. He was dressed in a bottle-green coat with a black velvet collar; wore white trousers; and carried a smart bamboo cane under his arm. He had taken up a book from the stall, and there he stood, reading away, as hard as if he were in his elbow-chair, in his own study. It is very possible that he fancied himself there, indeed; for it was plain, from his abstraction, that he saw not the book-stall, nor the street, nor the boys, nor, in short, anything but the book itself: which he was reading straight through: turning over the leaf when he got to the bottom of a page, beginning at the top line of the next one, and going regularly on, with the greatest interest and eagerness.
domestication: 这位老先生可是个很有派头的人物，一头白粉，架着金丝眼镜。他穿着一件墨绿的外套，领子是黑天鹅绒的，下面是一条白裤子，腋下还夹着一根精致的竹手杖。他从书摊上拿起一本书，站在那儿，读得比坐在自家书房的扶手椅里还要投入。说不定他真的以为自己在书房里呢；从他的神情来看，他显然对外面的世界一无所知，既看不见书摊，也看不见街道，更看不见那些小孩子，眼里只有那本书：一页页地翻，一行行地看，兴趣盎然，津津有味。
foreignization: 这位老先生是一位非常体面的人物，头上的头发扑了粉，戴着金边眼镜。他穿着一件瓶绿色的外套，领子是黑色天鹅绒的；穿白色裤子；腋下夹着一根漂亮的竹手杖。他从书摊上拿起一本书，就站在那里读了起来，就像坐在自己书房里的扶手椅上一样认真。事实上，他可能真的以为自己在那里；因为从他的出神状态来看，他没有看到书摊，也没有看到街道，没有看到孩子们，简而言之，除了书本身，他什么都没有看到：他正从头到尾地读这本书：每读完一页的底部就翻页，从下一页的顶部开始读，有条不紊地继续读下去，带着极大的兴趣和热情。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The old gentleman was a very respectable-looking personage, with a powdered head and gold spectacles. He was dressed in a bottle-green coat with a black velvet collar; wore white trousers; and carried a smart bamboo cane under his arm. He had taken up a book from the stall, and there he stood, reading away, as hard as if he were in his elbow-chair, in his own study. It is very possible that he fancied himself there, indeed; for it was plain, from his abstraction, that he saw not the book-stall, nor the street, nor the boys, nor, in short, anything but the book itself: which he was reading straight through: turning over the leaf when he got to the bottom of a page, beginning at the top line of the next one, and going regularly on, with the greatest interest and eagerness.

这位老先生是一位非常体面的人物，头上的头发扑了粉，戴着金边眼镜。他穿着一件瓶绿色的外套，领子是黑色天鹅绒的；穿白色裤子；腋下夹着一根漂亮的竹手杖。他从书摊上拿起一本书，就站在那里读了起来，就像坐在自己书房里的扶手椅上一样认真。事实上，他可能真的以为自己在那里；因为从他的出神状态来看，他没有看到书摊，也没有看到街道，没有看到孩子们，简而言之，除了书本身，他什么都没有看到：他正从头到尾地读这本书：每读完一页的底部就翻页，从下一页的顶部开始读，有条不紊地继续读下去，带着极大的兴趣和热情。

source: There was such peace and beauty in the scene; so much of brightness and mirth in the sunny landscape; such blithesome music in the songs of the summer birds; such freedom in the rapid flight of the rook, careering overhead; so much of life and joyousness in all; that, when the boy raised his aching eyes, and looked about, the thought instinctively occurred to him, that this was not a time for death; that Rose could surely never die when humbler things were all so glad and gay; that graves were for cold and cheerless winter: not for sunlight and fragrance. He almost thought that shrouds were for the old and shrunken; and that they never wrapped the young and graceful form in their ghastly folds.
domestication: 眼前的景象如此宁静美好，阳光洒在大地上，一切都显得那么明媚欢快；夏日的鸟儿唱着欢乐的歌，自由自在地飞翔；乌鸦在头顶上空疾飞，自在无忧；万物都充满了生机与活力。小男孩抬起疲倦的眼睛，环顾四周，心中不由自主地涌出一个念头：这绝不是死亡的时刻；像罗丝这样的人怎么可能在这万物欢腾的季节离世呢？坟墓属于寒冷萧瑟的冬天，而不是充满阳光和花香的季节。他甚至觉得寿衣是给那些年迈枯槁的人准备的，永远不应该包裹住年轻而美好的身躯。
foreignization: 场面上有如此的宁静与美丽；阳光明媚的风景中有如此多的光明和欢乐；夏日鸟儿的歌声中有如此欢快的音乐；乌鸦在头顶疾飞时有如此的自由；一切之中有如此的生命和欢乐；以至于当男孩抬起他疼痛的眼睛，环顾四周时，他本能地想到，这并不是死亡的时候；罗斯肯定不会在卑微的事物都如此快乐和活泼时死去；坟墓是为寒冷而凄凉的冬天准备的：而不是为阳光和芬芳。他几乎认为寿衣是为年老和萎缩的人准备的；它们从不将年轻人和优美的身形包裹在它们可怕的褶皱中。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There was such peace and beauty in the scene; so much of brightness and mirth in the sunny landscape; such blithesome music in the songs of the summer birds; such freedom in the rapid flight of the rook, careering overhead; so much of life and joyousness in all; that, when the boy raised his aching eyes, and looked about, the thought instinctively occurred to him, that this was not a time for death; that Rose could surely never die when humbler things were all so glad and gay; that graves were for cold and cheerless winter: not for sunlight and fragrance. He almost thought that shrouds were for the old and shrunken; and that they never wrapped the young and graceful form in their ghastly folds.

source: “Stop, stop!” said the old woman in a loud whisper. “Will she be buried tomorrow, or next day, or tonight? I laid her out; and I must walk, you know. Send me a large cloak: a good warm one: for it is bitter cold. We should have cake and wine, too, before we go! Never mind; send some bread—only a loaf of bread and a cup of water. Shall we have some bread, dear?” she said eagerly: catching at the undertaker’s coat, as he once more moved towards the door.
domestication: “停，停！”老妇人大声低语道。“她是明天下葬，后天下葬，还是今晚呢？我给她料理后事了，我得去走一遭。给我送件大斗篷来，要暖和的，这天冷得刺骨。我们出发前还应该吃点蛋糕，喝点酒！算了，送点面包吧——只要一个面包，一杯水就行。亲爱的，我们能有面包吗？”她急切地问道，一把抓住正在向门口走去的殡仪员的衣角。
foreignization: “停，停！”老妇人大声低语道。“她会是明天下葬，还是后天，还是今晚？我给她整理了遗容；你知道，我必须走一趟。给我送件大斗篷来：一件暖和的斗篷：因为天气非常冷。我们出发前还应该吃点蛋糕喝点酒！没关系；送点面包吧——只是一条面包和一杯水。我们会有点面包吗，亲爱的？”她急切地问道，一把抓住正在再次向门口走去的殡仪员的外套。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Stop, stop!” said the old woman in a loud whisper. “Will she be buried tomorrow, or next day, or tonight? I laid her out; and I must walk, you know. Send me a large cloak: a good warm one: for it is bitter cold. We should have cake and wine, too, before we go! Never mind; send some bread—only a loaf of bread and a cup of water. Shall we have some bread, dear?” she said eagerly: catching at the undertaker’s coat, as he once more moved towards the door.

source: Having said this, the worthy old lady hurried back into the parlour again; and seating herself on a sofa, burst into tears. The girl, who was not quite so susceptible, had run upstairs meanwhile; and now returned with a request that Mr. Bumble would follow her immediately: which he did.
domestication: 说完这番话，老太太又匆匆忙忙地回到客厅，一屁股坐在沙发上，眼泪就止不住了。那姑娘倒没这么脆弱，她趁机跑上楼去，一会儿工夫回来，让邦布尔先生立刻跟她走，邦布尔先生也就跟着去了。
foreignization: 说了这话之后，这位可敬的老太太急忙又回到客厅里；坐在沙发上，哭了起来。那女孩，没有那么敏感， meanwhile；这时跑上了楼；现在回来要求布姆布尔先生立刻跟她走：他照做了。

source: Mrs. Corney twice essayed to speak: and twice failed. At length summoning up courage, she threw her arms around Mr. Bumble’s neck, and said, it might be as soon as ever he pleased, and that he was “a irresistible duck.”
domestication: 科尼太太两次想开口说话，两次都没说成。终于鼓足了勇气，她一把搂住邦布尔先生的脖子，说道，随便他什么时候都行，他还真是个“不可抗拒的帅小伙”。
foreignization: 科尼太太两次试图说话，两次都失败了。最后鼓起勇气，她扑到邦布尔先生的脖子上，说可以随他的便，越快越好，并且说他是“一个不可抗拒的鸭子。”

source: Let the tears which fell, and the broken words which were exchanged in the long close embrace between the orphans, be sacred. A father, sister, and mother, were gained, and lost, in that one moment. Joy and grief were mingled in the cup; but there were no bitter tears: for even grief itself arose so softened, and clothed in such sweet and tender recollections, that it became a solemn pleasure, and lost all character of pain.
domestication: 让孤儿们在长久紧拥中流下的泪水和破碎的话语成为神圣的记忆。在那一刻，他们得到了父亲、姐姐和母亲，又瞬间失去了他们。杯中的情感交织着喜悦与悲伤，但没有苦涩的眼泪：因为连悲伤本身也变得柔和，披上了甜美温馨的回忆，变成了庄重的愉悦，不再带有任何痛苦的色彩。
foreignization: 让那些流下的眼泪，以及孤儿们在长时间的紧拥中交换的破碎的话语成为神圣。在那一刻，一个父亲、一个姐姐和一个母亲同时得到了，又失去了。杯中的欢乐与悲伤交织在一起；但没有苦涩的眼泪：因为即使悲伤本身也变得如此柔和，并披上了如此甜美和温柔的回忆，以至于它变成了一种庄严的愉悦，失去了所有的痛苦特性。

source: “None,” replied the landlord of the Cripples; for it was he. “He won’t stir till it’s all safe. Depend on it, they’re on the scent down there; and that if he moved, he’d blow upon the thing at once. He’s all right enough, Barney is, else I should have heard of him. I’ll pound it, that Barney’s managing properly. Let him alone for that.”
domestication: “没有，”瘸子客栈的老板答道，就是他本人。“他不会轻举妄动，除非确保万无一失。我敢肯定，他们下面的人肯定在盯着；要是他一动，准会坏事。巴尼这小子没问题，不然我早该听说了。我敢打赌，巴尼正巧妙地处理呢。这方面他可是一把好手，放心吧。”
foreignization: “没有，”瘸子的房东回答道；因为就是他本人。“除非一切安全了，他是不会动的。放心吧，他们下面的人已经嗅到风声了；如果他一动，马上就会暴露。巴尼没问题，否则我早就听说了。我敢肯定，巴尼正在妥善处理。这方面你尽管放心。”

source: The men laid hands upon him, and disengaging Oliver from his grasp, held him back. He struggled with the power of desperation, for an instant; and then sent up cry upon cry that penetrated even those massive walls, and rang in their ears until they reached the open yard.
domestication: 那几个人一拥而上，把奥利弗从那人手中拉开，死死按住他。奥利弗拼了命地挣扎，但只一瞬间，便哭天喊地起来，那撕心裂肺的哭声穿透了厚实的墙壁，直到他们把他拖到院子里还能听见。
foreignization: 那些人抓住他，将奥利弗从他的掌握中解脱出来，把他拉了回去。他以绝望的力量挣扎了一瞬间；然后发出一声又一声的呼喊，这些声音穿透了那厚厚的墙壁，直到他们到达开阔的院子时仍在他们的耳畔回响。

source: With the same perversity of feeling and irresolution that had fastened upon him, despite himself, all day, the murderer, finding that he was not followed, and that they most probably considered him some drunken sullen fellow, turned back up the town, and getting out of the glare of the lamps of a stage-coach that was standing in the street, was walking past, when he recognised the mail from London, and saw that it was standing at the little post-office. He almost knew what was to come; but he crossed over, and listened.
domestication: 杀人犯心里那股子拧劲儿和犹豫不决，一整天都缠着他，让他自己都控制不住。见没人追他，估计是把他当成了个喝醉了的闷葫芦，他就折回镇上。避开街上的马车灯，正走着，忽然认出这是从伦敦来的邮车，停在了小小的邮局门口。他心里差不多已经猜到接下来会发生什么，但还是过马路凑了过去，竖起耳朵听。
foreignization: 怀着同样的乖张情绪和优柔寡断，这种情绪和优柔寡断一整天都缠着他，尽管他自己并不愿意，凶手发现没有人跟踪他，他们很可能认为他只是一个醉醺醺的闷闷不乐的家伙，于是转身回到镇上，在避开街上的马车灯的强光后，他正走着，突然认出了从伦敦来的邮车，并看到它停在了小邮局前。他几乎预知了接下来会发生什么；但他还是穿过街道，倾听着。

He almost knew what was to come; but he crossed over, and listened.

source: “Especially, when the exclusive young man has got a friend stopping with him, that’s arrived sooner than was expected from foreign parts, and is too modest to want to be presented to the Judges on his return,” added Mr. Kags.
domestication: 特别是，这位独来独往的小伙子还带了个朋友，从外地来的，比预计的早到了，这小伙子谦虚得很，回来后也不愿麻烦法官们正式介绍，”卡格斯先生补充道。
foreignization: “特别是，当这个独来独往的年轻人有个朋友和他住在一起，这个朋友从国外来的比预期的要早，而且太谦虚了，不想在回来时被介绍给法官们，”卡格斯先生补充道。

source: “Now listen to me,” said the stranger, after closing the door and window. “I came down to this place, today, to find you out; and, by one of those chances which the devil throws in the way of his friends sometimes, you walked into the very room I was sitting in, while you were uppermost in my mind. I want some information from you. I don’t ask you to give it for nothing, slight as it is. Put up that, to begin with.”
domestication: 你给我听着，”陌生人关上门窗后说道，“我今天下来就是找你的。巧了，真是巧了，我正想着你呢，你就自己走进了我坐着的这间屋子。我有些事情要问你。虽然事情不大，但我不会让你白说。先拿着这个。”

“Now listen to me,” said the stranger, after closing the door and window. “I came down to this place, today, to find you out; and, by one of those chances which the devil throws in the way of his friends sometimes, you walked into the very room I was sitting in, while you were uppermost in my mind. source: “It is a true tale of grief and trial, and sorrow, young man,” returned Mr. Brownlow, “and such tales usually are; if it were one of unmixed joy and happiness, it would be very brief. At length one of those rich relations to strengthen whose interest and importance your father had been sacrificed, as others are often—it is no uncommon case—died, and to repair the misery he had been instrumental in occasioning, left him his panacea for all griefs—Money. It was necessary that he should immediately repair to Rome, whither this man had sped for health, and where he had died, leaving his affairs in great confusion. He went; was seized with mortal illness there; was followed, the moment the intelligence reached Paris, by your mother who carried you with her; he died the day after her arrival, leaving no will—no will—so that the whole property fell to her and you.”
domestication: 这真是个充满悲痛和磨难的故事，小伙子，”布朗洛先生说，“这样的故事通常是这样；如果全是快乐和幸福，那故事也就没啥可讲的了。你父亲就是为了一位有权有势的亲戚，才被牺牲了，这种事并不少见。那位亲戚后来死了，为了弥补他造成的痛苦，给你父亲留下了他所谓的万能药——钱。你父亲必须马上去罗马，因为那人是为了治病去了那儿，结果死在了那里，留下了一团糟的事情。你父亲去了，却在那里病倒了，病得很重。消息传到巴黎后，你母亲立刻带着你赶了过去。你父亲在你母亲到达的第二天就去世了，没留下遗嘱，一点都没留，所以全部财产都归了你母亲和你。
foreignization: “这是一个真实的故事，充满了悲伤和磨难，年轻人，”布朗洛先生回答道，“这样的故事通常都是如此；如果是一个纯粹的欢乐和幸福的故事，那它就会非常简短。终于，你父亲曾为了增强其兴趣和重要性而被牺牲的那些富有的亲戚之一——这种情况并不少见——去世了，为了弥补他所造成的苦难，他留给了你父亲他解决一切悲伤的灵丹妙药——金钱。他必须立即前往罗马，这个人为了健康去了那里，并在那里去世，留下了极大的混乱。他去了；在那里患上了致命的疾病；消息一传到巴黎，你的母亲就带着你赶去了；他在她到达的第二天去世，没有留下遗嘱——没有遗嘱——因此，全部财产都归她和你所有。”

source: The Jew seemed much vexed by Oliver’s not expressing any greater curiosity on the subject; but the truth is, that, although Oliver felt very anxious, he was too much confused by the earnest cunning of Fagin’s looks, and his own speculations, to make any further inquiries just then. He had no other opportunity: for the Jew remained very surly and silent till night: when he prepared to go abroad.
domestication: 犹太人见奥利弗对这事没什么好奇，似乎很不高兴；可事实上，奥利弗心里虽急，但被费金那狡猾的眼神弄得晕头转向，再加上自己的一堆猜测，一时半会儿也问不出口。这之后，奥利弗再没机会开口：因为那犹太人一直板着脸，闷声不响，直到晚上，他准备出门去了。
foreignization: 那个犹太人似乎因为奥利弗没有表现出更大的好奇而感到非常恼火；但事实是，尽管奥利弗感到非常焦急，他却被费金的表情中的热切狡诈以及他自己的猜测弄得太过困惑，以至于当时无法再进一步询问。他没有其他机会：因为那犹太人直到晚上都一直很阴沉和沉默，那时他准备外出。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The Jew seemed much vexed by Oliver’s not expressing any greater curiosity on the subject; but the truth is, that, although Oliver felt very anxious, he was too much confused by the earnest cunning of Fagin’s looks, and his own speculations, to make any further inquiries just then. He had no other opportunity: for the Jew remained very surly and silent till night: when he prepared to go abroad.

source: Now, whether a peculiar contraction of the Jew’s red eye-brows, and a half closing of his deeply-set eyes, warned Miss Nancy that she was disposed to be too communicative, is not a matter of much importance. The fact is all we need care for here; and the fact is, that she suddenly checked herself, and with several gracious smiles upon Mr. Sikes, turned the conversation to other matters. In about ten minutes’ time, Mr. Fagin was seized with a fit of coughing; upon which Nancy pulled her shawl over her shoulders, and declared it was time to go. Mr. Sikes, finding that he was walking a short part of her way himself, expressed his intention of accompanying her; they went away together, followed, at a little distant, by the dog, who slunk out of a back-yard as soon as his master was out of sight.
domestication: 现在，犹太老头儿那红色眉毛一挑，深陷的眼睛半眯着，是不是提醒南希小姐别多嘴，这倒不重要了。关键在于，她突然闭上了嘴，对着赛克斯先生甜甜地一笑，就转到其他话题上去了。大约过了十分钟，费金先生开始咳嗽起来。南希见状，便披上披肩，说该走了。赛克斯发现他正好顺路，便提出要送她一程；他们俩便一起离开了，后面还跟着那只狗，它等主人走远后，从后院悄悄跟了上去。
foreignization: 现在，犹太人的红眉毛是否奇怪地皱了一下，他那深深的眼窝是否半闭着，警告南希小姐她过于健谈了，这并不是什么重要的事情。我们在这里只需要关注事实；而事实是，她突然停住了话头，对着赛克斯先生露出几次优雅的微笑后，把话题转向了其他的事情。大约十分钟后，费金先生发作了咳嗽；这时南希拉起她的披肩盖在肩膀上，宣布该走了。赛克斯先生发现他要走的路有一段和她的路相同，便表示要陪她一起去；他们一起离开了，狗在后面不远处跟着，等它的主人一走出视线，它就从后院溜了出来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Now, whether a peculiar contraction of the Jew’s red eye-brows, and a half closing of his deeply-set eyes, warned Miss Nancy that she was disposed to be too communicative, is not a matter of much importance. The fact is all we need care for here; and the fact is, that she suddenly checked herself, and with several gracious smiles upon Mr. Sikes, turned the conversation to other matters. In about ten minutes’ time, Mr. Fagin was seized with a fit of coughing; upon which Nancy pulled her shawl over her shoulders, and declared it was time to go. Mr. Sikes, finding that he was walking a short part of her way himself, expressed his intention of accompanying her; they went away together, followed, at a little distant, by the dog, who slunk out of a back-yard as soon as his master was out of sight.

source: He hastily descended, as the room-door opened, and the girl came out. Fagin drew him behind a small partition which was curtained off, and they held their breaths as she passed within a few feet of their place of concealment, and emerged by the door at which they had entered.
domestication: 他急忙往下走，这时房门开了，姑娘出来了。费金赶紧把他拉到一个用帘子隔开的小隔间里，两人屏住呼吸，姑娘离他们藏身的地方不过几尺远，从他们进来时的那扇门出去了。

source: “The boy grew so like his mother,” said the woman, rambling on, and not heeding the question, “that I could never forget it when I saw his face. Poor girl! poor girl! She was so young, too! Such a gentle lamb! Wait; there’s more to tell. I have not told you all, have I?”
domestication: 这男孩长得太像他妈妈了，我一看到他的脸就忘不了。可怜的姑娘！可怜的姑娘！她年纪轻轻的，又是个温顺的羊羔。等等，还有更多要说的呢。我还没全告诉你吧？
foreignization: “这男孩长得太像他妈妈了，”女人自顾自地说着，没有注意到问题，“每当我看到他的脸，我就无法忘记。可怜的女孩！可怜的女孩！她还那么年轻！这么温柔的小羊！等一下；还有更多的事情要说。我还没有把一切都告诉你，是吗？”

source: “If you do it well, a pound, my dear. One pound,” said Fagin, wishing to interest him in the scent as much as possible. “And that’s what I never gave yet, for any job of work where there wasn’t valuable consideration to be gained.”
domestication: 你要是干得好，就给你一镑，亲爱的，一镑呢。费金说道，想尽办法激起他的兴趣。“这可是我头一回为了干活儿出这么高的价，以前从没有过，除非能从中捞到大好处。”

source: The circumstance did not dwell in his recollection long, however: for when he reached the cottage, there was enough to occupy his mind, and to drive all considerations of self completely from his memory.

The circumstance did not dwell in his recollection long, however: for when he reached the cottage, there was enough to occupy his mind, and to drive all considerations of self completely from his memory.
然而，这种情况在他的记忆中并没有停留太久：因为他到达小屋时，有足够多的事情占据他的心思，使他完全忘记了所有的自我考量。

source: “By mine,” replied Mr. Brownlow. “Those persons are indemnified by me. If you complain of being deprived of your liberty—you had power and opportunity to retrieve it as you came along, but you deemed it advisable to remain quiet—I say again, throw yourself for protection on the law. I will appeal to the law too; but when you have gone too far to recede, do not sue to me for leniency, when the power will have passed into other hands; and do not say I plunged you down the gulf into which you rushed, yourself.”
domestication: “由我负责，”布朗洛先生回答道。“那些人的损失我会赔偿。要是你抱怨失去了自由——你本来有机会和力量恢复自由，但你选择保持安静——我再说一遍，去找法律保护自己吧。我也会诉诸法律；但当你走得太远，无法回头时，不要再来向我求情，那时大权旁落，我也无能为力；别说我把你推下深渊，是你自己跳下去的。”
foreignization: “由我负责，”布朗洛先生回答。“那些人都由我赔偿。如果你抱怨被剥夺了自由——你有力量和机会在来的路上恢复自由，但你认为保持安静更为明智——我再说一次，向法律寻求保护吧。我也会诉诸法律；但当你走得太远无法回头时，不要向我请求宽恕，因为那时权力将落入他人之手；也不要说我把你推入了你自己冲进去的深渊。”

source: “You won’t do nothing of the kind,” rejoined Mr. Sikes. “The Artful’s a deal too artful, and would forget to come, or lose his way, or get dodged by traps and so be perwented, or anything for an excuse, if you put him up to it. Nancy shall go to the ken and fetch it, to make all sure; and I’ll lie down and have a snooze while she’s gone.”
domestication: 你可别想美了，”赛克斯先生说道。“机灵鬼太狡猾了，他要么会忘了来，要么会迷路，要么会被警察盯上找借口脱身，你让他去肯定不行。还是让南希去窝点取东西，这样保险些；趁着她去的工夫，我躺下打个盹儿。”
foreignization: “你不会那样做的，”赛克斯先生回答说。“机灵鬼太机灵了，他会忘了来，或者迷路，或者被警察截住，所以被阻止了，或者找任何借口，如果你让他去的话。南希会去窝点把它取来，以确保万无一失；而我在这儿躺下，趁她不在的时候小睡一会儿。”

source: “When ladies as young, and good, and beautiful as you are,” replied the girl steadily, “give away your hearts, love will carry you all lengths—even such as you, who have home, friends, other admirers, everything, to fill them. When such as I, who have no certain roof but the coffinlid, and no friend in sickness or death but the hospital nurse, set our rotten hearts on any man, and let him fill the place that has been a blank through all our wretched lives, who can hope to cure us? Pity us, lady—pity us for having only one feeling of the woman left, and for having that turned, by a heavy judgment, from a comfort and a pride, into a new means of violence and suffering.”
domestication: “像您这样年轻、善良、美丽的小姐，”女孩平静地回答，“一旦动了真心，爱情就会让人不顾一切——哪怕是像您这样，有家、有朋友、有仰慕者、拥有一切的人。像我这样，无依无靠，只有棺材盖是自己的屋顶，病了、死了也只有医院的护士能照顾的人，一旦把那颗烂透的心给了谁，让他填满我们一生中空白的地方，还有谁能救得了我们呢？求您可怜可怜我们吧，可怜我们只剩下一点做女人的感觉，却偏偏被这无情的命运变成了新的痛苦和折磨。”
foreignization: “当像您这样年轻、善良、美丽的女士，”女孩坚定地回答，“献出你们的心时，爱会带领你们走向一切——即使是像您这样拥有家庭、朋友、其他仰慕者和一切的人。当像我这样没有固定住所只有棺材盖，生病或死亡时只有医院护士为友的人，把我们那腐烂的心寄托在某个男人身上，让他填补我们悲惨一生中的空白，谁又能治愈我们呢？怜悯我们吧，女士——怜悯我们只剩下一种女性的感觉，而这种感觉却因沉重的判决，从安慰和骄傲变成了新的暴力和痛苦的手段。”

messages_domestication.content:

Pity us, lady—pity us for having only one feeling of the woman left, and for having that turned, by a heavy judgment, from a comfort and a pride, into a new means of violence and suffering.”

source: In short, after some more examination, and a great deal more conversation, a neighbouring magistrate was readily induced to take the joint bail of Mrs. Maylie and Mr. Losberne for Oliver’s appearance if he should ever be called upon; and Blathers and Duff, being rewarded with a couple of guineas, returned to town with divided opinions on the subject of their expedition: the latter gentleman on a mature consideration of all the circumstances, inclining to the belief that the burglarious attempt had originated with the Family Pet; and the former being equally disposed to concede the full merit of it to the great Mr. Conkey Chickweed.
domestication: 总而言之，经过一番仔细检查和大量的交谈后，附近的一位官员很容易就被梅利太太和罗斯伯恩先生联合担保说服了，如果奥利弗将来被传唤时，他们会保证他出庭。布雷瑟斯和达夫得到了两英镑的酬金，带着对这次行动的不同看法回到了城里：达夫先生在全面考虑所有情况后，倾向于认为这次入室行窃是家里的宠物干的；而布雷瑟斯则同样倾向于将这桩功劳归功于大名鼎鼎的康基·奇克威德先生。
foreignization: 简言之，在进一步的调查和大量的交谈之后，一位邻近的治安法官很容易就被说服，接受了梅利太太和洛斯伯恩先生为奥利弗提供的共同保释，如果他日后被传唤的话；而布莱瑟斯和达夫在得到了两畿尼的报酬后，带着对这次行动的不同看法回到了城里：后者在仔细考虑了所有情况后，倾向于认为这起盗窃企图是由家庭宠物引起的；前者则同样倾向于将此事的全部功劳归功于伟大的康基·奇克威德先生。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In short, after some more examination, and a great deal more conversation, a neighbouring magistrate was readily induced to take the joint bail of Mrs. Maylie and Mr. Losberne for Oliver’s appearance if he should ever be called upon; and Blathers and Duff, being rewarded with a couple of guineas, returned to town with divided opinions on the subject of their expedition: the latter gentleman on a mature consideration of all the circumstances, inclining to the belief that the burglarious attempt had originated with the Family Pet; and the former being equally disposed to concede the full merit of it to the great Mr. Conkey Chickweed.
总而言之，经过一番仔细检查和大量的交谈后，附近的一位官员很容易就被梅利太太和罗斯伯恩先生联合担保说服了，如果奥利弗将来被传唤时，他们会保证他出庭。布雷瑟斯和达夫得到了两英镑的酬金，带着对这次行动的不同看法回到了城里：达夫先生在全面考虑所有情况后，倾向于认为这次入室行窃是家里的宠物干的；而布雷瑟斯则同样倾向于将这桩功劳归功于大名鼎鼎的康基·奇克威德先生。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: Mr. Bumble, seeing with excruciating feelings, the delight of the two old paupers, who were tittering together most rapturously, hesitated for an instant. Mrs. Bumble, whose patience brooked no delay, caught up a bowl of soap-suds, and motioning him towards the door, ordered him instantly to depart, on pain of receiving the contents upon his portly person.
domestication: 班布尔先生见那两个老贫民笑得前仰后合，心里别提有多不是滋味了，一时间竟有些犹豫不决。班布尔太太可没这耐心，她一把抄起一碗肥皂水，朝班布尔先生一指门，厉声喝道：再不走就把这碗水全泼到他那胖乎乎的身上。
foreignization: 班布尔先生看到两个老贫民欣喜若狂地咯咯笑在一起，心里感到极其难受，犹豫了一下。班布尔太太的耐心不容拖延，她拿起一碗肥皂水，朝他挥了挥手，命令他立刻离开，否则就把这些肥皂水泼到他那肥胖的身体上。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Bumble, seeing with excruciating feelings, the delight of the two old paupers, who were tittering together most rapturously, hesitated for an instant. Mrs. Bumble, whose patience brooked no delay, caught up a bowl of soap-suds, and motioning him towards the door, ordered him instantly to depart, on pain of receiving the contents upon his portly person.
班布尔先生见那两个老贫民笑得前仰后合，心里别提有多不是滋味了，一时间竟有些犹豫不决。班布尔太太可没这耐心，她一把抄起一碗肥皂水，朝班布尔先生一指门，厉声喝道：再不走就把这碗水全泼到他那胖乎乎的身上。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Bumble, seeing with excruciating feelings, the delight of the two old paupers, who were tittering together most rapturously, hesitated for an instant. Mrs. Bumble, whose patience brooked no delay, caught up a bowl of soap-suds, and motioning him towards the door, ordered him instantly to depart, on pain of receiving the contents upon his portly person.
班布尔先生看到两个老贫民欣喜若狂地咯咯笑在一起，心里感到极其难受，犹豫了一下。班布尔太太的耐心不容拖延，她拿起一碗肥皂水，朝他挥了挥手，命令他立刻离开，否则就把这些肥皂水泼到他那肥胖的身体上。

source: Among other ingenious surmises, the question was then raised, whether Mr. Giles had really hit anybody; and upon examination of the fellow pistol to that which he had fired, it turned out to have no more destructive loading than gunpowder and brown paper: a discovery which made a considerable impression on everybody but the doctor, who had drawn the ball about ten minutes before. Upon no one, however, did it make a greater impression than on Mr. Giles himself; who, after labouring, for some hours, under the fear of having mortally wounded a fellow-creature, eagerly caught at this new idea, and favoured it to the utmost. Finally, the officers, without troubling themselves very much about Oliver, left the Chertsey constable in the house, and took up their rest for that night in the town; promising to return the next morning.
domestication: 在众多巧妙的猜测中，有人提出了一个问题：吉尔斯先生是否真的打到了人？经过对另一支手枪的检查，发现里面装的只是火药和牛皮纸，没有致命的弹丸。这一发现让所有人都大吃一惊，除了那位医生，他早在十分钟前就已经取出了弹丸。然而，最感到震惊的还是吉尔斯先生本人。他在几个小时里一直担心自己可能致命地伤害了别人，如今这个新发现让他如释重负，急忙抓住这个机会，极力支持这一说法。最终，官员们并没有太在意奥利弗，留下切特西的警察守着房子，自己则在镇上找地方过夜，并答应第二天早上回来。
foreignization: 在其他一些巧妙的猜测中，有人提出了这样的问题：吉尔斯先生是否真的击中了任何人；在检查他所发射的手枪的另一只手枪时，发现其装填物并不比火药和棕色纸更有破坏性：这一发现给所有人（除了那位医生，他在大约十分钟前已经取出了子弹）留下了深刻的印象。然而，没有人比吉尔斯先生自己对此印象更深；在几个小时内一直担心自己可能致命地伤害了一个同类之后，他急切地抓住了这个新想法，并尽可能地支持它。最终，官员们没有过多地关注奥利弗，留下切特西的警察在房子里，自己则在镇上过夜休息；并承诺第二天早上返回。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Among other ingenious surmises, the question was then raised, whether Mr. Giles had really hit anybody; and upon examination of the fellow pistol to that which he had fired, it turned out to have no more destructive loading than gunpowder and brown paper: a discovery which made a considerable impression on everybody but the doctor, who had drawn the ball about ten minutes before. Upon no one, however, did it make a greater impression than on Mr. Giles himself; who, after labouring, for some hours, under the fear of having mortally wounded a fellow-creature, eagerly caught at this new idea, and favoured it to the utmost. Finally, the officers, without troubling themselves very much about Oliver, left the Chertsey constable in the house, and took up their rest for that night in the town; promising to return the next morning.

Finally, the officers, without troubling themselves very much about Oliver, left the Chertsey constable in the house, and took up their rest for that night in the town; promising to return the next morning.

在其他一些巧妙的猜测中，有人提出了这样的问题：吉尔斯先生是否真的击中了任何人；在检查他所发射的手枪的另一只手枪时，发现其装填物并不比火药和棕色纸更有破坏性：这一发现给所有人（除了那位医生，他在大约十分钟前已经取出了子弹）留下了深刻的印象。然而，没有人比吉尔斯先生自己对此印象更深；在几个小时内一直担心自己可能致命地伤害了一个同类之后，他急切地抓住了这个新想法，并尽可能地支持它。最终，官员们没有过多地关注奥利弗，留下切特西的警察在房子里，自己则在镇上过夜休息；并承诺第二天早上返回。

source: “But even if he has been wicked,” pursued Rose, “think how young he is; think that he may never have known a mother’s love, or the comfort of a home; that ill-usage and blows, or the want of bread, may have driven him to herd with men who have forced him to guilt. Aunt, dear aunt, for mercy’s sake, think of this, before you let them drag this sick child to a prison, which in any case must be the grave of all his chances of amendment. Oh! as you love me, and know that I have never felt the want of parents in your goodness and affection, but that I might have done so, and might have been equally helpless and unprotected with this poor child, have pity upon him before it is too late!”
domestication: “就算他做了坏事，”罗丝接着说，“可他年纪还小；也许他从没感受过母爱，没享受过家的温暖；可能是因为挨打受虐，或者吃不上饭，才被迫和那些引诱他犯罪的人混在一起。亲爱的姑妈，看在老天爷的份上，在让他们把这个生病的孩子拖进监狱之前，想想这些吧！监狱对他来说，可是断了改过自新的最后希望啊。哦，您要是爱我，知道我虽然在您的关爱下从未缺少过父母的照顾，但我也可能和这个可怜的孩子一样无助，一样没有保护，求求您在他走投无路之前，发发慈悲吧！”
foreignization: “但即使他做过坏事，”罗丝继续说，“想想他多么年轻；想想他可能从未感受过母爱，或家庭的温暖；想想可能是虐待和殴打，或是缺少面包，迫使他与那些逼迫他犯罪的人为伍。亲爱的姨妈，看在怜悯的份上，在让他们把这个生病的孩子拖进监狱之前，请您想想这些，这所监狱无论如何都将是他所有改过自新机会的坟墓。哦！看在您对我的爱，知道我在您的善良和关爱中从未感到缺少父母，但我也可能如此，可能与这个可怜的孩子一样无助和缺乏保护，看在他还有救的份上，请您怜悯他吧！”

Aunt, dear aunt, for mercy’s sake, think of this, before you let them drag this sick child to a prison, which in any case must be the grave of all his chances of amendment. Oh! as you love me, and know that I have never felt the want of parents in your goodness and affection, but that I might have done so, and might have been equally helpless and unprotected with this poor child, have pity upon him before it is too late!”

source: Oliver knew, perfectly well, that he was in his own little room; that his books were lying on the table before him; that the sweet air was stirring among the creeping plants outside. And yet he was asleep. Suddenly, the scene changed; the air became close and confined; and he thought, with a glow of terror, that he was in the Jew’s house again. There sat the hideous old man, in his accustomed corner, pointing at him, and whispering to another man, with his face averted, who sat beside him.
domestication: 奥利弗清楚地知道，他正躺在自己的小房间里；桌上散落着他的书本；窗外的空气拂过攀爬的植物，带着一丝甜蜜。可他却睡着了。突然，周围的情景变了，空气变得闷热起来。他心里一惊，仿佛自己又回到了那个犹太人的家里。那个丑陋的老头子还坐在老地方，朝他指指点点，还在跟旁边的一个男人窃窃私语，那人的脸背对着他。
foreignization: 奥利弗非常清楚，他就在自己的小房间里；他的书就躺在他面前的桌子上；清新的空气正在外面的爬藤植物间轻轻吹拂。然而，他却睡着了。突然，场景发生了变化；空气变得闷热而压抑；他带着一股恐惧的热度想到，自己又回到了那个犹太人的房子里。那个可怕的老人坐在他惯常的角落里，指着奥利弗，对着旁边坐着的一个侧过脸去的男人耳语。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver knew, perfectly well, that he was in his own little room; that his books were lying on the table before him; that the sweet air was stirring among the creeping plants outside. And yet he was asleep. Suddenly, the scene changed; the air became close and confined; and he thought, with a glow of terror, that he was in the Jew’s house again. There sat the hideous old man, in his accustomed corner, pointing at him, and whispering to another man, with his face averted, who sat beside him.
奥利弗清楚地知道，他正躺在自己的小房间里；桌上散落着他的书本；窗外的空气拂过攀爬的植物，带着一丝甜蜜。可他却睡着了。突然，周围的情景变了，空气变得闷热起来。他心里一惊，仿佛自己又回到了那个犹太人的家里。那个丑陋的老头子还坐在老地方，朝他指指点点，还在跟旁边的一个男人窃窃私语，那人的脸背对着他。

Oliver knew, perfectly well, that he was in his own little room; that his books were lying on the table before him; that the sweet air was stirring among the creeping plants outside. And yet he was asleep. Suddenly, the scene changed; the air became close and confined; and he thought, with a glow of terror, that he was in the Jew’s house again. There sat the hideous old man, in his accustomed corner, pointing at him, and whispering to another man, with his face averted, who sat beside him.
奥利弗非常清楚，他就在自己的小房间里；他的书就躺在他面前的桌子上；清新的空气正在外面的爬藤植物间轻轻吹拂。然而，他却睡着了。突然，场景发生了变化；空气变得闷热而压抑；他带着一股恐惧的热度想到，自己又回到了那个犹太人的房子里。那个可怕的老人坐在他惯常的角落里，指着奥利弗，对着旁边坐着的一个侧过脸去的男人耳语。

source: Barney complied by ushering them into a small back-room, and setting the required viands before them; having done which, he informed the travellers that they could be lodged that night, and left the amiable couple to their refreshment.
domestication: 巴尼照办了，领他们进了后头的小屋，摆上了所需的食物。忙完这些，他告诉旅人今晚可以住宿，便让这对和气的夫妇自己享用去了。
foreignization: 巴尼照办了，把他们领进一个小后室，并在他们面前摆上了所需的食物；做完这些后，他告诉旅客们当晚可以住宿，然后让这对可爱的夫妇自便享用。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Barney complied by ushering them into a small back-room, and setting the required viands before them; having done which, he informed the travellers that they could be lodged that night, and left the amiable couple to their refreshment.
巴尼照办了，领他们进了后头的小屋，摆上了所需的食物。忙完这些，他告诉旅人今晚可以住宿，便让这对和气的夫妇自己享用去了。

Barney complied by ushering them into a small back-room, and setting the required viands before them; having done which, he informed the travellers that they could be lodged that night, and left the amiable couple to their refreshment.
巴尼照办了，把他们领进一个小后室，并在他们面前摆上了所需的食物；做完这些后，他告诉旅客们当晚可以住宿，然后让这对可爱的夫妇自便享用。

source: At length, a low cry of pain broke the stillness that prevailed; and uttering it, the boy awoke. His left arm, rudely bandaged in a shawl, hung heavy and useless at his side; the bandage was saturated with blood. He was so weak, that he could scarcely raise himself into a sitting posture; when he had done so, he looked feebly round for help, and groaned with pain. Trembling in every joint, from cold and exhaustion, he made an effort to stand upright; but, shuddering from head to foot, fell prostrate on the ground.
domestication: 终于，寂静中响起一声低低的痛呼；这一声，也把他从梦中惊醒。他的左臂用一条披肩草草地包着，沉沉地垂在身侧，毫无用处；那披肩已被血染透。他虚弱得几乎坐不起来，勉强撑起身子，无力地四下张望，想找人帮忙，却疼得直呻吟。浑身发抖，又冷又累，他挣扎着想站起身来，但全身一哆嗦，从头到脚都跟着颤抖，最后还是瘫倒在了地上。
foreignization: 终于，一声低低的痛苦呻吟打破了寂静；发出这声音后，男孩醒了过来。他的左臂被粗鲁地用披肩包扎着，沉重而无用地垂在身侧；绷带浸透了血。他非常虚弱，几乎不能把自己扶到坐姿；当他这样做时，他无力地四下张望寻求帮助，并因疼痛而呻吟。因寒冷和疲惫而浑身发抖，他努力尝试站起来；但全身颤抖，从头到脚都在打颤，最终扑倒在了地上。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

His left arm, rudely bandaged in a shawl, hung heavy and useless at his side; the bandage was saturated with blood. He was so weak, that he could scarcely raise himself into a sitting posture; when he had done so, he looked feebly round for help, and groaned with pain. Trembling in every joint, from cold and exhaustion, he made an effort to stand upright; but, shuddering from head to foot, fell prostrate on the ground.

At length, a low cry of pain broke the stillness that prevailed; and uttering it, the boy awoke. His left arm, rudely bandaged in a shawl, hung heavy and useless at his side; the bandage was saturated with blood. He was so weak, that he could scarcely raise himself into a sitting posture; when he had done so, he looked feebly round for help, and groaned with pain. Trembling in every joint, from cold and exhaustion, he made an effort to stand upright; but, shuddering from head to foot, fell prostrate on the ground.
终于，一声低低的痛苦呻吟打破了寂静；发出这声音后，男孩醒了过来。他的左臂被粗鲁地用披肩包扎着，沉重而无用地垂在身侧；绷带浸透了血。他非常虚弱，几乎不能把自己扶到坐姿；当他这样做时，他无力地四下张望寻求帮助，并因疼痛而呻吟。因寒冷和疲惫而浑身发抖，他努力尝试站起来；但全身颤抖，从头到脚都在打颤，最终扑倒在了地上。

source: “Why, the traps have got him, and that’s all about it,” said the Dodger, sullenly. “Come, let go o’ me, will you!” And, swinging himself, at one jerk, clean out of the big coat, which he left in the Jew’s hands, the Dodger snatched up the toasting fork, and made a pass at the merry old gentleman’s waistcoat; which, if it had taken effect, would have let a little more merriment out than could have been easily replaced.
domestication: “嘿，他这是自投罗网，没跑了。”杰克·道奇阴沉沉地说。“放开我，听见没有！”说着，道奇猛一使劲，身体从那件大衣里挣脱出来，把大衣留在了那个老犹太手里。他抓起烤叉，朝那位笑眯眯的老先生的背心就是一刺，要是真刺到了，那点乐呵劲儿可就泄得差不多了。
foreignization: “嘿，陷阱抓住他了，就是这样，”多德杰阴沉地说。“来，放开我，行不行！”说着，他一使劲，干净利落地从那件大衣里挣脱出来，把大衣留在了犹太人的手里。多德杰抓起烤叉，向那位快活的老先生的背心刺去；如果这一刺得逞的话，放出来的欢乐可就不是那么容易能补回来的了。

source: “Here,” said Toby, as the young Jew placed some fragments of food, and a bottle upon the table, “Success to the crack!” He rose to honour the toast; and, carefully depositing his empty pipe in a corner, advanced to the table, filled a glass with spirits, and drank off its contents. Mr. Sikes did the same.
domestication: 托比说道：“在这儿。”说着，犹太小孩把一些食物碎片和一瓶酒放在桌子上。“祝咱们这次行动成功！”他站起来敬了个酒，小心翼翼地把抽空的烟斗放在角落里，走到桌前，倒了一杯酒，一饮而尽。西克斯先生也照样做了。

source: They held their course at this rate, until they had passed Hyde Park corner, and were on their way to Kensington: when Sikes relaxed his pace, until an empty cart which was at some little distance behind, came up. Seeing “Hounslow” written on it, he asked the driver with as much civility as he could assume, if he would give them a lift as far as Isleworth.
domestication: 他们就这么一路疾走，直到过了海德公园拐角，往肯辛顿方向走去。这时，赛克斯放慢了脚步，等一辆稍后些的空马车跟了上来。他看到车上写着“豪恩斯洛”，便尽量客气地问车夫能不能捎他们一程，送到艾尔斯沃思。
foreignization: 他们以这样的速度前行，直到穿过海德公园拐角，前往肯辛顿的路上：这时西克斯放慢了步伐，直到一辆稍后一些的空马车跟了上来。看到车上写着“汉默史密斯”，他尽可能礼貌地问司机是否可以让他们搭到伊斯沃思。

他们以这样的速度前行，直到穿过海德公园拐角，前往肯辛顿的路上：这时西克斯放慢了步伐，直到一辆稍后一些的空马车跟了上来。看到车上写着“汉默史密斯”，他尽可能礼貌地问司机是否可以让他们搭到伊斯沃思。

source: “Now, Miss Maylie,” said Mr. Brownlow, “to return to the subject in which your humanity is so much interested. Will you let me know what intelligence you have of this poor child: allowing me to promise that I exhausted every means in my power of discovering him, and that since I have been absent from this country, my first impression that he had imposed upon me, and had been persuaded by his former associates to rob me, has been considerably shaken.”
domestication: 现在，梅利小姐，布朗洛先生说道，“咱们还是回到您这么关心的这个话题吧。您能不能告诉我，关于这可怜的孩子，您打听到些什么消息？我向您保证，为了找到他，我可是想尽了办法。而且，自从我离开这个国家后，我最初的怀疑——认为他欺骗了我，并且被以前的同伙说服去偷我的东西——这个想法已经动摇了不少。”
foreignization: “现在，梅利小姐，”布朗洛先生说，“回到你如此关心的话题上。你能告诉我关于这个可怜的孩子你有什么消息吗：允许我保证我已经竭尽全力去寻找他，而且自从我离开这个国家后，我最初认为他欺骗了我，并被他的旧同伙说服去偷我的印象已经大大动摇。”

source: “No, no,” said the Jew, hastily; as though, however desirous he might be to see the person in question, he was nevertheless relieved by his absence. “Tell him I came here to see him; and that he must come to me tonight. No, say tomorrow. As he is not here, tomorrow will be time enough.”
domestication: “不不，”那犹太人赶忙说道，好像他虽然想见这个人，但不见他也挺高兴的。“告诉他我来过了，叫他今晚到我那儿去。不对，说明天吧。反正他今天不在，说明天也行。”
foreignization: “不，不，”那犹太人连忙说道；仿佛不管他多么想见那个人，他还是因为那个人不在而感到如释重负。“告诉他我来这儿看他了；他必须今晚来找我。不，说明天吧。因为他不在，明天也够了。”

source: “I never was more convinced of anything in my life,” said the gentleman in the white waistcoat, as he knocked at the gate and read the bill next morning: “I never was more convinced of anything in my life, than I am that that boy will come to be hung.”
domestication: “我这辈子从没这么肯定过一件事，”那位穿着白背心的先生边敲门边看着告示说，“我这辈子从没这么肯定过一件事，就是这个小子将来肯定得上绞刑架。”
foreignization: “我一生中从未对任何事情如此确信过，”那位穿着白色背心的先生边敲门边读着告示，第二天早晨说：“我一生中从未对任何事情如此确信过，就像我确信那个男孩将来会被绞死一样。”

source: The doctor seemed especially troubled by the fact of the robbery having been unexpected, and attempted in the night-time; as if it were the established custom of gentlemen in the housebreaking way to transact business at noon, and to make an appointment, by post, a day or two previous.
domestication: 医生特别苦恼的是，这起抢劫来得突然，而且是在晚上动手的；好像那些做破门而入勾当的绅士们，一向习惯在中午干活，还提前一两天通过邮局预约似的。
foreignization: 医生似乎特别为抢劫的发生出乎意料且发生在夜间而感到困扰；仿佛那些从事破门而入的绅士们惯常在中午办理业务，并且会在一两天前通过邮件预约。

source: “No!” replied the girl. “I have not done this for money. Let me have that to think of. And yet—give me something that you have worn: I should like to have something—no, no, not a ring—your gloves or handkerchief—anything that I can keep, as having belonged to you, sweet lady. There. Bless you! God bless you. Good-night, good-night!”
domestication: “不！”女孩回答，“我没这么做是为了钱。让我自己想想。可是——给我一样你用过的东西吧：我想要留个纪念——不不，不是戒指——你的手套或者手帕——任何我能留作纪念的东西，亲爱的小姐。好了。感谢你！愿上帝保佑你。晚安，晚安！”
foreignization: “不！”女孩回答道。“我不是为了钱才这么做的。让我有这个想法吧。然而——给我一些你戴过的东西：我想要拥有一些——不，不，不是戒指——你的手套或手帕——任何我可以保存的东西，作为属于你的纪念，亲爱的女士。好了。愿你幸福！上帝保佑你。晚安，晚安！”

source: It was late next morning when Oliver awoke, from a sound, long sleep. There was no other person in the room but the old Jew, who was boiling some coffee in a saucepan for breakfast, and whistling softly to himself as he stirred it round and round, with an iron spoon. He would stop every now and then to listen when there was the least noise below: and when he had satisfied himself, he would go on whistling and stirring again, as before.
domestication: 第二天早上，奥利弗睡了个大觉，直到很晚才醒来。房间里只有老犹太人在，他正在用锅煮咖啡当早餐，一边用铁勺子搅着，一边轻轻地吹着口哨。只要下面一有动静，他就停下来听听；等确认没事了，就又继续搅咖啡，吹口哨，跟刚才一样。
foreignization: 第二天早上很晚的时候，奥利弗从一个深沉而漫长的睡眠中醒来。房间里除了那个老犹太人之外没有其他人，他正在用平底锅煮早餐咖啡，并用一把铁勺子在里面不停地搅动，同时轻轻地吹着口哨。每当下边有一点声音时，他就会停下来倾听；当他让自己满意之后，又会像之前一样继续吹口哨和搅拌。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

He would stop every now and then to listen when there was the least noise below: and when he had satisfied himself, he would go on whistling and stirring again, as before.

source: At this moment the noise grew louder. Sikes, again looking round, could discern that the men who had given chase were already climbing the gate of the field in which he stood; and that a couple of dogs were some paces in advance of them.
domestication: 这时，声音越来越响。西克斯回头一看，发现追赶他的人已经爬上了他所在的田野的栅门，还有两条狗跑在前面，离他们有几丈远。
foreignization: 此时此刻，声音变得更大了。西克斯再次回头，能够分辨出那些追赶他的人已经爬上了他所在田野的门；有两条狗走在他们前面几步远的地方。

source: The girl obeyed. Sikes, locking her hand in his, fell back upon the pillow: turning his eyes upon her face. They closed; opened again; closed once more; again opened. He shifted his position restlessly; and, after dozing again, and again, for two or three minutes, and as often springing up with a look of terror, and gazing vacantly about him, was suddenly stricken, as it were, while in the very attitude of rising, into a deep and heavy sleep. The grasp of his hand relaxed; the upraised arm fell languidly by his side; and he lay like one in a profound trance.
domestication: 女孩照做了。西克斯握紧她的手，往枕头上一靠，眼睛盯着她的脸看。眼皮合上了，又睁开；再合上，又再睁开。他不安地动了动身子，迷迷糊糊地打了几个盹儿，每次都不超过两三分钟，每次醒来都吓得跳起来，茫然四顾。就在他正要起身的时候，突然就像被什么击中了一样，陷入了沉沉的睡眠。他的手松开了，抬起的手臂无力地垂在身侧，整个人像是陷入了深不见底的梦中。
foreignization: 女孩服从了。西克斯锁住了她的手，向后倒在枕头上，眼睛盯着她的脸。他的眼睛闭上了；又睁开；再次闭上；又一次睁开。他不安地动了动身体；在又打了两三次盹之后，每次都带着恐惧的表情猛然跳起来，茫然地环顾四周，突然仿佛在要起身的姿势中被打入了深深的沉睡。他的手松开了；举起的手臂无力地垂在身侧；他躺得就像陷入了深重的昏睡之中。

source: “Brittles always was a slow boy, ma’am,” replied the attendant. And seeing, by the bye, that Brittles had been a slow boy for upwards of thirty years, there appeared no great probability of his ever being a fast one.
domestication: “布里特尔斯这孩子从小就慢条斯理的，夫人。”服务员答道。再说，布里特尔斯都三十好几了，还是这副德行，看来这辈子是改不了了。
foreignization: “布里特尔斯一直是个反应慢的男孩，夫人，”侍者回答道。顺便说一句，看到布里特尔斯已经当了三十多年的慢男孩，似乎他永远不可能成为一个快男孩了。

source: Oliver felt such fear come over him when he recognised the place, that, for the instant, he forgot the agony of his wound, and thought only of flight. Flight! He could scarcely stand: and if he were in full possession of all the best powers of his slight and youthful frame, whither could he fly? He pushed against the garden-gate; it was unlocked, and swung open on its hinges. He tottered across the lawn; climbed the steps; knocked faintly at the door; and, his whole strength failing him, sunk down against one of the pillars of the little portico.
domestication: 奥利弗认出了那个地方，恐惧顿时笼罩了他，一时之间，他竟忘了伤口的剧痛，满脑子只想逃跑。逃跑！他连站都快站不稳了，就算身体好得不能再好，他又能逃到哪儿去呢？他用力推了推花园的门，门没锁，吱呀一声开了。他摇摇晃晃地穿过草坪，爬上了台阶，有气无力地敲了敲门，全身的力气仿佛都被抽干了，最后瘫倒在门廊的一根柱子旁。
foreignization: 奥利弗认出那个地方时，感到如此恐惧，以至于在那一刻，他忘记了伤口的剧痛，只想着逃跑。逃跑！他几乎站都站不稳：即使他完全拥有自己那瘦弱年轻身体的最佳力量，他又能逃到哪里去呢？他推了推花园的门；门没锁，转动着铰链打开了。他摇摇晃晃地穿过草坪；爬上台阶；虚弱地敲了敲门；然后，他全身的力量都离开了他，倒在了小门廊的一根柱子上。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver felt such fear come over him when he recognised the place, that, for the instant, he forgot the agony of his wound, and thought only of flight. Flight! He could scarcely stand: and if he were in full possession of all the best powers of his slight and youthful frame, whither could he fly? He pushed against the garden-gate; it was unlocked, and swung open on its hinges. He tottered across the lawn; climbed the steps; knocked faintly at the door; and, his whole strength failing him, sunk down against one of the pillars of the little portico.

source: “If your answer be what I almost dare to hope it is,” retorted Harry, “it will shed a gleam of happiness upon my lonely way, and light the path before me. It is not an idle thing to do so much, by the utterance of a few brief words, for one who loves you beyond all else. Oh, Rose: in the name of my ardent and enduring attachment; in the name of all I have suffered for you, and all you doom me to undergo; answer me this one question!”
domestication: 哈利反驳道：“如果你的回答如我所几乎敢奢望的那样，那将给我的孤独之路带来一缕幸福的光芒，照亮我前方的道路。为了你这几句简短的话，对一个爱你胜过一切的人来说，绝不是无足轻重的事。哦，罗斯：以我对你的热烈而持久的爱为名；以我为你所受的一切苦难和你注定要我承受的一切为名；请回答我这一个问题！”
foreignization: “如果你的回答是我几乎敢希望的那样，”哈里反驳道，“它将在我孤独的路上投下幸福的光芒，并照亮我前方的道路。仅仅通过几句简短的话就为一个爱你胜过一切的人做这么多，这并不是一件轻率的事情。哦，罗丝：以我对你的热烈而持久的爱的名义；以我为你所承受的一切，以及你注定要我经历的一切的名义；回答我这个问题！”

Oh, Rose: in the name of my ardent and enduring attachment; in the name of all I have suffered for you, and all you doom me to undergo; answer me this one question!”

source: Oliver was frightened at the sight of so many gentlemen, which made him tremble: and the beadle gave him another tap behind, which made him cry. These two causes made him answer in a very low and hesitating voice; whereupon a gentleman in a white waistcoat said he was a fool. Which was a capital way of raising his spirits, and putting him quite at his ease.
domestication: 奥利弗一见到这么多先生就吓得直发抖，差役又在他背后推了一把，让他忍不住哭出声来。这两下子，他只能战战兢兢地小声回答。这时，一个穿白背心的先生说他是傻瓜。这可真是个让人心情舒畅的好办法，让他一下子放松了不少。不过这话听起来更像是在雪上加霜，让人心里更不是滋味。
foreignization: 奥利弗见到这么多绅士感到害怕，这使他发抖：教区执事又在他背后推了一下，这使他哭了起来。这两个原因使他回答时声音非常低且犹豫；这时一位穿着白色背心的绅士说他是一个傻子。这无疑是个极好的方式来提振他的精神，让他完全放松下来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver was frightened at the sight of so many gentlemen, which made him tremble: and the beadle gave him another tap behind, which made him cry. These two causes made him answer in a very low and hesitating voice; whereupon a gentleman in a white waistcoat said he was a fool. Which was a capital way of raising his spirits, and putting him quite at his ease.
奥利弗一见到这么多先生就吓得直发抖，差役又在他背后推了一把，让他忍不住哭出声来。这两下子，他只能战战兢兢地小声回答。这时，一个穿白背心的先生说他是傻瓜。这可真是个让人心情舒畅的好办法，让他一下子放松了不少。不过这话听起来更像是在雪上加霜，让人心里更不是滋味。

These two causes made him answer in a very low and hesitating voice; whereupon a gentleman in a white waistcoat said he was a fool. Which was a capital way of raising his spirits, and putting him quite at his ease.

source: “There is something in that boy’s face,” said the old gentleman to himself as he walked slowly away, tapping his chin with the cover of the book, in a thoughtful manner; “something that touches and interests me. Can he be innocent? He looked like— Bye the bye,” exclaimed the old gentleman, halting very abruptly, and staring up into the sky, “Bless my soul!—where have I seen something like that look before?”
domestication: 这男孩的脸上有些东西，老先生一边缓缓走着，一边用手中的书皮轻敲着下巴，若有所思地自言自语道：这孩子脸上有股让人动容的东西，真让人心生兴趣。他难道是清白的？他的眼神像极了——哎呀，等等，老先生突然停下脚步，抬头望向天空，惊呼道：我的天哪！我之前在哪里见过这样的目光？
foreignization: “那个男孩的脸上有些东西，”老先生边慢慢走开边自言自语，用书皮轻敲着下巴，一副若有所思的样子；“有些东西触动了我，让我感兴趣。他能是无辜的吗？他看起来像——等一下，”老先生突然停下脚步，抬头望向天空，“天哪！——我以前在哪里见过那样的神情？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“There is something in that boy’s face,” said the old gentleman to himself as he walked slowly away, tapping his chin with the cover of the book, in a thoughtful manner; “something that touches and interests me. Can he be innocent? He looked like— Bye the bye,” exclaimed the old gentleman, halting very abruptly, and staring up into the sky, “Bless my soul!—where have I seen something like that look before?”

“There is something in that boy’s face,” said the old gentleman to himself as he walked slowly away, tapping his chin with the cover of the book, in a thoughtful manner; “something that touches and interests me. Can he be innocent? He looked like— Bye the bye,” exclaimed the old gentleman, halting very abruptly, and staring up into the sky, “Bless my soul!—where have I seen something like that look before?”
“那个男孩的脸上有些东西，”老先生边慢慢走开边自言自语，用书皮轻敲着下巴，一副若有所思的样子；“有些东西触动了我，让我感兴趣。他能是无辜的吗？他看起来像——等一下，”老先生突然停下脚步，抬头望向天空，“天哪！——我以前在哪里见过那样的神情？”

source: “Those were his words,” said Nancy, glancing uneasily round, as she had scarcely ceased to do, since she began to speak, for a vision of Sikes haunted her perpetually. “And more. When he spoke of you and the other lady, and said it seemed contrived by Heaven, or the devil, against him, that Oliver should come into your hands, he laughed, and said there was some comfort in that too, for how many thousands and hundreds of thousands of pounds would you not give, if you had them, to know who your two-legged spaniel was.”
domestication: “这话是他亲口说的。”南希不安地环顾四周，自打她开口说话，这动作就没停过，因为赛克斯的影子一直在她脑海里挥之不去。“他还说了更多。说到你和那位女士时，他说奥利弗落到你们手里，似乎是天意，或是魔鬼在作怪。他笑着说这也算是一丝安慰，毕竟要是你们有成千上万的钱，你们愿意花多少钱来知道，你们的那条两脚狗究竟是谁啊。”
foreignization: “那些都是他的话，”南希说，不安地环顾四周，她自开始说话以来几乎一直没有停止过这种动作，因为赛克斯的幻影一直萦绕在她心头。“他还说了更多。当他说到你和那位女士，并说奥利弗落到你们手里似乎是上天或魔鬼针对他的安排时，他笑了，还说这里面也有一些安慰，因为如果你们有成千上万甚至数百万英镑，你们会愿意付出多少来知道你们的两腿走狗是谁。”

messages_domestication.content:

“Those were his words,” said Nancy, glancing uneasily round, as she had scarcely ceased to do, since she began to speak, for a vision of Sikes haunted her perpetually. “And more. When he spoke of you and the other lady, and said it seemed contrived by Heaven, or the devil, against him, that Oliver should come into your hands, he laughed, and said there was some comfort in that too, for how many thousands and hundreds of thousands of pounds would you not give, if you had them, to know who your two-legged spaniel was.”

source: For a second or two, Oliver glanced up the street, and down the street, and over the way: impressed with the belief that the unknown, who had addressed him through the key-hole, had walked a few paces off, to warm himself; for nobody did he see but a big charity-boy, sitting on a post in front of the house, eating a slice of bread and butter: which he cut into wedges, the size of his mouth, with a clasp-knife, and then consumed with great dexterity.
domestication: 奥利弗朝街上前后左右扫了一眼，心想刚才那个从钥匙孔里和他说话的陌生人，大概是走到不远处取暖去了。可是，除了一个坐在门前柱子上的大个儿慈善学校的孩子，他什么人也没看见。那孩子正拿着一片面包抹黄油，用一把折刀切成正好一口大小的三角块，然后灵巧地吃着。
foreignization: 奥利弗抬起头来，朝街的这头望了望，又朝那头望了望，还朝对面望了望：他心中认定，那个通过钥匙孔和他说话的陌生人，一定是走开了几步，去暖和一下自己；因为他除了一个坐在房子前面柱子上的大慈善学校的孩子外，什么人也没看见，那孩子正吃着一片涂了黄油的面包：他用一把折叠刀将面包切成和嘴一样大小的楔形，然后非常灵巧地吃了下去。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

For a second or two, Oliver glanced up the street, and down the street, and over the way: impressed with the belief that the unknown, who had addressed him through the key-hole, had walked a few paces off, to warm himself; for nobody did he see but a big charity-boy, sitting on a post in front of the house, eating a slice of bread and butter: which he cut into wedges, the size of his mouth, with a clasp-knife, and then consumed with great dexterity.

source: “And so it was you that was your own friend, was it?” asked Mr. Claypole, otherwise Bolter, when, by virtue of the compact entered into between them, he had removed next day to Fagin’s house. “Cod, I thought as much last night!”
domestication: “原来是你自己当自己的朋友啊？”克莱波尔先生，也就是博尔特，这么问道。他根据两人之间的约定，第二天就搬到了费金的家里。“天啊，我昨晚就猜到了！”
foreignization: “原来是你自己是自己的朋友，是吗？”克莱波尔先生，也就是博尔特，在依据他们之间达成的协议次日搬到费金家后问道。“天哪，我昨晚就料到了！”

“原来是你自己当自己的朋友啊？”克莱波尔先生，也就是博尔特，这么问道。他根据两人之间的约定，第二天就搬到了费金的家里。“天啊，我昨晚就猜到了！”

“原来是你自己是自己的朋友，是吗？”克莱波尔先生，也就是博尔特，在依据他们之间达成的协议次日搬到费金家后问道。“天哪，我昨晚就料到了！”

source: “When they had been separated for some time,” returned Mr. Brownlow, “and your mother, wholly given up to continental frivolities, had utterly forgotten the young husband ten good years her junior, who, with prospects blighted, lingered on at home, he fell among new friends. This circumstance, at least, you know already.”
domestication: 布朗洛先生说道：“他们分开了一段时间后，你母亲一心扑在欧洲的那些轻浮享乐上，彻底忘了她那位比她小十岁的年轻丈夫。他原本前途无量，却因她而断送，只能在家苦熬日子。后来，他交上了新朋友。这一点，你已经知道了。”
foreignization: “当他们分开了一段时间后，”布朗洛先生回答道，“你母亲完全沉浸在大陆的轻浮生活中，彻底忘记了那个比她年轻十岁的年轻丈夫，他因前途渺茫，一直留在家里，后来结交了新朋友。至少，这一点你已经知道了。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“When they had been separated for some time,” returned Mr. Brownlow, “and your mother, wholly given up to continental frivolities, had utterly forgotten the young husband ten good years her junior, who, with prospects blighted, lingered on at home, he fell among new friends. This circumstance, at least, you know already.”
布朗洛先生说道：“他们分开了一段时间后，你母亲一心扑在欧洲的那些轻浮享乐上，彻底忘了她那位比她小十岁的年轻丈夫。他原本前途无量，却因她而断送，只能在家苦熬日子。后来，他交上了新朋友。这一点，你已经知道了。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“When they had been separated for some time,” returned Mr. Brownlow, “and your mother, wholly given up to continental frivolities, had utterly forgotten the young husband ten good years her junior, who, with prospects blighted, lingered on at home, he fell among new friends. This circumstance, at least, you know already.”
“当他们分开了一段时间后，”布朗洛先生回答道，“你母亲完全沉浸在大陆的轻浮生活中，彻底忘记了那个比她年轻十岁的年轻丈夫，他因前途渺茫，一直留在家里，后来结交了新朋友。至少，这一点你已经知道了。”

source: For some time, Mr. Bumble drew Oliver along, without notice or remark; for the beadle carried his head very erect, as a beadle always should: and, it being a windy day, little Oliver was completely enshrouded by the skirts of Mr. Bumble’s coat as they blew open, and disclosed to great advantage his flapped waistcoat and drab plush knee-breeches. As they drew near to their destination, however, Mr. Bumble thought it expedient to look down, and see that the boy was in good order for inspection by his new master: which he accordingly did, with a fit and becoming air of gracious patronage.
domestication: 有一阵子，班布尔先生拉着奥利弗往前走，一句话也没说。班布尔先生挺着胸膛，摆出一副管事该有的威严模样；再加上那天风很大，小奥利弗完全被班布尔先生被风吹起的衣摆给遮住了，只能看到他那翻领背心和土黄色的绒布膝盖短裤。快到目的地时，班布尔先生觉得应该低头看看，确保这孩子在新主人面前能有个好样子。于是，他带着一副恰到好处的恩赐表情，俯身查看起奥利弗来。
foreignization: 有一段时间，班布尔先生拉着奥利弗走，没有注意到他或对他说什么；因为班布尔先生像一个执事应该做的那样，昂首挺胸地走着：而且那天风很大，小奥利弗完全被班布尔先生被风吹开的外套下摆遮住了，这反而很好地展示了他那带翻领的背心和驼色绒布膝裤。然而，当他们接近目的地时，班布尔先生认为有必要低头看看，确保这个男孩在新主人面前看起来体面：于是他确实这么做了，带着一种恰当而合适的恩主般的亲切姿态。

As they drew near to their destination, however, Mr. Bumble thought it expedient to look down, and see that the boy was in good order for inspection by his new master: which he accordingly did, with a fit and becoming air of gracious patronage.
有一阵子，班布尔先生拉着奥利弗往前走，一句话也没说。班布尔先生挺着胸膛，摆出一副管事该有的威严模样；再加上那天风很大，小奥利弗完全被班布尔先生被风吹起的衣摆给遮住了，只能看到他那翻领背心和土黄色的绒布膝盖短裤。快到目的地时，班布尔先生觉得应该低头看看，确保这孩子在新主人面前能有个好样子。于是，他带着一副恰到好处的恩赐表情，俯身查看起奥利弗来。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

For some time, Mr. Bumble drew Oliver along, without notice or remark; for the beadle carried his head very erect, as a beadle always should: and, it being a windy day, little Oliver was completely enshrouded by the skirts of Mr. Bumble’s coat as they blew open, and disclosed to great advantage his flapped waistcoat and drab plush knee-breeches. As they drew near to their destination, however, Mr. Bumble thought it expedient to look down, and see that the boy was in good order for inspection by his new master: which he accordingly did, with a fit and becoming air of gracious patronage.
有一段时间，班布尔先生拉着奥利弗走，没有注意到他或对他说什么；因为班布尔先生像一个执事应该做的那样，昂首挺胸地走着：而且那天风很大，小奥利弗完全被班布尔先生被风吹开的外套下摆遮住了，这反而很好地展示了他那带翻领的背心和驼色绒布膝裤。然而，当他们接近目的地时，班布尔先生认为有必要低头看看，确保这个男孩在新主人面前看起来体面：于是他确实这么做了，带着一种恰当而合适的恩主般的亲切姿态。

source: Oliver rose next morning, in better heart, and went about his usual occupations, with more hope and pleasure than he had known for many days. The birds were once more hung out, to sing, in their old places; and the sweetest wild flowers that could be found, were once more gathered to gladden Rose with their beauty. The melancholy which had seemed to the sad eyes of the anxious boy to hang, for days past, over every object, beautiful as all were, was dispelled by magic. The dew seemed to sparkle more brightly on the green leaves; the air to rustle among them with a sweeter music; and the sky itself to look more blue and bright. Such is the influence which the condition of our own thoughts, exercise, even over the appearance of external objects. Men who look on nature, and their fellow-men, and cry that all is dark and gloomy, are in the right; but the sombre colours are reflections from their own jaundiced eyes and hearts. The real hues are delicate, and need a clearer vision.
domestication: 奥利弗第二天早上心情好了许多，精神抖擞地开始了他日常的工作，心里的希望和快乐比过去几天多了不少。鸟儿们又挂出去，在原来的地方欢快地歌唱；最漂亮的野花也被重新采来，用它们的美丽让罗丝开怀。那些忧郁，似乎在过去的日子里，让焦虑的小男孩的眼睛看到的一切都蒙上了阴影，现在却像被施了魔法一样消失了。露珠在绿叶上闪得更亮了；微风在树叶间吹出更悦耳的音乐；天空也显得更蓝、更明亮了。人的心情和思想，竟然能影响到周围事物的样子，真是不可思议。那些看自然、看人，觉得一切都很黑暗、很阴郁的人，他们说的也没错；但那些阴暗的颜色，不过是从他们自己那黯淡的眼睛和心中反射出来的罢了。真正的色彩是柔和而细腻的，需要更加清澈的眼眸才能看见。
foreignization: 奥利弗第二天早上心情好转，带着比过去几天更多的希望和愉悦开始了他日常的工作。鸟儿再次被挂在了原来的地方，它们的歌声再次回荡；最美艳的野花也被重新采摘，用来以它们的美丽让罗丝高兴。这种忧郁，曾几何时，通过那焦虑男孩悲伤的眼睛看来，似乎笼罩在每个美丽的物体上，持续了数日，如今却如魔法般烟消云散了。露珠似乎在绿叶上更加明亮地闪耀；空气似乎在它们之间以更甜美的音乐沙沙作响；天空本身也显得更加蔚蓝和明亮。这就是我们思想状态的影响，即使对外部事物的外观也是如此。那些看着自然、看着他们的同类，然后哀叹一切皆黑暗阴郁的人，他们是对的；但是那些阴暗的色彩是来自他们自己黄疸的眼睛和心灵的反射。真正的色调是细腻的，需要更加清澈的目光。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver rose next morning, in better heart, and went about his usual occupations, with more hope and pleasure than he had known for many days. The birds were once more hung out, to sing, in their old places; and the sweetest wild flowers that could be found, were once more gathered to gladden Rose with their beauty. The melancholy which had seemed to the sad eyes of the anxious boy to hang, for days past, over every object, beautiful as all were, was dispelled by magic. The dew seemed to sparkle more brightly on the green leaves; the air to rustle among them with a sweeter music; and the sky itself to look more blue and bright. Such is the influence which the condition of our own thoughts, exercise, even over the appearance of external objects. Men who look on nature, and their fellow-men, and cry that all is dark and gloomy, are in the right; but the sombre colours are reflections from their own jaundiced eyes and hearts. The real hues are delicate, and need a clearer vision.

source: Morning drew on apace. The air became more sharp and piercing, as its first dull hue—the death of night, rather than the birth of day—glimmered faintly in the sky. The objects which had looked dim and terrible in the darkness, grew more and more defined, and gradually resolved into their familiar shapes. The rain came down, thick and fast, and pattered noisily among the leafless bushes. But, Oliver felt it not, as it beat against him; for he still lay stretched, helpless and unconscious, on his bed of clay.
domestication: 天色渐亮，寒意却愈发浓烈，天空中泛起的第一缕灰白，更像是夜的尽头而非日的初生，微弱地闪烁着。原本在黑暗中显得模糊而恐怖的景物，渐渐清晰起来，慢慢恢复了它们熟悉的模样。雨点密集而急促，落在光秃秃的灌木丛中，发出嘈杂的声音。但这一切都未能惊扰到奥利弗，他躺在泥泞的床上，毫无知觉，动弹不得。
foreignization: 黎明迅速逼近。空气变得更加刺骨，当第一缕昏暗的光线——更像是夜的死亡而非白昼的诞生——在天空中微微闪烁时。那些在黑暗中显得模糊而可怕的物体，逐渐变得清晰，渐渐恢复成它们熟悉的形状。雨下得又密又急，在无叶的灌木丛中发出嘈杂的声响。但是，奥利弗并没有感觉到雨水打在他身上；因为他仍然无助而毫无知觉地躺在泥泞的床上。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Morning drew on apace. The air became more sharp and piercing, as its first dull hue—the death of night, rather than the birth of day—glimmered faintly in the sky. The objects which had looked dim and terrible in the darkness, grew more and more defined, and gradually resolved into their familiar shapes. The rain came down, thick and fast, and pattered noisily among the leafless bushes. But, Oliver felt it not, as it beat against him; for he still lay stretched, helpless and unconscious, on his bed of clay.
天色渐亮，寒意却愈发浓烈，天空中泛起的第一缕灰白，更像是夜的尽头而非日的初生，微弱地闪烁着。原本在黑暗中显得模糊而恐怖的景物，渐渐清晰起来，慢慢恢复了它们熟悉的模样。雨点密集而急促，落在光秃秃的灌木丛中，发出嘈杂的声音。但这一切都未能惊扰到奥利弗，他躺在泥泞的床上，毫无知觉，动弹不得。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Morning drew on apace. The air became more sharp and piercing, as its first dull hue—the death of night, rather than the birth of day—glimmered faintly in the sky. The objects which had looked dim and terrible in the darkness, grew more and more defined, and gradually resolved into their familiar shapes. The rain came down, thick and fast, and pattered noisily among the leafless bushes. But, Oliver felt it not, as it beat against him; for he still lay stretched, helpless and unconscious, on his bed of clay.
黎明迅速逼近。空气变得更加刺骨，当第一缕昏暗的光线——更像是夜的死亡而非白昼的诞生——在天空中微微闪烁时。那些在黑暗中显得模糊而可怕的物体，逐渐变得清晰，渐渐恢复成它们熟悉的形状。雨下得又密又急，在无叶的灌木丛中发出嘈杂的声响。但是，奥利弗并没有感觉到雨水打在他身上；因为他仍然无助而毫无知觉地躺在泥泞的床上。

source: “Oh my gracious!” said the young woman, “I have found him! Oh! Oliver! Oliver! Oh you naughty boy, to make me suffer such distress on your account! Come home, dear, come. Oh, I’ve found him. Thank gracious goodness heavins, I’ve found him!” With these incoherent exclamations, the young woman burst into another fit of crying, and got so dreadfully hysterical, that a couple of women who came up at the moment asked a butcher’s boy with a shiny head of hair anointed with suet, who was also looking on, whether he didn’t think he had better run for the doctor. To which, the butcher’s boy: who appeared of a lounging, not to say indolent disposition: replied, that he thought not.
domestication: “哎哟天哪！”年轻女子喊道，“我找到他了！哦！奥利弗！奥利弗！你这淘气包，居然让我为你这么操心！快回家吧，亲爱的，快回来。哦，我找到你了。感谢老天，我终于找到你了！”说着这些断断续续的话，年轻女子又哭了起来，哭得那么伤心，以至于两个正好路过的大妈见状，问一个头发油光发亮的屠夫小伙，是不是应该去叫医生。这个看起来懒洋洋的屠夫小伙回答说，不用吧。
foreignization: “哦，天哪！”年轻女子说，“我找到他了！哦！奥利弗！奥利弗！哦，你这个淘气的男孩，竟然让我为你如此担忧！回家吧，亲爱的，回家。哦，我找到他了。感谢上天，我找到他了！”说着这些语无伦次的感叹，年轻女子又哭了起来，情绪变得非常歇斯底里，以至于当时走过来的两个女人问一个头发抹了牛油、闪闪发光的屠夫小伙，他是否认为最好去叫医生。对此，这个看起来懒散、甚至可以说是懒惰的屠夫小伙回答说，他认为不必。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Oh my gracious!” said the young woman, “I have found him! Oh! Oliver! Oliver! Oh you naughty boy, to make me suffer such distress on your account! Come home, dear, come. Oh, I’ve found him. Thank gracious goodness heavins, I’ve found him!” With these incoherent exclamations, the young woman burst into another fit of crying, and got so dreadfully hysterical, that a couple of women who came up at the moment asked a butcher’s boy with a shiny head of hair anointed with suet, who was also looking on, whether he didn’t think he had better run for the doctor. To which, the butcher’s boy: who appeared of a lounging, not to say indolent disposition: replied, that he thought not.
“哎哟天哪！”年轻女子喊道，“我找到他了！哦！奥利弗！奥利弗！你这淘气包，居然让我为你这么操心！快回家吧，亲爱的，快回来。哦，我找到你了。感谢老天，我终于找到你了！”说着这些断断续续的话，年轻女子又哭了起来，哭得那么伤心，以至于两个正好路过的大妈见状，问一个头发油光发亮的屠夫小伙，是不是应该去叫医生。这个看起来懒洋洋的屠夫小伙回答说，不用吧。

source: It was nearly two hours before day-break; that time which in the autumn of the year, may be truly called the dead of night; when the streets are silent and deserted; when even sounds appear to slumber, and profligacy and riot have staggered home to dream; it was at this still and silent hour, that Fagin sat watching in his old lair, with face so distorted and pale, and eyes so red and blood-shot, that he looked less like a man, than like some hideous phantom, moist from the grave, and worried by an evil spirit.
domestication: 天蒙蒙亮前的两个钟头，正是深秋时节真正的夜半时分，街上一片寂静，空无一人，连声音都像是在沉睡，那些放荡不羁的人也摇摇晃晃地回家做梦去了。就在这个万籁俱寂的时刻，费金坐在他那老窝里守候着，脸色扭曲苍白，眼睛红肿充血，看起来更像是刚从坟墓里爬出来的鬼怪，被邪灵缠身，根本不像是个人样。
foreignization: 天亮前将近两个小时；在一年中的秋天，这可以真正称为夜深人静的时刻；街道上寂静而荒凉；甚至连声音似乎都在沉睡，放荡和喧嚣已经摇摇晃晃地回家做梦去了；就在这个安静无声的时刻，费金坐在他那老旧的藏身之处，脸庞扭曲而苍白，眼睛红肿充血，看起来更像是从坟墓中渗出的可怖幽灵，被邪灵所困扰，而不像是个人。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was nearly two hours before day-break; that time which in the autumn of the year, may be truly called the dead of night; when the streets are silent and deserted; when even sounds appear to slumber, and profligacy and riot have staggered home to dream; it was at this still and silent hour, that Fagin sat watching in his old lair, with face so distorted and pale, and eyes so red and blood-shot, that he looked less like a man, than like some hideous phantom, moist from the grave, and worried by an evil spirit.

Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was nearly two hours before day-break; that time which in the autumn of the year, may be truly called the dead of night; when the streets are silent and deserted; when even sounds appear to slumber, and profligacy and riot have staggered home to dream; it was at this still and silent hour, that Fagin sat watching in his old lair, with face so distorted and pale, and eyes so red and blood-shot, that he looked less like a man, than like some hideous phantom, moist from the grave, and worried by an evil spirit.
天亮前将近两个小时；在一年中的秋天，这可以真正称为夜深人静的时刻；街道上寂静而荒凉；甚至连声音似乎都在沉睡，放荡和喧嚣已经摇摇晃晃地回家做梦去了；就在这个安静无声的时刻，费金坐在他那老旧的藏身之处，脸庞扭曲而苍白，眼睛红肿充血，看起来更像是从坟墓中渗出的可怖幽灵，被邪灵所困扰，而不像是个人。

source: The girl drew closer to the table, and glancing at Monks with an air of careless levity, withdrew her eyes; but as he turned towards Fagin, she stole another look; so keen and searching, and full of purpose, that if there had been any bystander to observe the change, he could hardly have believed the two looks to have proceeded from the same person.
domestication: 女孩靠近桌子，漫不经心地瞥了蒙克斯一眼，随即移开目光；可当蒙克斯转向费金时，她又偷偷瞄了一眼，那眼神锐利而深邃，充满目的性，要是有旁人在场看到这变化，恐怕都难以相信这是同一个人的眼神。
foreignization: 女孩走近桌子，漫不经心地瞥了蒙克斯一眼，然后移开了目光；但当他转向费金时，她又偷看了一眼，那眼神如此敏锐、探究，并且充满目的，如果当时有旁观者注意到这种变化，他几乎不会相信这两道目光出自同一个人。

source: The man followed to the chariot door, uttering the wildest imprecations and curses all the way; but as Mr. Losberne turned to speak to the driver, he looked into the carriage, and eyed Oliver for an instant with a glance so sharp and fierce and at the same time so furious and vindictive, that, waking or sleeping, he could not forget it for months afterwards. He continued to utter the most fearful imprecations, until the driver had resumed his seat; and when they were once more on their way, they could see him some distance behind: beating his feet upon the ground, and tearing his hair, in transports of real or pretended rage.
domestication: 那人一路跟到马车旁，嘴里骂个不停，诅咒连连。可当洛斯伯恩先生转头跟车夫说话时，他却往车厢里一瞧，盯着奥利弗狠狠地瞪了一眼，那眼神既凶狠又愤怒，仿佛要吃人一般，让奥利弗好几个月都忘不了。他继续骂个没完，直到车夫重新坐上驾驶座。等他们再次上路时，还能看见他远远地跟在后面，又是跺脚又是撕头发，一副真怒或假怒的样子，让人看了都觉得害怕。
foreignization: 那人跟到马车门口，一路上发出最疯狂的咒骂和诅咒；但当洛斯伯恩先生转过身来对司机说话时，他向马车里看了一眼，用一种既尖锐又凶猛、同时又充满愤怒和复仇的目光注视了奥利弗一瞬间，使得奥利弗在以后的几个月里无论醒着还是睡着都无法忘记这目光。他继续发出最可怕的咒骂，直到司机重新坐好；当他们再次上路时，还能看到他在后面一段距离处：用脚跺地，撕扯头发，处于狂怒或假装的狂怒之中。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He continued to utter the most fearful imprecations, until the driver had resumed his seat; and when they were once more on their way, they could see him some distance behind: beating his feet upon the ground, and tearing his hair, in transports of real or pretended rage.

source: The momentous interview was no sooner concluded, and Oliver composed to rest again, than the doctor, after wiping his eyes, and condemning them for being weak all at once, betook himself downstairs to open upon Mr. Giles. And finding nobody about the parlours, it occurred to him, that he could perhaps originate the proceedings with better effect in the kitchen; so into the kitchen he went.
domestication: 隆重的采访一结束，奥利弗就又躺下休息了。医生擦了擦眼睛，埋怨自己的眼泪说流就流，随后便下楼去找吉尔斯先生。他发现客厅里一个人也没有，心想或许在厨房开始谈话会更有效，于是就径直走进了厨房。
foreignization: 重大的采访一结束，奥利弗便再次安顿下来休息，医生擦了擦眼睛，责备它们突然变得脆弱，然后下楼去找吉尔斯先生。发现客厅里没有人，他想到或许可以在厨房更有效地开始行动；于是他就走进了厨房。

source: They led him through a paved room under the court, where some prisoners were waiting till their turns came, and others were talking to their friends, who crowded round a grate which looked into the open yard. There was nobody there to speak to him; but, as he passed, the prisoners fell back to render him more visible to the people who were clinging to the bars: and they assailed him with opprobrious names, and screeched and hissed. He shook his fist, and would have spat upon them; but his conductors hurried him on, through a gloomy passage lighted by a few dim lamps, into the interior of the prison.
domestication: 他们领着他穿过法庭下面的一间铺了石板的房间，那里有些囚犯正等着轮到自己，还有些在和围着栅栏的朋友说话，栅栏外面就是开放的院子。没人搭理他，但当他经过时，囚犯们自动让开，好让那些抓着栏杆的人看得更清楚。这些人对着他破口大骂，尖声怪叫，嘘声连连。他挥舞拳头，恨不得朝他们吐口水；但护送他的人却加快脚步，穿过一条昏暗的走廊，走廊上只有几盏昏黄的灯，把他带进了监狱深处。
foreignization: 他们带他穿过法庭下面的一间铺砌的房间，那里有些囚犯在等待轮到他们，还有一些人在与他们的朋友交谈，这些朋友挤在一块铁栅栏周围，铁栅栏通向露天的院子。那儿没有人跟他说话；但是，当他经过时，囚犯们退后，使那些紧抓着栏杆的人能更清楚地看到他：他们用侮辱性的名字攻击他，并尖声叫喊和嘘声。他挥动拳头，想朝他们吐口水；但他的押解者催促他快走，穿过一条由几盏昏暗的灯照明的阴暗通道，进入监狱的内部。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

They led him through a paved room under the court, where some prisoners were waiting till their turns came, and others were talking to their friends, who crowded round a grate which looked into the open yard. There was nobody there to speak to him; but, as he passed, the prisoners fell back to render him more visible to the people who were clinging to the bars: and they assailed him with opprobrious names, and screeched and hissed. He shook his fist, and would have spat upon them; but his conductors hurried him on, through a gloomy passage lighted by a few dim lamps, into the interior of the prison.
他们领着他穿过法庭下面的一间铺了石板的房间，那里有些囚犯正等着轮到自己，还有些在和围着栅栏的朋友说话，栅栏外面就是开放的院子。没人搭理他，但当他经过时，囚犯们自动让开，好让那些抓着栏杆的人看得更清楚。这些人对着他破口大骂，尖声怪叫，嘘声连连。他挥舞拳头，恨不得朝他们吐口水；但护送他的人却加快脚步，穿过一条昏暗的走廊，走廊上只有几盏昏黄的灯，把他带进了监狱深处。

source: Of all bad deeds that, under cover of the darkness, had been committed within wide London’s bounds since night hung over it, that was the worst. Of all the horrors that rose with an ill scent upon the morning air, that was the foulest and most cruel.
domestication: 在伦敦这偌大的城市里，自打夜幕降临，各种坏事层出不绝，但这件事最恶劣。清晨时分，各种恐怖之事如恶臭般弥漫开来，这件事最为残忍，让人无法容忍。
foreignization: 在夜幕笼罩下的广阔伦敦范围内所犯下的所有恶劣行为中，那件是最恶劣的。在随着清晨空气升起的所有恐怖之事中，那件是最污秽和最残忍的。

source: “Ah!” said the gentleman. “A Turk turns his face, after washing it well, to the East, when he says his prayers; these good people, after giving their faces such a rub against the World as to take the smiles off, turn with no less regularity, to the darkest side of Heaven. Between the Mussulman and the Pharisee, commend me to the first!”
domestication: “啊！”先生说道，“土耳其人在祷告时，会洗净脸，面向东方；这些人呢，经过世间的磨砺，笑容早已不在，却同样规律地转向了最黑暗的天空。相比之下，我宁愿选择穆斯林，而不是那些伪君子！”
foreignization: “啊！”那位先生说。“土耳其人在洗完脸后祈祷时，会面向东方；这些人，在与世界摩擦得连笑容都消失了之后，也同样规律地转向最黑暗的天际。在穆斯林和法利赛人之间，我更喜欢前者！”

messages_domestication.content:

Between the Mussulman and the Pharisee, commend me to the first!”

source: “I won’t stand by and see it done, Fagin,” cried the girl. “You’ve got the boy, and what more would you have?—Let him be—let him be—or I shall put that mark on some of you, that will bring me to the gallows before my time.”
domestication: “我不会袖手旁观的，费金！”女孩喊道。“你们已经抓到那男孩了，还想怎么样？——放过他吧，放过他吧——不然我会在你们身上留下记号，让我自己还没到时候就上绞架。”
foreignization: “我不会袖手旁观让你这么做的，费金，”女孩喊道。“你已经抓住了那男孩，你还想怎么样？——放过他——放过他——否则我会在你们某个人身上留下那个印记，那会让我提前走上绞刑架。”

source: The prudence of this line of action, indeed, was obvious; but, unfortunately, there was one very strong objection to its being adopted. This was, that the Dodger, and Charley Bates, and Fagin, and Mr. William Sikes, happened, one and all, to entertain a violent and deeply-rooted antipathy to going near a police-office on any ground or pretext whatever.
domestication: 这个做法的谨慎性显而易见，但可惜的是，有一个非常强烈的反对理由。原来，机灵鬼、查理·贝茨、费金和威廉·赛克斯这几位，个个都对靠近警察局这件事有着强烈的、根深蒂固的抵触情绪，不管有什么理由或借口，他们都避之不及。
foreignization: 这种做法的谨慎性，确实是很明显的；但是，不幸的是，有一个非常强烈的反对意见使得它无法被采纳。这是因为，机灵鬼、查理·贝茨、费金和威廉·西克斯先生，一个个都对以任何理由或借口接近警察局怀有强烈而根深蒂固的反感。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The prudence of this line of action, indeed, was obvious; but, unfortunately, there was one very strong objection to its being adopted. This was, that the Dodger, and Charley Bates, and Fagin, and Mr. William Sikes, happened, one and all, to entertain a violent and deeply-rooted antipathy to going near a police-office on any ground or pretext whatever.
这个做法的谨慎性显而易见，但可惜的是，有一个非常强烈的反对理由。原来，机灵鬼、查理·贝茨、费金和威廉·赛克斯这几位，个个都对靠近警察局这件事有着强烈的、根深蒂固的抵触情绪，不管有什么理由或借口，他们都避之不及。

source: “A pretty thing it would be, wouldn’t it to go and stop at the very first public-house outside the town, so that Sowerberry, if he come up after us, might poke in his old nose, and have us taken back in a cart with handcuffs on,” said Mr. Claypole in a jeering tone. “No! I shall go and lose myself among the narrowest streets I can find, and not stop till we come to the very out-of-the-wayest house I can set eyes on. Cod, yer may thanks yer stars I’ve got a head; for if we hadn’t gone, at first, the wrong road a purpose, and come back across country, yer’d have been locked up hard and fast a week ago, my lady. And serve yer right for being a fool.”
domestication: 要是一出城就停在头一个酒馆，让索尔伯里追上来，伸他那老鼻子进来，再把我们铐起来，装进马车送回去，那可真是好戏一场了，克雷波尔先生讥笑道。不！我要带着你们钻最窄的小巷，直到找到最偏僻的房子才停。嘿，你们得感谢我脑筋转得快；要是开始没故意走错路，再绕回来，你这小姐早就被关得牢牢的，一个星期前就完蛋了。活该，谁让你这么笨。
foreignization: “要是我们一出城就停在第一个酒馆，让索博瑞追上来，插进他的老鼻子，让我们被戴上手铐，用车子送回去，那该多好笑啊，”克莱波尔先生嘲讽地说。“不！我要去那些最狭窄的街道，直到找到一个最偏僻的房子才停下来。老天，你应该感谢我有头脑；因为我们一开始故意走错了路，然后又抄近路回来，要不然你一周前就会被关得紧紧的，我的小姐。活该你是个傻瓜。”

source: “All I know is,” said Mr. Losberne, at last: sitting down with a kind of desperate calmness, “that we must try and carry it off with a bold face. The object is a good one, and that must be our excuse. The boy has strong symptoms of fever upon him, and is in no condition to be talked to any more; that’s one comfort. We must make the best of it; and if bad be the best, it is no fault of ours. Come in!”
domestication: “我知道的就这些，”洛斯伯恩先生最后说道，坐下来，一脸决绝的平静，“咱们只能硬着头皮上了。目的总归是好的，这也算是我们的借口吧。那孩子高烧不退，已经没法再跟他说话了，这倒是件好事。咱们只能尽力而为了，要是结果不好，也不能怪我们。进来吧！”
foreignization: “我所知道的，”洛斯伯恩先生最后说道，坐下来带着一种近乎绝望的冷静，“是我们必须尝试装出一副大胆的样子来应对。目标是好的，这必须成为我们的借口。男孩身上有强烈的发热症状，已经没有条件再继续交谈了；这倒是一个安慰。我们必须尽力而为；如果最坏的情况发生了，那也不是我们的错。进来吧！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

We must make the best of it; and if bad be the best, it is no fault of ours. Come in!”

source: The contest, however, was too unequal to last long. Sikes had him down, and his knee was on his throat, when Crackit pulled him back with a look of alarm, and pointed to the window. There were lights gleaming below, voices in loud and earnest conversation, the tramp of hurried footsteps—endless they seemed in number—crossing the nearest wooden bridge. One man on horseback seemed to be among the crowd; for there was the noise of hoofs rattling on the uneven pavement. The gleam of lights increased; the footsteps came more thickly and noisily on. Then, came a loud knocking at the door, and then a hoarse murmur from such a multitude of angry voices as would have made the boldest quail.
domestication: 然而，这比赛太不对等了，根本没持续多久。希克斯一把按倒他，膝盖压在他喉咙上，这时克里基特惊恐地把他拉了回来，手指向窗户。窗外有灯光闪烁，人声嘈杂，脚步声急促而密集，似乎数不胜数，正从最近的木桥上走过。人群中好像还有个骑马的人，因为能听到马蹄在不平的地面上哒哒作响。灯光越来越亮，脚步声也越来越密、越来越响。接着，门口传来了大声的敲门声，随后是无数愤怒的声音低沉地咆哮着，就算是最胆大的人也会被吓破胆。
foreignization: 然而，比赛太不对等了，无法持续很久。西克斯把他按倒在地，膝盖压在他的喉咙上，这时克拉基特带着惊恐的表情把他拉了回来，并指向窗户。下面有灯光闪烁，声音在大声而急切地交谈，匆匆的脚步声——似乎数不胜数——正穿过最近的木桥。一个骑马的人似乎混在人群中；因为可以听到马蹄在不平的路面上发出的声响。灯光越来越亮；脚步声变得更加密集和嘈杂。接着，传来了敲门声，然后是一阵低沉的怒吼声，那是一大群愤怒的人发出的声音，足以让最勇敢的人也胆战心惊。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The contest, however, was too unequal to last long. Sikes had him down, and his knee was on his throat, when Crackit pulled him back with a look of alarm, and pointed to the window. There were lights gleaming below, voices in loud and earnest conversation, the tramp of hurried footsteps—endless they seemed in number—crossing the nearest wooden bridge. One man on horseback seemed to be among the crowd; for there was the noise of hoofs rattling on the uneven pavement. The gleam of lights increased; the footsteps came more thickly and noisily on. Then, came a loud knocking at the door, and then a hoarse murmur from such a multitude of angry voices as would have made the boldest quail.

source: Having disposed of these evil-minded persons for the night, Mr. Bumble sat himself down in the house at which the coach stopped; and took a temperate dinner of steaks, oyster sauce, and porter. Putting a glass of hot gin-and-water on the chimney-piece, he drew his chair to the fire; and, with sundry moral reflections on the too-prevalent sin of discontent and complaining, composed himself to read the paper.
domestication: 打发了这些心术不正的人后，邦布尔先生在马车停下的那家旅馆里坐了下来，吃了顿简单晚饭，有牛排、牡蛎酱和黑啤酒。他在壁炉架上放了一杯热杜松子酒加水，然后把椅子拉到火边。一边念叨着知足常乐的道理，抱怨世人总是不满现状、牢骚满腹，一边舒舒服服地看起了报纸。
foreignization: 打发了这些心怀恶意的人之后，邦布尔先生在马车停下的那所房子里坐了下来；吃了一顿适度的晚餐，包括牛排、牡蛎酱和黑啤酒。他在壁炉架上放了一杯热杜松子水，把椅子拉到火边；一边进行着关于不满和抱怨这种普遍罪行的道德反思，一边安顿下来读报纸。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Having disposed of these evil-minded persons for the night, Mr. Bumble sat himself down in the house at which the coach stopped; and took a temperate dinner of steaks, oyster sauce, and porter. Putting a glass of hot gin-and-water on the chimney-piece, he drew his chair to the fire; and, with sundry moral reflections on the too-prevalent sin of discontent and complaining, composed himself to read the paper.
打发了这些心术不正的人后，邦布尔先生在马车停下的那家旅馆里坐了下来，吃了顿简单晚饭，有牛排、牡蛎酱和黑啤酒。他在壁炉架上放了一杯热杜松子酒加水，然后把椅子拉到火边。一边念叨着知足常乐的道理，抱怨世人总是不满现状、牢骚满腹，一边舒舒服服地看起了报纸。

source: He was roused by a touch on the shoulder, and a request from the man with the keys to follow him into the office. He closed his book hastily; and was at once ushered into the imposing presence of the renowned Mr. Fang.
domestication: 他肩膀上被人轻轻拍了一下，拿着钥匙的人请他跟着去办公室。他赶忙合上书，就被领进了大名鼎鼎的方先生面前。方先生的气场可真不是盖的。
foreignization: 他被肩上的一触和拿钥匙的人要求跟随着他进入办公室而唤醒。他急忙合上书；随即被引见给著名的方先生那威严的身影。

He was roused by a touch on the shoulder, and a request from the man with the keys to follow him into the office. He closed his book hastily; and was at once ushered into the imposing presence of the renowned Mr. Fang.
他肩膀上被人轻轻拍了一下，拿着钥匙的人请他跟着去办公室。他赶忙合上书，就被领进了大名鼎鼎的方先生面前。方先生的气场可真不是盖的。
