Crackit went to the window, and shaking all over, drew in his head. There was no need to tell them who it was; his pale face was enough. The dog too was on the alert in an instant, and ran whining to the door.
克拉基特走到窗前，浑身发抖，缩回了头。没有必要告诉他们是谁；他那苍白的脸就足够了。狗也立刻警觉起来，哀鸣着跑向门口。

source: “Bless us, and save us! Wash your hands, and let me part your hair nicely for you, child,” said Mrs. Bedwin. “Dear heart alive! If we had known he would have asked for you, we would have put you a clean collar on, and made you as smart as sixpence!”
domestication: 保佑我们，救救我们吧！快去洗手，孩子，让我来帮你好好梳梳头。天哪，要是早知道他会叫你，咱们肯定得给你换上干净的领子，打扮得漂漂亮亮的，就像新的一样！
foreignization: “保佑我们，拯救我们！洗洗手，让我帮你好好梳梳头发，孩子，”贝德温太太说。“天哪！如果我们知道他会叫你，我们早就给你换上干净的领子，把你打扮得像六便士一样整洁了！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Bless us, and save us! Wash your hands, and let me part your hair nicely for you, child,” said Mrs. Bedwin. “Dear heart alive! If we had known he would have asked for you, we would have put you a clean collar on, and made you as smart as sixpence!”
保佑我们，救救我们吧！快去洗手，孩子，让我来帮你好好梳梳头。天哪，要是早知道他会叫你，咱们肯定得给你换上干净的领子，打扮得漂漂亮亮的，就像新的一样！

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Bless us, and save us! Wash your hands, and let me part your hair nicely for you, child,” said Mrs. Bedwin. “Dear heart alive! If we had known he would have asked for you, we would have put you a clean collar on, and made you as smart as sixpence!”
“保佑我们，拯救我们！洗洗手，让我帮你好好梳梳头发，孩子，”贝德温太太说。“天哪！如果我们知道他会叫你，我们早就给你换上干净的领子，把你打扮得像六便士一样整洁了！”

source: In pursuance of this determination, little Oliver, to his excessive astonishment, was released from bondage, and ordered to put himself into a clean shirt. He had hardly achieved this very unusual gymnastic performance, when Mr. Bumble brought him, with his own hands, a basin of gruel, and the holiday allowance of two ounces and a quarter of bread. At this tremendous sight, Oliver began to cry very piteously: thinking, not unnaturally, that the board must have determined to kill him for some useful purpose, or they never would have begun to fatten him up in that way.
domestication: 为了达到这个目的，小奥利弗大吃一惊地被从劳役中解放出来，还被命令穿上干净的衬衫。他好不容易完成了这少见的“体操”后，邦布尔先生亲自给他端来了一盆稀饭，还有节日里额外分发的两盎司又四分之一的面包。一看到这阵势，奥利弗开始可怜兮兮地哭了起来：他心想，董事会肯定是决定要拿他做点什么有用的事了，不然他们绝不会这么破天荒地给他“补养”。
foreignization: 为了实现这一决心，小奥利弗被从束缚中释放出来，并被命令换上一件干净的衬衫。他刚刚完成了这个非常不寻常的体操动作，班布尔先生就亲自给他端来了一盆粥和节日配给的两盎司又四分之一的面包。看到这令人震惊的景象，奥利弗开始非常可怜地哭泣：他想到，董事会一定是决定为了某种有用的目的而要杀死他，否则他们绝不会以这种方式开始给他喂肥。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In pursuance of this determination, little Oliver, to his excessive astonishment, was released from bondage, and ordered to put himself into a clean shirt. He had hardly achieved this very unusual gymnastic performance, when Mr. Bumble brought him, with his own hands, a basin of gruel, and the holiday allowance of two ounces and a quarter of bread. At this tremendous sight, Oliver began to cry very piteously: thinking, not unnaturally, that the board must have determined to kill him for some useful purpose, or they never would have begun to fatten him up in that way.
为了达到这个目的，小奥利弗大吃一惊地被从劳役中解放出来，还被命令穿上干净的衬衫。他好不容易完成了这少见的“体操”后，邦布尔先生亲自给他端来了一盆稀饭，还有节日里额外分发的两盎司又四分之一的面包。一看到这阵势，奥利弗开始可怜兮兮地哭了起来：他心想，董事会肯定是决定要拿他做点什么有用的事了，不然他们绝不会这么破天荒地给他“补养”。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In pursuance of this determination, little Oliver, to his excessive astonishment, was released from bondage, and ordered to put himself into a clean shirt. He had hardly achieved this very unusual gymnastic performance, when Mr. Bumble brought him, with his own hands, a basin of gruel, and the holiday allowance of two ounces and a quarter of bread. At this tremendous sight, Oliver began to cry very piteously: thinking, not unnaturally, that the board must have determined to kill him for some useful purpose, or they never would have begun to fatten him up in that way.
为了实现这一决心，小奥利弗被从束缚中释放出来，并被命令换上一件干净的衬衫。他刚刚完成了这个非常不寻常的体操动作，班布尔先生就亲自给他端来了一盆粥和节日配给的两盎司又四分之一的面包。看到这令人震惊的景象，奥利弗开始非常可怜地哭泣：他想到，董事会一定是决定为了某种有用的目的而要杀死他，否则他们绝不会以这种方式开始给他喂肥。

source: In compliance with Mr. Fagin’s request, the Artful untied this bundle, which was of large size, and formed of an old table-cloth; and handed the articles it contained, one by one, to Charley Bates: who placed them on the table, with various encomiums on their rarity and excellence.
domestication: 按费金先生的要求，狡猾的小子解开了这个大包裹，里头包着一块旧桌布。他一件件地把东西递给查理·贝茨，查理则把这些东西放在桌子上，一边放还一边不住口地称赞这些物件的稀罕和珍贵。
foreignization: 按照费金先生的要求，机灵鬼解开了这个大包裹，它是用一块旧桌布包着的；并将里面的东西一件件递给了查理·贝茨：后者将它们放在桌子上，同时对它们的稀有和优良品质赞不绝口。

source: When she reached the more wealthy quarter of the town, the streets were comparatively deserted; and here her headlong progress excited a still greater curiosity in the stragglers whom she hurried past. Some quickened their pace behind, as though to see whither she was hastening at such an unusual rate; and a few made head upon her, and looked back, surprised at her undiminished speed; but they fell off one by one; and when she neared her place of destination, she was alone.
domestication: 她一走进镇上的富人区，街上就显得冷清多了。她这副拼命三郎的架势，更让那些零星路人感到好奇。有人见状也加快了脚步，想看看她急急忙忙往哪儿赶；还有几个试图追上她，回头一看，惊讶于她的速度始终不减；但这些人一个个都掉队了。等她快到目的地时，身边已无人相伴。
foreignization: 当她到达镇上较为富裕的区域时，街道相对冷清；在这里，她急匆匆的前进更加引起了那些被她匆匆超过的零星行人的注意。有些人加快了步伐跟在后面，好像要看她以如此不寻常的速度急着去往何处；有几个人甚至迎面走来，回头望去，对她未减的速度感到惊讶；但他们一个接一个地落后了；当她接近目的地时，她已经独自一人。

source: It was a chill, damp, windy night, when the Jew: buttoning his great-coat tight round his shrivelled body, and pulling the collar up over his ears so as completely to obscure the lower part of his face: emerged from his den. He paused on the step as the door was locked and chained behind him; and having listened while the boys made all secure, and until their retreating footsteps were no longer audible, slunk down the street as quickly as he could.
domestication: 那是个寒风刺骨、湿冷的夜晚，老犹太人缩着瘦弱的身体，紧紧扣上大衣，把领子竖起来遮住半张脸，才从他藏身的地洞里钻出来。他在门口停了一下，等门锁好、链条挂上后，听着几个小子在里面忙活，直到他们的脚步声渐渐远去，听不见了，才像只老鼠似的，飞快地溜进了夜色中。
foreignization: 那是一个寒冷、潮湿、多风的夜晚，当那个犹太人：将大衣紧紧地扣在他干瘪的身体上，并把衣领拉到耳朵上，完全遮住了他脸的下半部分时：从他的藏身处走了出来。他在门口停了一下，等门被锁上并上了链；在听到孩子们确保一切安全，并直到他们逐渐远去的脚步声不再听得见后，他尽可能快地溜下了街道。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: “There—there’s enough of that,” interposed Sikes, impatiently; and stooping over his recumbant friend, he whispered a few words in his ear: at which Mr. Crackit laughed immensely, and honoured Oliver with a long stare of astonishment.
domestication: “行了——差不多得了。”赛克斯不耐烦地插话道，然后他俯身在倒下的朋友耳边低语了几句，克瑞基特笑得前仰后合，还盯着奥利弗看了好一会儿，那眼神里满是惊讶。
foreignization: “好—好啦，够了，”赛克斯不耐烦地插话道，并俯身向他躺着的朋友耳边低声说了几句话：克里基特先生听了大笑起来，并以长时间的惊讶目光注视着奥利弗。

“There—there’s enough of that,” interposed Sikes, impatiently; and stooping over his recumbant friend, he whispered a few words in his ear: at which Mr. Crackit laughed immensely, and honoured Oliver with a long stare of astonishment.

source: “This is far enough,” said a voice, which was evidently that of the gentleman. “I will not suffer the young lady to go any farther. Many people would have distrusted you too much to have come even so far, but you see I am willing to humour you.”
domestication: “这就够远了，”一个显然是那位先生的声音说道，“我可不能让这位小姐再往前走了。换了别人，恐怕走到一半就信不过你了，但我看你这副样子，也就勉强配合你一回。”
foreignization: “这就够远了，”一个显然是那位先生的声音说道，“我不允许这位年轻的女士再往前走了。许多人甚至到这里都会对你太过不信任，但你看到，我愿意迁就你。”

“This is far enough,” said a voice, which was evidently that of the gentleman. “I will not suffer the young lady to go any farther. Many people would have distrusted you too much to have come even so far, but you see I am willing to humour you.”
“这就够远了，”一个显然是那位先生的声音说道，“我可不能让这位小姐再往前走了。换了别人，恐怕走到一半就信不过你了，但我看你这副样子，也就勉强配合你一回。”

“This is far enough,” said a voice, which was evidently that of the gentleman. “I will not suffer the young lady to go any farther. Many people would have distrusted you too much to have come even so far, but you see I am willing to humour you.”
“这就够远了，”一个显然是那位先生的声音说道，“我不允许这位年轻的女士再往前走了。许多人甚至到这里都会对你太过不信任，但你看到，我愿意迁就你。”

source: But it was pure, earnest, joyful reality. They drove straight to the door of the chief hotel (which Oliver used to stare up at, with awe, and think a mighty palace, but which had somehow fallen off in grandeur and size); and here was Mr. Grimwig all ready to receive them, kissing the young lady, and the old one too, when they got out of the coach, as if he were the grandfather of the whole party, all smiles and kindness, and not offering to eat his head—no, not once; not even when he contradicted a very old postboy about the nearest road to London, and maintained he knew it best, though he had only come that way once, and that time fast asleep. There was dinner prepared, and there were bedrooms ready, and everything was arranged as if by magic.
domestication: 但这却是一番纯真、热切、欢乐的现实。他们直奔镇上最大的旅馆（奥利弗以前总仰望着它，觉得像一座雄伟的宫殿，但不知怎么的，它似乎失去了往日的辉煌和规模）。一到门口，就见格里姆威格先生早已等候多时，当两位女士下车时，他亲吻了她们，仿佛是全家的长辈一样，满脸笑容，和蔼可亲，连一次都没说要吃掉自己的头。即便是跟一个老邮差争执去伦敦最近的路时，他也没有发火，坚持说他知道得最清楚，尽管他只来过这里一次，而且那次还是在梦中。桌上已经备好了晚餐，房间也准备妥当，一切都像是变魔术似的安排好了。
foreignization: 但这是一片纯真、热切、快乐的现实。他们径直开到了首席旅馆的大门（奥利弗以前常常怀着敬畏之情仰望这座大楼，认为它是一座宏伟的宫殿，但不知怎么的，它的雄伟和规模似乎已经减退了）；在这里，格里姆威格先生已经准备好迎接他们，当他们从马车里出来时，他亲吻了那位年轻女士，也亲吻了年长的女士，仿佛他是整个派对的祖父，满脸笑容和善意，没有表示要吃掉自己的头——不，一次也没有；甚至当他与一个非常老的邮差争论去伦敦最近的路时，坚持说他知道得最好，尽管他只来过这条路一次，而且那次还是在沉睡中。已经准备好了晚餐，房间也已经准备好，一切都像是魔法般安排好了。

They drove straight to the door of the chief hotel (which Oliver used to stare up at, with awe, and think a mighty palace, but which had somehow fallen off in grandeur and size); and here was Mr. Grimwig all ready to receive them, kissing the young lady, and the old one too, when they got out of the coach, as if he were the grandfather of the whole party, all smiles and kindness, and not offering to eat his head—no, not once; not even when he contradicted a very old postboy about the nearest road to London, and maintained he knew it best, though he had only come that way once, and that time fast asleep. There was dinner prepared, and there were bedrooms ready, and everything was arranged as if by magic.

source: Sikes, invoking terrific imprecations upon Fagin’s head for sending Oliver on such an errand, plied the crowbar vigorously, but with little noise. After some delay, and some assistance from Toby, the shutter to which he had referred, swung open on its hinges.
domestication: 赛克斯一边咒骂着费金派奥利佛来干这种活，一边卖力地挥动着撬棍，但动作尽量轻声。折腾了好一阵子，在托比的帮忙下，那扇他提到的窗户终于吱呀一声打开了。

source: “I told you before,” replied Nancy, “that I was afraid to speak to you there. I don’t know why it is,” said the girl, shuddering, “but I have such a fear and dread upon me tonight that I can hardly stand.”
domestication: 我之前跟你说过，南希回答道，我害怕在那里跟你说话。这姑娘打了个寒颤，接着说，不知怎么的，今晚我心里特别害怕，连站都快站不稳了。
foreignization: “我以前告诉过你，”南希回答，“我在那里不敢和你说话。我不知道是怎么回事，”女孩颤抖着说，“但今晚我害怕得几乎站不住了。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I told you before,” replied Nancy, “that I was afraid to speak to you there. I don’t know why it is,” said the girl, shuddering, “but I have such a fear and dread upon me tonight that I can hardly stand.”
我之前跟你说过，南希回答道，我害怕在那里跟你说话。这姑娘打了个寒颤，接着说，不知怎么的，今晚我心里特别害怕，连站都快站不稳了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I told you before,” replied Nancy, “that I was afraid to speak to you there. I don’t know why it is,” said the girl, shuddering, “but I have such a fear and dread upon me tonight that I can hardly stand.”
“我以前告诉过你，”南希回答，“我在那里不敢和你说话。我不知道是怎么回事，”女孩颤抖着说，“但今晚我害怕得几乎站不住了。”

source: Oliver was about to say that he would go along with anybody with great readiness, when, glancing upward, he caught sight of Mrs. Mann, who had got behind the beadle’s chair, and was shaking her fist at him with a furious countenance. He took the hint at once, for the fist had been too often impressed upon his body not to be deeply impressed upon his recollection.
domestication: 奥利弗正想说他非常乐意跟任何人走，可一抬头，看见曼太太站在执事椅背后，气得满脸通红，挥着拳头冲他比划。他立刻明白了她的意思，这拳头在他身上留下的印象太深了，让他一辈子也忘不了。
foreignization: 奥利弗正要说他很乐意跟任何人一起去，但就在这时，他抬头看到了曼太太，她已经躲到了教区执事的椅子后面，正怒气冲冲地向他挥舞着拳头。他立刻明白了这个暗示，因为那只拳头曾经多次在他的身上留下印记，使他对此记忆犹新。

source: “The term you use,” said Mr. Brownlow, sternly, “is a reproach to those long since passed beyond the feeble censure of the world. It reflects disgrace on no one living, except you who use it. Let that pass. He was born in this town.”
domestication: “你用的这个词，”布朗洛先生严厉地说，“是对那些早已不在人世的人的一种诋毁。这词除了你自己以外，不会给任何活人带来耻辱。咱们不说这个了。他就是在本镇出生的。”
foreignization: “你使用的这个词，”布朗洛先生严厉地说，“是对那些早已超出世间微弱谴责的人的诋毁。它除了使用它的人以外，不会给任何活人带来耻辱。我们不谈这个了。他出生在这个镇上。”

“The term you use,” said Mr. Brownlow, sternly, “is a reproach to those long since passed beyond the feeble censure of the world. It reflects disgrace on no one living, except you who use it. Let that pass. He was born in this town.”
“你使用的这个词，”布朗洛先生严厉地说，“是对那些早已超出世间微弱谴责的人的诋毁。它除了使用它的人以外，不会给任何活人带来耻辱。我们不谈这个了。他出生在这个镇上。”

source: Oliver walked twenty miles that day; and all that time tasted nothing but the crust of dry bread, and a few draughts of water, which he begged at the cottage-doors by the road-side. When the night came, he turned into a meadow; and, creeping close under a hay-rick, determined to lie there, till morning. He felt frightened at first, for the wind moaned dismally over the empty fields, and he was cold and hungry, and more alone than he had ever felt before. Being very tired with his walk, however, he soon fell asleep and forgot his troubles.
domestication: 奥利弗那天走了二十英里路，一路上除了干面包皮和在路边农舍讨来的几口水，什么东西都没吃。天黑时，他拐进了一片草地，钻到一个草垛下面，决定就在这儿过夜，等到天亮再走。一开始他有点害怕，因为风在空旷的田野上呼啸，冷飕飕的，又饿又孤单，比以往任何时候都感到无助。不过，因为他实在是太累了，很快就睡着了，把烦恼都抛到了脑后。
foreignization: 奥利弗那天走了二十英里；在这段时间里，他除了吃了一块干面包皮和几口水外，什么也没吃，这些水是他沿途在路边的农舍门口乞讨来的。当夜幕降临时，他走进一片草地；爬到一个草堆下面，决定在那里躺到天亮。起初他感到害怕，因为风凄凉地吹过空旷的田野，他又冷又饿，比以前任何时候都更加孤独。然而，由于走得非常累，他很快就睡着了，忘掉了他的烦恼。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver walked twenty miles that day; and all that time tasted nothing but the crust of dry bread, and a few draughts of water, which he begged at the cottage-doors by the road-side. When the night came, he turned into a meadow; and, creeping close under a hay-rick, determined to lie there, till morning. He felt frightened at first, for the wind moaned dismally over the empty fields, and he was cold and hungry, and more alone than he had ever felt before. Being very tired with his walk, however, he soon fell asleep and forgot his troubles.
奥利弗那天走了二十英里路，一路上除了干面包皮和在路边农舍讨来的几口水，什么东西都没吃。天黑时，他拐进了一片草地，钻到一个草垛下面，决定就在这儿过夜，等到天亮再走。一开始他有点害怕，因为风在空旷的田野上呼啸，冷飕飕的，又饿又孤单，比以往任何时候都感到无助。不过，因为他实在是太累了，很快就睡着了，把烦恼都抛到了脑后。

source: “Pretty well, I think, for a beginner,” remarked Mr. Bolter complacently. “The pots I took off airy railings, and the milk-can was standing by itself outside a public-house. I thought it might get rusty with the rain, or catch cold, yer know. Eh? Ha! ha! ha!”
domestication: 博尔特先生自鸣得意地说：“我觉得对于一个新手来说，干得还不错。”他接着说，“那些花盆我是从栏杆上拿的，牛奶罐子是放在一家酒馆外头的。我想这雨天它可能会生锈，或者着凉，你知道的。哈哈，哈哈！”
foreignization: “对于一个初学者来说，我觉得还不错，”博尔特先生自鸣得意地说。“那些花盆是我从栏杆上拿下来的，而牛奶罐则是自己放在一家酒店外面的。我想它可能会被雨水锈蚀，或者着凉，你知道的。嗯？哈！哈！哈！”

“Pretty well, I think, for a beginner,” remarked Mr. Bolter complacently. “The pots I took off airy railings, and the milk-can was standing by itself outside a public-house. I thought it might get rusty with the rain, or catch cold, yer know. Eh? Ha! ha! ha!”
博尔特先生自鸣得意地说：“我觉得对于一个新手来说，干得还不错。”他接着说，“那些花盆我是从栏杆上拿的，牛奶罐子是放在一家酒馆外头的。我想这雨天它可能会生锈，或者着凉，你知道的。哈哈，哈哈！”

“Pretty well, I think, for a beginner,” remarked Mr. Bolter complacently. “The pots I took off airy railings, and the milk-can was standing by itself outside a public-house. I thought it might get rusty with the rain, or catch cold, yer know. Eh? Ha! ha! ha!”
“对于一个初学者来说，我觉得还不错，”博尔特先生自鸣得意地说。“那些花盆是我从栏杆上拿下来的，而牛奶罐则是自己放在一家酒店外面的。我想它可能会被雨水锈蚀，或者着凉，你知道的。嗯？哈！哈！哈！”

source: These preliminaries adjusted, Mr. Sikes proceeded to drink brandy at a furious rate, and to flourish the crowbar in an alarming manner; yelling forth, at the same time, most unmusical snatches of song, mingled with wild execrations. At length, in a fit of professional enthusiasm, he insisted upon producing his box of housebreaking tools: which he had no sooner stumbled in with, and opened for the purpose of explaining the nature and properties of the various implements it contained, and the peculiar beauties of their construction, than he fell over the box upon the floor, and went to sleep where he fell.
domestication: 这些准备工作一做完，赛克斯先生就开始狂喝白兰地，挥舞着撬棍，样子十分吓人；同时，他还不停地吼出一些不着调的歌，夹杂着野蛮的咒骂。终于，在职业热情的驱使下，他坚持要拿出自己的入室盗窃工具箱。刚一踉踉跄跄地拿来，打开箱子，准备解释里面各种工具的用途和构造之美，就一头栽倒在箱子上，呼呼大睡起来。
foreignization: 这些准备工作完成后，西克斯先生开始以惊人的速度喝白兰地，并以令人不安的方式挥舞着撬棍；同时，他大声吼出最不和谐的歌曲片段，夹杂着狂野的咒骂。最终，在职业热情的驱使下，他坚持要展示他的入室盗窃工具箱：他一瘸一拐地把它带进来，打开箱子，打算解释箱内各种工具的性质和特点，以及它们构造的独特之处，但他刚一打开箱子，就跌倒在箱子上，倒在地上睡着了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

These preliminaries adjusted, Mr. Sikes proceeded to drink brandy at a furious rate, and to flourish the crowbar in an alarming manner; yelling forth, at the same time, most unmusical snatches of song, mingled with wild execrations. At length, in a fit of professional enthusiasm, he insisted upon producing his box of housebreaking tools: which he had no sooner stumbled in with, and opened for the purpose of explaining the nature and properties of the various implements it contained, and the peculiar beauties of their construction, than he fell over the box upon the floor, and went to sleep where he fell.
这些准备工作一做完，赛克斯先生就开始狂喝白兰地，挥舞着撬棍，样子十分吓人；同时，他还不停地吼出一些不着调的歌，夹杂着野蛮的咒骂。终于，在职业热情的驱使下，他坚持要拿出自己的入室盗窃工具箱。刚一踉踉跄跄地拿来，打开箱子，准备解释里面各种工具的用途和构造之美，就一头栽倒在箱子上，呼呼大睡起来。

At length, in a fit of professional enthusiasm, he insisted upon producing his box of housebreaking tools: which he had no sooner stumbled in with, and opened for the purpose of explaining the nature and properties of the various implements it contained, and the peculiar beauties of their construction, than he fell over the box upon the floor, and went to sleep where he fell.

source: Thoroughly overpowered with the notion of Mr. Chitling being the victim of the tender passion, Master Bates threw himself back in his chair with such violence, that he lost his balance, and pitched over upon the floor; where (the accident abating nothing of his merriment) he lay at full length until his laugh was over, when he resumed his former position, and began another laugh.
domestication: 巴茨先生一想到奇林先生竟成了恋爱的牺牲品，便猛地往后一靠，椅子一下子失去了平衡，他整个人扑倒在了地上。这一摔可没让他消停，反而笑得更起劲了，躺在地上直乐，直到笑够了才爬起来，坐回椅子上，又开始大笑起来。
foreignization: 彻底被奇林先生是柔情的牺牲品这一想法所压倒，贝茨少爷往后一仰，椅子因受到如此猛烈的力道而失去了平衡，他整个人向后摔倒在地板上；（这次意外丝毫没有减少他的欢乐）他全身伸展躺在地上，一直笑到笑够了，才恢复到原来的位置，又开始大笑起来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Thoroughly overpowered with the notion of Mr. Chitling being the victim of the tender passion, Master Bates threw himself back in his chair with such violence, that he lost his balance, and pitched over upon the floor; where (the accident abating nothing of his merriment) he lay at full length until his laugh was over, when he resumed his former position, and began another laugh.

彻底被奇林先生是柔情的牺牲品这一想法所压倒，贝茨少爷往后一仰，椅子因受到如此猛烈的力道而失去了平衡，他整个人向后摔倒在地板上；（这次意外丝毫没有减少他的欢乐）他全身伸展躺在地上，一直笑到笑够了，才恢复到原来的位置，又开始大笑起来。

source: “You’ll drive me on the something desperate,” muttered the girl placing both hands upon her breast, as though to keep down by force some violent outbreak. “Let me go, will you,—this minute—this instant.”
domestication: “你再这样下去，我可要急疯了。”女孩说着，两手按在胸口，像是要强压下心中的狂躁。“你快放开我，这会儿，这瞬间。”
foreignization: “你会逼我做出什么绝望的事情的，”女孩喃喃道，双手放在胸前，仿佛要用力压制住某种强烈的爆发。“让我走，你听见没有——现在就放我走——立刻。”

“你再这样下去，我可要急疯了。”女孩说着，两手按在胸口，像是要强压下心中的狂躁。“你快放开我，这会儿，这瞬间。”

source: Brittles looked doubtfully at Mr. Giles; Mr. Giles looked doubtfully at Brittles; the constable put his hand behind his ear, to catch the reply; the two women and the tinker leaned forward to listen; the doctor glanced keenly round; when a ring was heard at the gate, and at the same moment, the sound of wheels.
domestication: 布里特尔斯怀疑地看着吉尔斯先生；吉尔斯先生也怀疑地看着布里特尔斯；警察把手放到耳朵后面，想听清回答；那两个女人和补锅匠往前倾身子听着；医生敏锐地环视四周；就在这时，大门外传来了敲门声，紧接着是车轮滚动的声音。
foreignization: 布里特尔斯怀疑地看了看吉尔斯先生；吉尔斯先生怀疑地看了看布里特尔斯；警官把手放在耳朵后面，以便听到回答；那两个女人和补锅匠 leans 前倾倾听；医生敏锐地环视四周；这时，大门响起了门铃声，同时传来了车轮的声音。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Brittles looked doubtfully at Mr. Giles; Mr. Giles looked doubtfully at Brittles; the constable put his hand behind his ear, to catch the reply; the two women and the tinker leaned forward to listen; the doctor glanced keenly round; when a ring was heard at the gate, and at the same moment, the sound of wheels.

source: “Here’s the house broken into,” said the doctor, “and a couple of men catch one moment’s glimpse of a boy, in the midst of gunpowder smoke, and in all the distraction of alarm and darkness. Here’s a boy comes to that very same house, next morning, and because he happens to have his arm tied up, these men lay violent hands upon him—by doing which, they place his life in great danger—and swear he is the thief. Now, the question is, whether these men are justified by the fact; if not, in what situation do they place themselves?”
domestication: 医生说道：“这儿房子被闯了，几个男人在火药烟雾中瞥见一个男孩，当时一片混乱，又紧张又黑。第二天早上，这男孩来到同一个房子，因为胳膊吊着绷带，这几个男人就把他抓了起来——这一抓还差点要了他的命——还一口咬定他是贼。现在的问题是，这几个男人这样做有没有事实依据？如果没有，他们自己又会处于什么境地呢？”
foreignization: “这就是被闯入的房子，”医生说，“有几个男人在火药烟雾中，以及惊慌和黑暗的混乱中，瞥见了一个男孩。第二天早上，有个男孩来到这同一所房子，因为他的手臂吊着，这些人就对他大打出手——这样做使他的生命处于极大的危险之中——并发誓他是小偷。现在的问题是，这些人的行为是否得到了事实的支持；如果没有，他们把自己置于什么境地？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Here’s the house broken into,” said the doctor, “and a couple of men catch one moment’s glimpse of a boy, in the midst of gunpowder smoke, and in all the distraction of alarm and darkness. Here’s a boy comes to that very same house, next morning, and because he happens to have his arm tied up, these men lay violent hands upon him—by doing which, they place his life in great danger—and swear he is the thief. Now, the question is, whether these men are justified by the fact; if not, in what situation do they place themselves?”
医生说道：“这儿房子被闯了，几个男人在火药烟雾中瞥见一个男孩，当时一片混乱，又紧张又黑。第二天早上，这男孩来到同一个房子，因为胳膊吊着绷带，这几个男人就把他抓了起来——这一抓还差点要了他的命——还一口咬定他是贼。现在的问题是，这几个男人这样做有没有事实依据？如果没有，他们自己又会处于什么境地呢？”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Here’s the house broken into,” said the doctor, “and a couple of men catch one moment’s glimpse of a boy, in the midst of gunpowder smoke, and in all the distraction of alarm and darkness. Here’s a boy comes to that very same house, next morning, and because he happens to have his arm tied up, these men lay violent hands upon him—by doing which, they place his life in great danger—and swear he is the thief. Now, the question is, whether these men are justified by the fact; if not, in what situation do they place themselves?”
“这就是被闯入的房子，”医生说，“有几个男人在火药烟雾中，以及惊慌和黑暗的混乱中，瞥见了一个男孩。第二天早上，有个男孩来到这同一所房子，因为他的手臂吊着，这些人就对他大打出手——这样做使他的生命处于极大的危险之中——并发誓他是小偷。现在的问题是，这些人的行为是否得到了事实的支持；如果没有，他们把自己置于什么境地？”

source: “There,” replied the woman. And, as if glad to be relieved of it, she hastily threw upon the table a small kid bag scarcely large enough for a French watch, which Monks pouncing upon, tore open with trembling hands. It contained a little gold locket: in which were two locks of hair, and a plain gold wedding-ring.
domestication: “在这儿。”女人说道，好像很高兴摆脱掉这东西似的，急忙把一个小羊皮袋扔到桌上，这袋子几乎只够装一块法国表。蒙克斯一把抢过，颤抖着手撕开袋子。里面有个小小的金吊坠，里面放着两缕头发和一枚素金结婚戒指。

source: A long silence ensued; during which the Jew was plunged in deep thought, with his face wrinkled into an expression of villainy perfectly demoniacal. Sikes eyed him furtively from time to time. Nancy, apparently fearful of irritating the housebreaker, sat with her eyes fixed upon the fire, as if she had been deaf to all that passed.
domestication: 一阵沉默随之而来，这期间犹太人陷入了深思，脸上皱纹堆叠，露出一副恶魔般的恶毒表情。西克斯不时偷偷瞄他几眼。南希似乎害怕激怒这个强盗，只是一动不动地坐在那里，眼睛盯着炉火，好像对周围发生的一切充耳不闻。
foreignization: 长时间的沉默随之而来；其间，那个犹太人陷入深深的思考中，脸上皱成一副极其恶魔般的奸诈表情。西克斯不时偷偷地瞥他一眼。南希似乎害怕激怒那个窃贼，双眼盯着火炉，仿佛对所发生的一切充耳不闻。

A long silence ensued; during which the Jew was plunged in deep thought, with his face wrinkled into an expression of villainy perfectly demoniacal. Sikes eyed him furtively from time to time. source: He had been crouching on the step for some time: wondering at the great number of public-houses (every other house in Barnet was a tavern, large or small), gazing listlessly at the coaches as they passed through, and thinking how strange it seemed that they could do, with ease, in a few hours, what it had taken him a whole week of courage and determination beyond his years to accomplish: when he was roused by observing that a boy, who had passed him carelessly some minutes before, had returned, and was now surveying him most earnestly from the opposite side of the way. He took little heed of this at first; but the boy remained in the same attitude of close observation so long, that Oliver raised his head, and returned his steady look. Upon this, the boy crossed over; and walking close up to Oliver, said,
domestication: 他蹲在台阶上好一阵子了：对巴内特镇上酒馆之多感到惊讶（几乎每两栋房子就有一家大小不一的酒馆），漫不经心地看着马车一辆接一辆地驶过，心里想着这些马车几个小时就能轻松走完他费尽勇气和决心、花了一整周才走完的路程，真是不可思议。正想着，他注意到刚才从他身边漫不经心走过的一个男孩又折回来了，这会儿正站在马路对面盯着他看。起先，他没怎么在意；但那男孩一直这么凝视着他，奥利弗终于抬起头来，也回望过去。这下，男孩走了过来，靠近奥利弗，说道：

source: “By the bye,” said Mr. Bumble, “you don’t know anybody who wants a boy, do you? A porochial ’prentis, who is at present a dead-weight; a millstone, as I may say, round the porochial throat? Liberal terms, Mr. Sowerberry, liberal terms?” As Mr. Bumble spoke, he raised his cane to the bill above him, and gave three distinct raps upon the words “five pounds”: which were printed thereon in Roman capitals of gigantic size.
domestication: 顺带一提，邦布尔先生说：“你不会正好认识哪个想要个男孩的吧？一个教区的学徒，现在可是个大累赘，就像块磨盘挂在教区的脖子上。索尔伯里先生，条件可是很宽松的哦？”说着，邦布尔先生举起手杖敲了敲头上的告示牌上“五英镑”这几个大字，敲了三下。这字是罗马体的大字，看上去很是醒目。
foreignization: “顺便问一下，”邦布尔先生说，“你不知道有谁想要个男孩吧？一个教区的学徒，目前是个累赘；我可以说，是套在教区脖子上的磨盘石？索伯里先生，条件很优厚，条件很优厚？”邦布尔先生说话时，举起手杖对着上面的告示，用三个不同的动作敲了敲“五英镑”这几个字：这几个字是以巨大的罗马大写字母印在上面的。

source: All this time he had, never once, turned his back upon the corpse; no, not for a moment. Such preparations completed, he moved, backward, towards the door: dragging the dog with him, lest he should soil his feet anew and carry out new evidence of the crime into the streets. He shut the door softly, locked it, took the key, and left the house.
domestication: 这段时间里，他连一刻也没背对过那具尸体。准备妥当后，他小心翼翼地倒退着向门口挪去，还牵着狗，生怕再次弄脏了脚，再带上新的罪证到街上。他轻轻关上门，锁好，拿上钥匙，便离开了这座房子。
foreignization: 所有这些时间里，他一次也没有背对着尸体；不，连一刻也没有。做完这些准备后，他向后移动，朝门口走去：拖着那条狗，以免再次弄脏自己的脚，并将新的犯罪证据带到街上。他轻轻地关上门，锁上，拿上钥匙，离开了房子。

source: “I’m doubtful about it,” replied Fagin, with a sigh. “If they don’t get any fresh evidence, it’ll only be a summary conviction, and we shall have him back again after six weeks or so; but, if they do, it’s a case of lagging. They know what a clever lad he is; he’ll be a lifer. They’ll make the Artful nothing less than a lifer.”
domestication: 费金叹了口气，回答说：“我对此表示怀疑。要是他们没有新的证据，就只能判个简易罪名，六周后他又会回来；可要是有新证据，那他就惨了。他们都知道这小子聪明，肯定要把他关一辈子。机灵鬼这次肯定得蹲大牢了。”
foreignization: “我对此表示怀疑，”费金叹了口气回答说。“如果他们没有得到新的证据，那只會是一个简易判决，六周左右我们就会再见到他；但如果有新的证据，那就是判刑的问题了。他们知道他是个聪明的孩子；他会被判终身监禁的。他们会判机灵鬼不少于终身监禁的。”

source: If Noah had been attired in his charity-boy’s dress, there might have been some reason for the Jew opening his eyes so wide; but as he had discarded the coat and badge, and wore a short smock-frock over his leathers, there seemed no particular reason for his appearance exciting so much attention in a public-house.
domestication: 要是诺亚穿着他那件慈善学校的衣服，犹太人瞪大眼睛也就有道理了；但他脱下了那身衣裳和徽章，穿上了短罩衫，罩在皮衣外面，这样的打扮在酒馆里本不应该引起那么多注意。

source: “Then what the devil is to be done?” said the impetuous doctor, when they had rejoined the two ladies. “Are we to pass a vote of thanks to all these vagabonds, male and female, and beg them to accept a hundred pounds, or so, apiece, as a trifling mark of our esteem, and some slight acknowledgment of their kindness to Oliver?”
domestication: “那该怎么办？”性急的医生跟两位女士会合后说道。“难道我们要向这些男女流浪汉道谢，还每人给上一百英镑，表示我们的一点心意，感谢他们对奥利弗的关照不成？”
foreignization: “那么该死的该怎么办？”当他们重新加入两位女士时，性急的医生说道。“我们是要向所有这些男女流浪汉表示感谢，并请求他们每人接受一百英镑左右，作为对我们对他们的好意的轻微承认和对他们对奥利弗的善意的小小报答吗？”

source: The dog no doubt heard; because Mr. Sikes spoke in the very harshest key of a very harsh voice; but, appearing to entertain some unaccountable objection to having his throat cut, he remained where he was, and growled more fiercely than before: at the same time grasping the end of the poker between his teeth, and biting at it like a wild beast.
domestication: 这狗肯定听见了；因为西克斯先生说话时声音极其刺耳，粗暴至极。但狗似乎对被割喉咙这事有些莫名的抗拒，于是它待在原地，比之前更加凶猛地狂吠起来。与此同时，它还用牙齿咬住铁棍的一端，像野兽一样撕咬。
foreignization: 狗无疑听到了；因为西克斯先生是用他那本就粗糙的声音中最刺耳的音调说的；但似乎出于某种不可理解的反对被割喉的想法，它留在原地，比之前更加凶猛地咆哮着：同时用牙齿咬住了拨火棍的一端，像野兽一样啃咬着。

source: “Pooh! pooh! nonsense!” said the gentleman in the white waistcoat. “He’d be cheap with nothing at all, as a premium. Take him, you silly fellow! He’s just the boy for you. He wants the stick, now and then: it’ll do him good; and his board needn’t come very expensive, for he hasn’t been overfed since he was born. Ha! ha! ha!”
domestication: “呸！呸！胡说八道！”穿白背心的先生说道。“他啊，白送给你都划算。你这傻瓜，快收下他吧！他正合适你家。他呀，偶尔打两下还挺有用的，对他有好处；再说他的伙食费也不用太高，因为他从生下来就没怎么吃饱过。哈哈哈！”
foreignization: “呸！呸！胡说八道！”穿白背心的先生说道。“他就算什么保费都没有也便宜。收下他吧，你这个傻瓜！他正适合你。他时不时需要挨顿打：这对他有好处；而且他的食宿费也不用太贵，因为他从出生起就没怎么吃过饱饭。哈哈哈！”

source: Oliver, who watched the old lady anxiously, observed that she was alarmed by these appearances; and so in truth, was he; but seeing that she affected to make light of them, he endeavoured to do the same, and they so far succeeded, that when Rose was persuaded by her aunt to retire for the night, she was in better spirits; and appeared even in better health: assuring them that she felt certain she should rise in the morning, quite well.
domestication: 奥利弗紧张地看着老太太，发现她被这些迹象吓坏了；其实他自己也挺害怕的。不过见老太太装作不当回事，他也努力跟着轻松起来。他们这样做还算成功，当罗丝被姨妈劝说去休息时，她的情绪已经好多了，看起来气色也不错，还保证说第二天早上一定会好起来。
foreignization: 奥利弗焦虑地观察着老夫人，看到她对这些情况感到惊恐；事实上，他自己也是如此；但见她装作不把这些当回事，他也努力这么做，结果他们成功到足以让罗斯在被她姑妈劝说去休息时，精神状态有所好转；甚至看起来更加健康：她向他们保证，她确信自己第二天早上起床时会完全恢复。

source: It was a little lattice window, about five feet and a half above the ground, at the back of the house: which belonged to a scullery, or small brewing-place, at the end of the passage. The aperture was so small, that the inmates had probably not thought it worth while to defend it more securely; but it was large enough to admit a boy of Oliver’s size, nevertheless. A very brief exercise of Mr. Sike’s art, sufficed to overcome the fastening of the lattice; and it soon stood wide open also.
domestication: 那是一扇小格子窗，离地大概有五尺半高，位于房子后面，通向过道尽头的一个洗碗间或是小小的酿酒处。窗户很小，屋里的人大概觉得不值得加固，但对奥利弗这样的小孩来说，却刚刚好能挤进去。西克先生稍一动手，轻而易举就撬开了窗栓，窗户随即大开。
foreignization: 那是一个小格子窗，离地面大约五英尺半，位于房子的后面：属于过道尽头的一个洗碗间或小酿酒处。那个开口如此之小，房子里的人大概认为不值得更安全地防护它；但对奥利弗这样大小的男孩来说，它足够大了。西克先生稍加施展技艺，就足以克服格子窗的锁扣；很快，它也大大地敞开了。

source: “Quick!” she said. “Tell them to fetch a hackney-coach, and be ready to go with me. I will take you there directly, without a minute’s loss of time. I will only tell my aunt that we are going out for an hour, and be ready as soon as you are.”
domestication: “快！”她说，“赶紧叫辆马车来，准备好跟我走。我立刻就带你去，一分钟也不耽搁。我只跟姨妈说我们出去一会儿，你准备好我就出发。”
foreignization: “快！”她说。“告诉他们去叫一辆出租马车，并准备好和我一起走。我会立刻带你去那里，一分钟也不耽搁。我只告诉我姑妈我们出去一个小时，等你准备好我就出发。”

source: Mr. Fang was a lean, long-backed, stiff-necked, middle-sized man, with no great quantity of hair, and what he had, growing on the back and sides of his head. His face was stern, and much flushed. If he were really not in the habit of drinking rather more than was exactly good for him, he might have brought action against his countenance for libel, and have recovered heavy damages.
domestication: 方先生个子不高也不矮，背有点儿长，脖子僵硬，头发稀疏，剩下的几根也集中在后脑勺和耳边。他脸上的表情总是严肃得吓人，脸色还特别红润。要说他平时真的一点儿酒都没多喝，那他的脸可真是委屈了，搞不好还能告它诽谤，索赔一大笔呢。
foreignization: 方先生是一个瘦削、背长、脖子僵硬、中等身材的人，头发不多，而且头发主要长在头的后部和两侧。他的脸色严峻，而且非常红润。如果他真的没有酗酒的习惯，他本可以因诽谤而对他的面容提起诉讼，并获得巨额赔偿。

source: Spring flew swiftly by, and summer came. If the village had been beautiful at first it was now in the full glow and luxuriance of its richness. The great trees, which had looked shrunken and bare in the earlier months, had now burst into strong life and health; and stretching forth their green arms over the thirsty ground, converted open and naked spots into choice nooks, where was a deep and pleasant shade from which to look upon the wide prospect, steeped in sunshine, which lay stretched beyond. The earth had donned her mantle of brightest green; and shed her richest perfumes abroad. It was the prime and vigour of the year; all things were glad and flourishing.
domestication: 春天匆匆而过，夏天就来了。村子原本就美，如今更是繁花似锦，绿意盎然。那些大树在早春时还显得干枯光秃，现在已焕发出勃勃生机；它们伸出翠绿的枝丫，遮盖着干渴的土地，把曾经的荒凉角落变成了一个个幽静的所在，让人可以在浓荫下悠然地欣赏远处沐浴在阳光中的美景。大地披上了最鲜亮的绿袍，散发着最浓郁的芬芳。这是年华的鼎盛时期，万物欣欣向荣，好不热闹。
foreignization: 春天飞逝而过，夏天来临。如果村子最初就很美丽，现在则处于其丰富性的全部光辉和繁茂之中。那些在早些月份看起来枯萎和光秃的大树，现在已经爆发出强健的生命力；它们伸展着绿色的枝条覆盖在干渴的地面上，将开阔和裸露的地方变成了精选的角落，在那里可以置身于深深的、令人愉悦的树荫下，眺望沐浴在阳光中的广阔景色。大地披上了最鲜亮的绿衣；散发出最浓郁的芬芳。这是年富力强的时节；万物欣欣向荣。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Spring flew swiftly by, and summer came. If the village had been beautiful at first it was now in the full glow and luxuriance of its richness. The great trees, which had looked shrunken and bare in the earlier months, had now burst into strong life and health; and stretching forth their green arms over the thirsty ground, converted open and naked spots into choice nooks, where was a deep and pleasant shade from which to look upon the wide prospect, steeped in sunshine, which lay stretched beyond. The earth had donned her mantle of brightest green; and shed her richest perfumes abroad. It was the prime and vigour of the year; all things were glad and flourishing.
春天匆匆而过，夏天就来了。村子原本就美，如今更是繁花似锦，绿意盎然。那些大树在早春时还显得干枯光秃，现在已焕发出勃勃生机；它们伸出翠绿的枝丫，遮盖着干渴的土地，把曾经的荒凉角落变成了一个个幽静的所在，让人可以在浓荫下悠然地欣赏远处沐浴在阳光中的美景。大地披上了最鲜亮的绿袍，散发着最浓郁的芬芳。这是年华的鼎盛时期，万物欣欣向荣，好不热闹。

source: Through these streets, Noah Claypole walked, dragging Charlotte after him; now stepping into the kennel to embrace at a glance the whole external character of some small public-house; now jogging on again, as some fancied appearance induced him to believe it too public for his purpose. At length, he stopped in front of one, more humble in appearance and more dirty than any he had yet seen; and, having crossed over and surveyed it from the opposite pavement, graciously announced his intention of putting up there, for the night.
domestication: 诺亚·克莱波尔拖着夏洛特穿过了这些街道，一会儿走进小酒馆前，一眼扫过整个外面，看看是不是他要找的地方；一会儿又加快脚步，觉得某处人太多，不太合适。终于，他停在了一个更破旧、更脏的小酒馆前，这地方比之前看过的都要差。他走到对面，仔细打量了一番，然后大方地宣布，就在这儿过夜了。
foreignization: 诺亚·克莱普尔穿过这些街道，拖着夏洛特跟在他后面；时而走进门廊，一瞥之间便把握住某个小酒馆的全部外观特征；时而又继续往前走，因为某种想象中的迹象使他相信那个地方对他来说太过公开了。最终，他在一个外表更为卑微、更加肮脏的小酒馆前停了下来；在过马路到对面的人行道上审视了一番之后，他仁慈地宣布了他打算在那里过夜的意图。

source: The coach rattled away, over nearly the same ground as that which Oliver had traversed when he first entered London in company with the Dodger; and, turning a different way when it reached the Angel at Islington, stopped at length before a neat house, in a quiet shady street near Pentonville. Here, a bed was prepared, without loss of time, in which Mr. Brownlow saw his young charge carefully and comfortably deposited; and here, he was tended with a kindness and solicitude that knew no bounds.
domestication: 马车咯吱作响，几乎沿着奥利弗第一次和杰克一起进伦敦时走过的路行驶。到了伊斯灵顿的天使旅馆时，它转了个弯，最后停在了彭顿维尔附近一条安静树荫道上的一个整洁的房子前。这里立马给奥利弗准备了一张床，布朗洛先生小心翼翼地将他安顿好；在这里，奥利弗受到了无微不至的照顾，真是关怀备至，体贴入微。
foreignization: 马车咯吱作响，几乎沿着奥利弗第一次在机灵鬼的陪伴下进入伦敦时走过的路线行驶；当到达伊斯灵顿的天使旅馆时，马车转向了另一条路，最终停在一栋位于彭顿维尔附近一条安静阴凉街道上的整洁房屋前。这里，立马为奥利弗准备了一张床，布朗洛先生看着他的年轻受托人被仔细而舒适地安置在床上；在这里，他受到了无微不至的关怀与照料。

Here, a bed was prepared, without loss of time, in which Mr. Brownlow saw his young charge carefully and comfortably deposited; and here, he was tended with a kindness and solicitude that knew no bounds.

source: “Your having interfered in this dear boy’s behalf before,” said Rose; “your coming here, at so great a risk, to tell me what you have heard; your manner, which convinces me of the truth of what you say; your evident contrition, and sense of shame; all lead me to believe that you might yet be reclaimed. Oh!” said the earnest girl, folding her hands as the tears coursed down her face, “do not turn a deaf ear to the entreaties of one of your own sex; the first—the first, I do believe, who ever appealed to you in the voice of pity and compassion. Do hear my words, and let me save you yet, for better things.”
domestication: 罗丝说：“你以前就帮过这可怜的孩子；你冒着这么大的风险来告诉我你听到的事；你的态度让我相信你说的是真话；你显然感到后悔，也很羞愧；这一切都让我觉得你还可能改过自新。哎！”这诚挚的女孩说着，双手合十，泪水顺着脸颊流淌，“不要对同性的人的请求充耳不闻吧；我相信，你是第一次听到有人以怜悯和同情的声音向你求助。听我说的话吧，让我还能救你，让你走向更好的未来。”
foreignization: “你以前就为这个可怜的孩子出面干预过，”罗丝说；“你冒着这么大的风险来这里，告诉我你所听到的；你的态度，使我确信你说的是真话；你显然感到懊悔和羞愧；这一切都使我相信，你还有可能被挽救。哦！”这位热心的女孩说着，双手合十，泪水顺着脸颊流下，“不要对你同性的恳求充耳不闻；第一个——第一个，我相信，是第一个以怜悯和同情的声音向你求助的人。听我说的话，让我还能把你拯救，走向更好的生活。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Your having interfered in this dear boy’s behalf before,” said Rose; “your coming here, at so great a risk, to tell me what you have heard; your manner, which convinces me of the truth of what you say; your evident contrition, and sense of shame; all lead me to believe that you might yet be reclaimed. Oh!” said the earnest girl, folding her hands as the tears coursed down her face, “do not turn a deaf ear to the entreaties of one of your own sex; the first—the first, I do believe, who ever appealed to you in the voice of pity and compassion. Do hear my words, and let me save you yet, for better things.”

source: “Yes, I think it rather pretty,” said the beadle, glancing proudly downwards at the large brass buttons which embellished his coat. “The die is the same as the porochial seal—the Good Samaritan healing the sick and bruised man. The board presented it to me on Newyear’s morning, Mr. Sowerberry. I put it on, I remember, for the first time, to attend the inquest on that reduced tradesman, who died in a doorway at midnight.”
domestication: “是啊，我觉得挺好看的。”执事说着，自豪地低头看了看他那身大衣上的大铜纽扣。“印章和教区的印章是一样的——好撒玛利亚人医治病人和受伤的人。索耶先生，董事会是在新年的早晨送给我的。我记得第一次穿它去参加那个破产商人半夜死在门道里的验尸会。”
foreignization: “是的，我觉得它相当漂亮，”执事说，骄傲地向下瞥了一眼装饰在他外套上的大黄铜纽扣。“模具和教区的印章一样——好撒玛利亚人在医治那生病和受伤的人。董事会在新年早晨赠送给我的，索尔伯里先生。我记得，我第一次穿上它是为了参加那个在午夜死在门道里的落魄商人的验尸。”

source: “It is,” replied Rose, “that you must endeavour to forget me; not as your old and dearly-attached companion, for that would wound me deeply; but, as the object of your love. Look into the world; think how many hearts you would be proud to gain, are there. Confide some other passion to me, if you will; I will be the truest, warmest, and most faithful friend you have.”
domestication: 罗斯回答说：“你要努力把我忘掉，不是忘了我这个老朋友，那样会伤透我的心；而是忘了我作为你心上人的模样。你看这世界多大，有多少值得你倾心的人等着你去发现。你要是愿意，可以把你的新感情也告诉我，我会是你最真心、最热情、最忠实的朋友。”
foreignization: “是的，”罗丝回答，“你必须努力忘记我；不是作为你老朋友和亲密伴侣，因为那会深深伤害我；而是作为你爱的对象。看看这个世界；想想有多少颗心你会为之自豪地去赢得。如果你愿意，可以把另一段感情托付给我；我会是你最真挚、最热情、最忠诚的朋友。”

source: The girl had taken a few restless turns to and fro—closely watched meanwhile by her hidden observer—when the heavy bell of St. Paul’s tolled for the death of another day. Midnight had come upon the crowded city. The palace, the night-cellar, the jail, the madhouse: the chambers of birth and death, of health and sickness, the rigid face of the corpse and the calm sleep of the child: midnight was upon them all.
domestication: 女孩在原地焦躁地来回踱步，而她的一举一动都被暗处的观察者密切注视着。圣保罗大教堂的钟声沉重地敲响，宣告着又一天的终结。午夜降临在这座繁华的城市。皇宫、夜店、监狱、疯人院，还有那些诞生和死亡的房间，健康与疾病的病房，僵硬的死人脸庞，还有安睡的孩子，午夜无差别地笼罩着这一切。
foreignization: 女孩已经来回不安地走动了几圈——期间一直被她那藏身的观察者密切注视着——这时圣保罗大教堂沉重的钟声敲响，宣告又一天的结束。午夜降临在这座熙熙攘攘的城市。皇宫、夜间酒馆、监狱、疯人院：诞生和死亡的房间、健康和疾病的房间、尸体僵硬的脸庞和孩子平静的睡眠：午夜笼罩着这一切。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The girl had taken a few restless turns to and fro—closely watched meanwhile by her hidden observer—when the heavy bell of St. Paul’s tolled for the death of another day. Midnight had come upon the crowded city. The palace, the night-cellar, the jail, the madhouse: the chambers of birth and death, of health and sickness, the rigid face of the corpse and the calm sleep of the child: midnight was upon them all.
女孩在原地焦躁地来回踱步，而她的一举一动都被暗处的观察者密切注视着。圣保罗大教堂的钟声沉重地敲响，宣告着又一天的终结。午夜降临在这座繁华的城市。皇宫、夜店、监狱、疯人院，还有那些诞生和死亡的房间，健康与疾病的病房，僵硬的死人脸庞，还有安睡的孩子，午夜无差别地笼罩着这一切。

messages_foreignization.content:

The girl had taken a few restless turns to and fro—closely watched meanwhile by her hidden observer—when the heavy bell of St. Paul’s tolled for the death of another day. Midnight had come upon the crowded city. The palace, the night-cellar, the jail, the madhouse: the chambers of birth and death, of health and sickness, the rigid face of the corpse and the calm sleep of the child: midnight was upon them all.
女孩已经来回不安地走动了几圈——期间一直被她那藏身的观察者密切注视着——这时圣保罗大教堂沉重的钟声敲响，宣告又一天的结束。午夜降临在这座熙熙攘攘的城市。皇宫、夜间酒馆、监狱、疯人院：诞生和死亡的房间、健康和疾病的房间、尸体僵硬的脸庞和孩子平静的睡眠：午夜笼罩着这一切。

source: “Take heed, Oliver! take heed!” said the old man, shaking his right hand before him in a warning manner. “He’s a rough man, and thinks nothing of blood when his own is up. Whatever falls out, say nothing; and do what he bids you. Mind!” Placing a strong emphasis on the last word, he suffered his features gradually to resolve themselves into a ghastly grin, and, nodding his head, left the room.
domestication: 小心点，奥利弗！小心点！老人说，右手在他面前晃动着，做出警告的样子。这人粗鲁得很，一上头了，根本不把血命当回事。不管出什么事，都别说话；他让你干什么就干什么。记住了！说到最后几个字时，他加重了语气，脸上慢慢露出一丝恐怖的笑，点点头，转身离开了房间。
foreignization: “当心，奥利弗！当心！”老人说，同时在他面前用右手做出警告的姿势。“他是个粗暴的人，一旦自己激动起来就毫不在乎流血。不管发生什么，什么都不要说；按他的吩咐去做。记住！”强调了最后一个词后，他让自己的表情逐渐变成了一个可怕的笑容，点了点头，然后离开了房间。

source: Whatever were Mr. Bumble’s intentions, however (and no doubt they were of the best): it unfortunately happened, as has been twice before remarked, that the table was a round one; consequently Mr. Bumble, moving his chair by little and little, soon began to diminish the distance between himself and the matron; and, continuing to travel round the outer edge of the circle, brought his chair, in time, close to that in which the matron was seated.
domestication: 不管邦布尔先生打的是什么主意（想来总是好的），偏偏那张桌子是个圆的，这已经是第三次提到这一点了。所以呢，邦布尔先生一点一点地挪动椅子，不知不觉就离那位女管家越来越近了。他继续沿着圆桌的边缘挪啊挪，最后他的椅子终于挪到了女管家的旁边。
foreignization: 不管邦布尔先生的意图如何（而无疑他的意图是好的）：不幸的是，正如前面两次提到的，这张桌子是圆的；因此，邦布尔先生一点一点地移动他的椅子，很快就开始减少了他与女管理员之间的距离；并且，继续沿着圆的外缘移动，最终将他的椅子移得离女管理员坐的椅子很近。

source: “The mother,” said the woman, making a more violent effort than before; “the mother, when the pains of death first came upon her, whispered in my ear that if her baby was born alive, and thrived, the day might come when it would not feel so much disgraced to hear its poor young mother named. ‘And oh, kind Heaven!’ she said, folding her thin hands together, ‘whether it be boy or girl, raise up some friends for it in this troubled world, and take pity upon a lonely desolate child, abandoned to its mercy!’”
domestication: 那女人更加用力地说：“那母亲，当她临终的痛苦第一次袭来时，她在我耳边轻声说，如果她的孩子能顺利出生，健康成长，总有一天，这孩子听到自己可怜年轻的母亲的名字时，不会感到那么羞耻。‘哦，仁慈的上天！’她合起那双瘦弱的手，祈求道，‘不管这孩子是男是女，求您在这纷扰的世间给他找个靠山，可怜可怜这个孤苦无依的孩子，让他得到一些关怀吧！’”
foreignization: “孩子的母亲，”女人说道，比之前更加用力地挣扎着；“当死亡的痛苦第一次袭来时，她在我耳边低语，如果她的孩子能顺利出生，并且健康成长，总有一天，听到它那可怜年轻的母亲的名字时，它不会感到如此羞愧。‘哦，仁慈的上天！’她合拢瘦弱的双手说道，‘无论是男孩还是女孩，都请在这个纷扰的世界中为他（她）找到一些朋友，并怜悯这个孤独无依的孩子，任由命运摆布吧！’”

source: She had taken up the same pen, and laid it down again fifty times, and had considered and reconsidered the first line of her letter without writing the first word, when Oliver, who had been walking in the streets, with Mr. Giles for a body-guard, entered the room in such breathless haste and violent agitation, as seemed to betoken some new cause of alarm.

source: “Nothing,” replied Mr. Brownlow, “nothing to you. But it was hers, and even at this distance of time brings back to me, an old man, the glow and thrill which I once felt, only to hear it repeated by a stranger. I am very glad you have changed it—very—very.”
domestication: 布朗洛先生回答：“没什么，跟你没关系。但那曾是她的，即使过了这么多年，一听到有人提起，还是能勾起我这老头子当年的感受。你改了它，我很高兴，真的，非常高兴。”
foreignization: “没什么，”布朗洛先生回答，“对你来说没什么。但这是她的，即使时隔多年，仍能让我这个老人回想起曾经的感受，只是听到一个陌生人重复它就足以让我心动。我非常高兴你改了它——非常——非常。”

source: “Ah!” said the old lady, “painters always make ladies out prettier than they are, or they wouldn’t get any custom, child. The man that invented the machine for taking likenesses might have known that would never succeed; it’s a deal too honest. A deal,” said the old lady, laughing very heartily at her own acuteness.
domestication: “哎呀！”老太太大笑道，“画家总是把女士画得比真人漂亮，不然怎么会有生意嘛，孩子。那个发明画像机器的人，应该知道这玩意儿肯定不行；它太老实了，太老实了！”说完，老太婆对自己的机智笑得前仰后合。
foreignization: “啊！”老夫人说，“画家总是把女士画得比实际更漂亮，否则他们就接不到生意了，孩子。发明肖像机的人本该知道这东西永远不会成功；它太诚实了。太诚实了，”老夫人说完，对自己的机智大笑起来。

source: “Always the way!” muttered the Jew to himself as he turned homeward. “The worst of these women is, that a very little thing serves to call up some long-forgotten feeling; and, the best of them is, that it never lasts. Ha! ha! The man against the child, for a bag of gold!”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The girl still held Oliver fast by the hand, and continued to pour into his ear, the warnings and assurances she had already imparted. All was so quick and hurried, that he had scarcely time to recollect where he was, or how he came there, when the carriage stopped at the house to which the Jew’s steps had been directed on the previous evening.
女孩仍然紧紧握着奥利弗的手，并继续向他的耳朵倾诉她已经传达的警告和保证。一切发生得如此迅速和匆忙，以至于马车停在犹太人前一晚所指的那座房子前时，他几乎来不及回想自己身在何处，是如何来到这里的。

source: They were no sooner gone, than Monks, who appeared to entertain an invincible repugnance to being left alone, called to a boy who had been hidden somewhere below. Bidding him go first, and bear the light, he returned to the chamber he had just quitted.
domestication: 他们刚一走，莫克斯就叫来了藏在下面某个地方的一个男孩。他似乎对独处有着无法克服的厌恶。让那男孩先走，拿着灯照亮，他自己则回到刚才离开的那个房间。
foreignization: 他们刚一走，蒙克斯就叫来一个一直藏在下面某处的男孩。他让那个男孩先走，并拿着灯，然后自己回到了刚才离开的那个房间。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

They were no sooner gone, than Monks, who appeared to entertain an invincible repugnance to being left alone, called to a boy who had been hidden somewhere below. Bidding him go first, and bear the light, he returned to the chamber he had just quitted.
他们刚一走，莫克斯就叫来了藏在下面某个地方的一个男孩。他似乎对独处有着无法克服的厌恶。让那男孩先走，拿着灯照亮，他自己则回到刚才离开的那个房间。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

They were no sooner gone, than Monks, who appeared to entertain an invincible repugnance to being left alone, called to a boy who had been hidden somewhere below. Bidding him go first, and bear the light, he returned to the chamber he had just quitted.
他们刚一走，蒙克斯就叫来一个一直藏在下面某处的男孩。他让那个男孩先走，并拿着灯，然后自己回到了刚才离开的那个房间。

source: In pursuance of this request, Nancy quickly laid the cloth; disappearing for a few minutes, she presently returned with a pot of porter and a dish of sheep’s heads: which gave occasion to several pleasant witticisms on the part of Mr. Sikes, founded upon the singular coincidence of “jemmies” being a can name, common to them, and also to an ingenious implement much used in his profession. Indeed, the worthy gentleman, stimulated perhaps by the immediate prospect of being on active service, was in great spirits and good humour; in proof whereof, it may be here remarked, that he humourously drank all the beer at a draught, and did not utter, on a rough calculation, more than four-score oaths during the whole progress of the meal.
domestication: 接到这个请求后，南希麻利地铺好了桌布；她消失了几分钟，很快就端着一壶波特酒和一盘羊头回来了。这情景让赛克斯先生开了不少玩笑，因为他发现“杰米”这个词既是这些羊头的俗称，也是他行当里一种常用工具的名字。这位先生或许是因为马上要出门办事而兴奋，心情特别好，特别高兴。他一口气把酒喝了个精光，还在那儿风趣地说笑。算起来，整个吃饭过程中，他大概只说了八十来句脏话。
foreignization: 按照这个要求，南希迅速铺好了餐布；消失了几分钟，她随即带着一壶波特酒和一盘羊头回来了：这给了西克斯先生一个机会，就“杰米”这个名字在他们之间以及在他的职业中常用的一种巧妙工具之间的奇特巧合，发表了一些愉快的俏皮话。事实上，这位可敬的先生或许是因为马上就要出去活动而受到刺激，表现得非常高兴和友好；为此可以在这里指出，他幽默地一口气喝光了所有的啤酒，并且在整个用餐过程中，粗略计算下来，没有说出超过八十次咒骂。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In pursuance of this request, Nancy quickly laid the cloth; disappearing for a few minutes, she presently returned with a pot of porter and a dish of sheep’s heads: which gave occasion to several pleasant witticisms on the part of Mr. Sikes, founded upon the singular coincidence of “jemmies” being a can name, common to them, and also to an ingenious implement much used in his profession. Indeed, the worthy gentleman, stimulated perhaps by the immediate prospect of being on active service, was in great spirits and good humour; in proof whereof, it may be here remarked, that he humourously drank all the beer at a draught, and did not utter, on a rough calculation, more than four-score oaths during the whole progress of the meal.
接到这个请求后，南希麻利地铺好了桌布；她消失了几分钟，很快就端着一壶波特酒和一盘羊头回来了。这情景让赛克斯先生开了不少玩笑，因为他发现“杰米”这个词既是这些羊头的俗称，也是他行当里一种常用工具的名字。这位先生或许是因为马上要出门办事而兴奋，心情特别好，特别高兴。他一口气把酒喝了个精光，还在那儿风趣地说笑。算起来，整个吃饭过程中，他大概只说了八十来句脏话。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In pursuance of this request, Nancy quickly laid the cloth; disappearing for a few minutes, she presently returned with a pot of porter and a dish of sheep’s heads: which gave occasion to several pleasant witticisms on the part of Mr. Sikes, founded upon the singular coincidence of “jemmies” being a can name, common to them, and also to an ingenious implement much used in his profession. Indeed, the worthy gentleman, stimulated perhaps by the immediate prospect of being on active service, was in great spirits and good humour; in proof whereof, it may be here remarked, that he humourously drank all the beer at a draught, and did not utter, on a rough calculation, more than four-score oaths during the whole progress of the meal.

按照这个要求，南希迅速铺好了餐布；消失了几分钟，她随即带着一壶波特酒和一盘羊头回来了：这给了西克斯先生一个机会，就“杰米”这个名字在他们之间以及在他的职业中常用的一种巧妙工具之间的奇特巧合，发表了一些愉快的俏皮话。事实上，这位可敬的先生或许是因为马上就要出去活动而受到刺激，表现得非常高兴和友好；为此可以在这里指出，他幽默地一口气喝光了所有的啤酒，并且在整个用餐过程中，粗略计算下来，没有说出超过八十次咒骂。

source: The search was all in vain. There were not even the traces of recent footsteps, to be seen. They stood now, on the summit of a little hill, commanding the open fields in every direction for three or four miles. There was the village in the hollow on the left; but, in order to gain that, after pursuing the track Oliver had pointed out, the men must have made a circuit of open ground, which it was impossible they could have accomplished in so short a time. A thick wood skirted the meadow-land in another direction; but they could not have gained that covert for the same reason.
domestication: 搜了个遍，连个脚印都没找着。他们现在站在一个小山顶上，四周三里地的田野尽收眼底。左边低洼处就是村子；但是要想从奥利弗指的方向过去，那些人必须绕过一大片开阔地，这么短的时间根本不可能。另一边是一片茂密的树林，但同样因为时间来不及，他们也不可能躲进去。
foreignization: 搜索全都徒劳无功。甚至没有最近的脚印痕迹可见。他们现在站在一个小山丘的山顶上，可以俯瞰四面三到四英里的开阔田野。左边的山谷里有那个村庄；但是，要想到达那里，在追踪奥利弗指出的路线之后，那些人必须绕过一片开阔地，这在这么短的时间内是不可能完成的。另一个方向有一片茂密的森林环绕着草地；但由于同样的原因，他们也不可能到达那个隐蔽处。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The search was all in vain. There were not even the traces of recent footsteps, to be seen. They stood now, on the summit of a little hill, commanding the open fields in every direction for three or four miles. There was the village in the hollow on the left; but, in order to gain that, after pursuing the track Oliver had pointed out, the men must have made a circuit of open ground, which it was impossible they could have accomplished in so short a time. A thick wood skirted the meadow-land in another direction; but they could not have gained that covert for the same reason.
搜了个遍，连个脚印都没找着。他们现在站在一个小山顶上，四周三里地的田野尽收眼底。左边低洼处就是村子；但是要想从奥利弗指的方向过去，那些人必须绕过一大片开阔地，这么短的时间根本不可能。另一边是一片茂密的树林，但同样因为时间来不及，他们也不可能躲进去。

The search was all in vain. There were not even the traces of recent footsteps, to be seen. They stood now, on the summit of a little hill, commanding the open fields in every direction for three or four miles. There was the village in the hollow on the left; but, in order to gain that, after pursuing the track Oliver had pointed out, the men must have made a circuit of open ground, which it was impossible they could have accomplished in so short a time. A thick wood skirted the meadow-land in another direction; but they could not have gained that covert for the same reason.
搜索全都徒劳无功。甚至没有最近的脚印痕迹可见。他们现在站在一个小山丘的山顶上，可以俯瞰四面三到四英里的开阔田野。左边的山谷里有那个村庄；但是，要想到达那里，在追踪奥利弗指出的路线之后，那些人必须绕过一片开阔地，这在这么短的时间内是不可能完成的。另一个方向有一片茂密的森林环绕着草地；但由于同样的原因，他们也不可能到达那个隐蔽处。

source: Oliver had been dozing; but looked worse, and was more feverish than he had appeared yet. Being assisted by the doctor, he managed to sit up in bed for a minute or so; and looked at the strangers without at all understanding what was going forward—in fact, without seeming to recollect where he was, or what had been passing.
domestication: 奥利弗一直在打盹；但看起来更糟糕了，也比之前更发烧。在医生的帮助下，他勉强坐起身来，撑了一两分钟；看着这些陌生人，却完全不知道发生了什么——实际上，他似乎连自己身在何处、之前发生了什么都记不起来了。
foreignization: 奥利弗一直在打盹；但看起来更糟，比之前显得更加发烧。在医生的帮助下，他勉强在床上坐了起来，持续了一两分钟；并且看着陌生人，完全不明白发生了什么——事实上，似乎不记得自己在哪里，或者发生了什么事。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver had been dozing; but looked worse, and was more feverish than he had appeared yet. Being assisted by the doctor, he managed to sit up in bed for a minute or so; and looked at the strangers without at all understanding what was going forward—in fact, without seeming to recollect where he was, or what had been passing.
奥利弗一直在打盹；但看起来更糟糕了，也比之前更发烧。在医生的帮助下，他勉强坐起身来，撑了一两分钟；看着这些陌生人，却完全不知道发生了什么——实际上，他似乎连自己身在何处、之前发生了什么都记不起来了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver had been dozing; but looked worse, and was more feverish than he had appeared yet. Being assisted by the doctor, he managed to sit up in bed for a minute or so; and looked at the strangers without at all understanding what was going forward—in fact, without seeming to recollect where he was, or what had been passing.
奥利弗一直在打盹；但看起来更糟，比之前显得更加发烧。在医生的帮助下，他勉强在床上坐了起来，持续了一两分钟；并且看着陌生人，完全不明白发生了什么——事实上，似乎不记得自己在哪里，或者发生了什么事。

source: Sikes had disappeared for an instant; but he was up again, and had him by the collar before the smoke had cleared away. He fired his own pistol after the men, who were already retreating; and dragged the boy up.
domestication: 西克斯一闪身不见了，可烟雾还没散尽，他就又跳了出来，一把抓住了那孩子的衣领。他朝那些正往后撤的人放了一枪，然后把孩子拖了起来。
foreignization: 西克斯消失了一瞬间；但烟雾还未散尽，他就再次站了起来，并抓住了那孩子的衣领。他朝已经撤退的那些人开了一枪；然后把男孩拉了起来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Sikes had disappeared for an instant; but he was up again, and had him by the collar before the smoke had cleared away. He fired his own pistol after the men, who were already retreating; and dragged the boy up.
西克斯一闪身不见了，可烟雾还没散尽，他就又跳了出来，一把抓住了那孩子的衣领。他朝那些正往后撤的人放了一枪，然后把孩子拖了起来。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Sikes had disappeared for an instant; but he was up again, and had him by the collar before the smoke had cleared away. He fired his own pistol after the men, who were already retreating; and dragged the boy up.
西克斯消失了一瞬间；但烟雾还未散尽，他就再次站了起来，并抓住了那孩子的衣领。他朝已经撤退的那些人开了一枪；然后把男孩拉了起来。

source: At six o’clock next morning, Mr. Bumble: having exchanged his cocked hat for a round one, and encased his person in a blue great-coat with a cape to it: took his place on the outside of the coach, accompanied by the criminals whose settlement was disputed; with whom, in due course of time, he arrived in London.
domestication: 次日清晨六点，邦布尔先生换下了他的三角帽，戴上了圆帽，又披上了一件带风帽的蓝色大衣，然后坐到了马车外，陪同那些身份有争议的罪犯一起出发，最终顺利抵达了伦敦。
foreignization: 第二天早晨六点钟，邦布尔先生：换下了他的三角帽，戴上了圆帽，并且穿上了带风帽的蓝色大衣：坐到了马车外侧的位置上，陪伴着那些居留地有争议的罪犯们；随着时间的推移，他和他们一起到达了伦敦。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

At six o’clock next morning, Mr. Bumble: having exchanged his cocked hat for a round one, and encased his person in a blue great-coat with a cape to it: took his place on the outside of the coach, accompanied by the criminals whose settlement was disputed; with whom, in due course of time, he arrived in London.
次日清晨六点，邦布尔先生换下了他的三角帽，戴上了圆帽，又披上了一件带风帽的蓝色大衣，然后坐到了马车外，陪同那些身份有争议的罪犯一起出发，最终顺利抵达了伦敦。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

At six o’clock next morning, Mr. Bumble: having exchanged his cocked hat for a round one, and encased his person in a blue great-coat with a cape to it: took his place on the outside of the coach, accompanied by the criminals whose settlement was disputed; with whom, in due course of time, he arrived in London.
第二天早晨六点钟，邦布尔先生：换下了他的三角帽，戴上了圆帽，并且穿上了带风帽的蓝色大衣：坐到了马车外侧的位置上，陪伴着那些居留地有争议的罪犯们；随着时间的推移，他和他们一起到达了伦敦。

source: Here the position of affairs had not at all improved. Sowerberry had not yet returned, and Oliver continued to kick, with undiminished vigour, at the cellar-door. The accounts of his ferocity as related by Mrs. Sowerberry and Charlotte, were of so startling a nature, that Mr. Bumble judged it prudent to parley, before opening the door. With this view he gave a kick at the outside, by way of prelude; and, then, applying his mouth to the keyhole, said, in a deep and impressive tone:
domestication: 这里的情况一点也没好转。索尔伯里先生还没回来，奥利弗还在使劲地踢着地下室的门。索尔伯里太太和夏洛特描述的他的凶猛程度让人胆战心惊，邦布尔先生觉得还是先谈谈再说，免得开门就出事。于是，他先在门上踹了一脚，算是个开场白，然后把嘴凑到钥匙孔前，用低沉而严肃的语气说：
foreignization: 这里的情况丝毫没有改善。索尔伯里先生还没有回来，奥利弗继续以不减的活力踢着地窖的门。索尔伯里太太和夏洛特讲述的关于他的凶猛程度是如此令人震惊，以至于班布尔先生认为在开门之前先进行谈判是明智的。为此，他先在外面踢了一脚，作为前奏；然后，将嘴对着钥匙孔，以一种深沉而庄重的语气说：

Here the position of affairs had not at all improved. Sowerberry had not yet returned, and Oliver continued to kick, with undiminished vigour, at the cellar-door. The accounts of his ferocity as related by Mrs. Sowerberry and Charlotte, were of so startling a nature, that Mr. Bumble judged it prudent to parley, before opening the door. With this view he gave a kick at the outside, by way of prelude; and, then, applying his mouth to the keyhole, said, in a deep and impressive tone:

The accounts of his ferocity as related by Mrs. Sowerberry and Charlotte, were of so startling a nature, that Mr. Bumble judged it prudent to parley, before opening the door. With this view he gave a kick at the outside, by way of prelude; and, then, applying his mouth to the keyhole, said, in a deep and impressive tone:

source: “What is it?” pursued Fagin, mad with rage. “When the boy’s worth hundreds of pounds to me, am I to lose what chance threw me in the way of getting safely, through the whims of a drunken gang that I could whistle away the lives of! And me bound, too, to a born devil that only wants the will, and has the power to, to—”
domestication: “到底是什么？”费金气得发疯，紧追不舍。“这小子值几百镑呢，难道我就因为一帮醉鬼的胡闹，就丢掉这个好不容易才安全到手的机会！我还能吹个口哨就要了他们的命呢！可我还得绑着自己去伺候一个天生的恶魔，他只要想干，就有本事——”
foreignization: “是什么？”费金愤怒地追问道。“当这男孩对我来说值几百英镑时，我难道要因为一群醉鬼的任性而失去这个偶然让我安全得到的机会！而且我还能吹个口哨就让他们送命！而我却还被绑在一个天生的魔鬼身上，他只缺意愿，而且有能力，有能力——”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“What is it?” pursued Fagin, mad with rage. “When the boy’s worth hundreds of pounds to me, am I to lose what chance threw me in the way of getting safely, through the whims of a drunken gang that I could whistle away the lives of! And me bound, too, to a born devil that only wants the will, and has the power to, to—”
“到底是什么？”费金气得发疯，紧追不舍。“这小子值几百镑呢，难道我就因为一帮醉鬼的胡闹，就丢掉这个好不容易才安全到手的机会！我还能吹个口哨就要了他们的命呢！可我还得绑着自己去伺候一个天生的恶魔，他只要想干，就有本事——”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“What is it?” pursued Fagin, mad with rage. “When the boy’s worth hundreds of pounds to me, am I to lose what chance threw me in the way of getting safely, through the whims of a drunken gang that I could whistle away the lives of! And me bound, too, to a born devil that only wants the will, and has the power to, to—”
“是什么？”费金愤怒地追问道。“当这男孩对我来说值几百英镑时，我难道要因为一群醉鬼的任性而失去这个偶然让我安全得到的机会！而且我还能吹个口哨就让他们送命！而我却还被绑在一个天生的魔鬼身上，他只缺意愿，而且有能力，有能力——”

source: “Silence!” cried Mr. Bumble, sternly. “Take yourself downstairs, ma’am. Noah, you shut up the shop; say another word till your master comes home, at your peril; and, when he does come home, tell him that Mr. Bumble said he was to send a old woman’s shell after breakfast tomorrow morning. Do you hear sir? Kissing!” cried Mr. Bumble, holding up his hands. “The sin and wickedness of the lower orders in this porochial district is frightful! If Parliament don’t take their abominable courses under consideration, this country’s ruined, and the character of the peasantry gone for ever!” With these words, the beadle strode, with a lofty and gloomy air, from the undertaker’s premises.
domestication: “安静！”班布尔先生厉声喝道，“夫人，您自己下楼去吧。诺亚，你关上店门；等你主人回来前，要是多说一个字，小心着！等他回来了，告诉他班布尔先生说明天早饭后派个老太太来一趟。听清楚了吗，小子？亲吻！”班布尔先生举手叫道，“这个教区的下层人真是罪孽深重啊！这罪过太可怕了！要是议会不尽快管管他们的恶劣行径，咱们国家就完蛋了，农民的好名声也要断送了！”说完这话，班布尔先生摆出一副高傲又阴沉的样子，大步走出了殡仪馆。
foreignization: “安静！”邦布尔先生严厉地喊道。“太太，你自己下楼去。诺亚，你关上店铺；在你主人回家之前，再敢说一个字，当心着；等他回来的时候，告诉他邦布尔先生说让他明天早饭后送个老太婆的壳来。听到了吗，先生？亲吻！”邦布尔先生举起双手喊道。“这个教区低层民众的罪恶和不道德真是可怕！如果议会不把他们的可恶行为考虑进去，这个国家就完蛋了，农民的品格也会永远丧失！”说完这番话，这位执事以一种高傲而阴郁的神情大步走出了殡仪馆。

source: In a dreadful state of doubt and uncertainty, the agonised young woman staggered to the gate, and then, exchanging her faltering walk for a swift run, returned by the most devious and complicated route she could think of, to the domicile of the Jew.

source: Brittles replying in the affirmative, and pointing out the building, the portly man stepped back to the garden-gate, and helped his companion to put up the gig: while Brittles lighted them, in a state of great admiration. This done, they returned to the house, and, being shown into a parlour, took off their great-coats and hats, and showed like what they were.
domestication: 布里特尔斯应声答道，并指了指那栋楼。那位胖先生便退回到花园门口，帮着同伴卸下了马车，布里特尔斯在一旁看得目瞪口呆，帮着打灯。弄好后，两人回到屋里，被领进了一间客厅，脱下大衣和帽子，露出了本来面目。
foreignization: 布里特尔斯点头同意，并指出了那座建筑物，那个胖男人便退回到花园门口，帮助他的同伴把双轮马车收起来：而布里特尔斯则在一旁，怀着极大的钦佩之情为他们照明。做完这些后，他们回到了房子里，被领进了一间客厅，脱下了大衣和帽子，露出了他们的真面目。

source: The noise subsided, and he was asked if he had anything to say why sentence of death should not be passed upon him. He had resumed his listening attitude, and looked intently at his questioner while the demand was made; but it was twice repeated before he seemed to hear it, and then he only muttered that he was an old man—an old man—and so, dropping into a whisper, was silent again.
domestication: 嘈杂声渐渐平息，有人问他是否有什么话要说，以避免被判处死刑。他重新摆出了倾听的姿势，当问话的人提出问题时，他定定地看着对方；但问了两遍，他才像是听到了，只是嘟囔着自己是个老头子——一个老头子——说完这话，声音越来越低，最终又沉默了。
foreignization: 嘈杂声平息下来，他被问到是否有话要说，以解释为什么不应该对他宣判死刑。他已经恢复了倾听的姿势，并在提问时凝视着提问者；但这个问题被重复了两次他才似乎听见，然后他只是咕哝说自己是个老人——一个老人——于是，声音低落到耳语，再次沉默下来。

source: It was no very difficult task, for the grave was so full, that the uppermost coffin was within a few feet of the surface. The grave-digger shovelled in the earth; stamped it loosely down with his feet: shouldered his spade; and walked off, followed by the boys, who murmured very loud complaints at the fun being over so soon.
domestication: 这活儿可不难，因为墓穴里的棺材已经堆得快顶到地面了。掘墓人铲土填进去，用脚随便踩了几下，扛起铁锹就走了。孩子们跟在他后面，叽叽喳喳地抱怨，说这么快就没了乐子。
foreignization: 这并不是一项非常困难的任务，因为墓穴已经如此满了，最上面的棺材距离地面只有几英尺。掘墓人把土铲了进去；用脚松散地踩了踩：扛起铁锹；然后走了，身后跟着的男孩们大声抱怨说好玩的事情这么快就结束了。

source: “No,” said Harry; “to hear you repeat it, if you will—finally repeat it! I will lay at your feet, whatever of station of fortune I may possess; and if you still adhere to your present resolution, will not seek, by word or act, to change it.”
domestication: 哈利说道：“不，如果你愿意，就请你再说一遍——最后再说一遍！我会把我所有的一切，包括我的地位和财富，都放在你的脚下；如果你依然坚持现在的决定，我绝不会再用言语或行动去改变它。”

domestication: “听见了！”赛克斯转过椅子对着她说，“听见了！再让我听你嚷嚷半分钟，我就让那条狗咬住你的喉咙，非得撕下点儿肉来不可。你这是吃错药了？到底怎么回事？”

source: As John Dawkins objected to their entering London before nightfall, it was nearly eleven o’clock when they reached the turnpike at Islington. They crossed from the Angel into St. John’s Road; struck down the small street which terminates at Sadler’s Wells Theatre; through Exmouth Street and Coppice Row; down the little court by the side of the workhouse; across the classic ground which once bore the name of Hockley-in-the-Hole; thence into Little Saffron Hill; and so into Saffron Hill the Great: along which the Dodger scudded at a rapid pace, directing Oliver to follow close at his heels.
domestication: 约翰·道金斯反对天黑前进伦敦，他们差不多到晚上十一点才抵达伊斯灵顿的收费亭。他们从天使客栈穿过圣约翰路，拐进通向萨德勒威尔斯剧院的小街；穿过埃克姆街和科皮斯巷；沿着济贫院旁边的小巷往下走；跨过曾经叫做霍克利-因-特-霍尔的经典地带；然后进入小藏红花山；再往大藏红花山而去。机灵鬼一路飞奔，让奥利弗紧跟着他的脚步。
foreignization: 由于约翰·道金斯反对他们在夜幕降临前进入伦敦，他们直到将近十一点钟才到达伊斯灵顿的收费关卡。他们从天使客栈穿过到圣约翰路；转入通往萨德勒威尔斯剧院的小街；穿过埃克塞特街和科普斯街；沿着工作所旁边的小巷下去；跨过曾经名为霍克利-因-德-霍尔的经典场地；然后进入小撒弗隆山；再进入大撒弗隆山：在这条街上，机灵鬼以快速的步伐疾走，指示奥利弗紧跟着他的脚跟。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As John Dawkins objected to their entering London before nightfall, it was nearly eleven o’clock when they reached the turnpike at Islington. They crossed from the Angel into St. John’s Road; struck down the small street which terminates at Sadler’s Wells Theatre; through Exmouth Street and Coppice Row; down the little court by the side of the workhouse; across the classic ground which once bore the name of Hockley-in-the-Hole; thence into Little Saffron Hill; and so into Saffron Hill the Great: along which the Dodger scudded at a rapid pace, directing Oliver to follow close at his heels.

As John Dawkins objected to their entering London before nightfall, it was nearly eleven o’clock when they reached the turnpike at Islington. They crossed from the Angel into St. John’s Road; struck down the small street which terminates at Sadler’s Wells Theatre; through Exmouth Street and Coppice Row; down the little court by the side of the workhouse; across the classic ground which once bore the name of Hockley-in-the-Hole; thence into Little Saffron Hill; and so into Saffron Hill the Great: along which the Dodger scudded at a rapid pace, directing Oliver to follow close at his heels.
由于约翰·道金斯反对他们在夜幕降临前进入伦敦，他们直到将近十一点钟才到达伊斯灵顿的收费关卡。他们从天使客栈穿过到圣约翰路；转入通往萨德勒威尔斯剧院的小街；穿过埃克塞特街和科普斯街；沿着工作所旁边的小巷下去；跨过曾经名为霍克利-因-德-霍尔的经典场地；然后进入小撒弗隆山；再进入大撒弗隆山：在这条街上，机灵鬼以快速的步伐疾走，指示奥利弗紧跟着他的脚跟。

source: And now that we have accompanied him so far on his road home, and have made all necessary preparations for the old woman’s funeral, let us set on foot a few inquires after young Oliver Twist, and ascertain whether he be still lying in the ditch where Toby Crackit left him.
domestication: 既然我们已经陪着他走了这么远的回家路，又为老太太的丧事做了所有必要的准备，现在就让我们去打听打听小奥利弗·特威斯特的消息，看看他是否还躺在托比·克拉基特留下的那个沟里。
foreignization: 现在我们已经陪他走了一段回家的路，并为老太太的葬礼做了所有必要的准备，让我们开始对奥利弗·特威斯特进行一些调查，查明他是否仍然躺在托比·克拉基特留下他的那个沟里。

现在我们已经陪他走了一段回家的路，并为老太太的葬礼做了所有必要的准备，让我们开始对奥利弗·特威斯特进行一些调查，查明他是否仍然躺在托比·克拉基特留下他的那个沟里。

source: This was indeed the case; but as it was not Mr. Claypole’s habit to repose a blind and foolish confidence in anybody, it should be observed, in justice to that gentleman, that he had trusted Charlotte to this extent, in order that, if they were pursued, the money might be found on her: which would leave him an opportunity of asserting his innocence of any theft, and would greatly facilitate his chances of escape. Of course, he entered at this juncture, into no explanation of his motives, and they walked on very lovingly together.
domestication: 确实如此；但克莱波尔先生可不是个轻易就对谁盲目信任的人。所以，为了给他自己留条后路，他才把钱交给夏洛特，万一被人追查起来，钱在她身上，他就可以说自己没偷，也能更容易脱身。当然了，这种心思他是不会在这时候说出来的，于是两人就亲亲热热地继续往前走。
foreignization: 这确实是这样；但因为克雷波尔先生的习惯并不是盲目和愚蠢地信任任何人，所以为了公正起见，应该指出，他把钱交给夏洛特到这种程度，是为了如果他们被追捕，钱可以在她身上找到：这将使他有机会声称自己对任何盗窃行为无辜，并且大大增加他逃脱的机会。当然，他在这一时刻并没有解释他的动机，他们非常恩爱地继续走着。

Of course, he entered at this juncture, into no explanation of his motives, and they walked on very lovingly together.
确实如此；但克莱波尔先生可不是个轻易就对谁盲目信任的人。所以，为了给他自己留条后路，他才把钱交给夏洛特，万一被人追查起来，钱在她身上，他就可以说自己没偷，也能更容易脱身。当然了，这种心思他是不会在这时候说出来的，于是两人就亲亲热热地继续往前走。

This was indeed the case; but as it was not Mr. Claypole’s habit to repose a blind and foolish confidence in anybody, it should be observed, in justice to that gentleman, that he had trusted Charlotte to this extent, in order that, if they were pursued, the money might be found on her: which would leave him an opportunity of asserting his innocence of any theft, and would greatly facilitate his chances of escape. Of course, he entered at this juncture, into no explanation of his motives, and they walked on very lovingly together.
这确实是这样；但因为克雷波尔先生的习惯并不是盲目和愚蠢地信任任何人，所以为了公正起见，应该指出，他把钱交给夏洛特到这种程度，是为了如果他们被追捕，钱可以在她身上找到：这将使他有机会声称自己对任何盗窃行为无辜，并且大大增加他逃脱的机会。当然，他在这一时刻并没有解释他的动机，他们非常恩爱地继续走着。

source: This was the handsome offer with which Mr. Grimwig backed and confirmed nearly every assertion he made; and it was the more singular in his case, because, even admitting for the sake of argument, the possibility of scientific improvements being brought to that pass which will enable a gentleman to eat his own head in the event of his being so disposed, Mr. Grimwig’s head was such a particularly large one, that the most sanguine man alive could hardly entertain a hope of being able to get through it at a sitting—to put entirely out of the question, a very thick coating of powder.
domestication: grimwig先生几乎每做一个断言都要用这个豪爽的提议来支持和确认；更奇怪的是，在他的情况下，即便为了辩论而假设科学进步到能让一位绅士在他愿意时吃掉自己的脑袋，grimwig先生的脑袋如此之大，就连最乐观的人也难以想象能一口气把它吃完，更不用说还有一层厚厚的粉末了。
foreignization: 这是格里姆威格先生对他几乎每一个论断的背书和支持；在他身上更为奇特的是，即使为了争论的缘故承认科学进步有可能达到这样的程度，即如果某人有这样的意愿，他可以吃掉自己的头，格里姆威格先生的头是如此特别的大，以至于最乐观的人也很难指望能在一顿饭的时间里把它吃完——更不用说还有一层厚厚的粉末。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

This was the handsome offer with which Mr. Grimwig backed and confirmed nearly every assertion he made; and it was the more singular in his case, because, even admitting for the sake of argument, the possibility of scientific improvements being brought to that pass which will enable a gentleman to eat his own head in the event of his being so disposed, Mr. Grimwig’s head was such a particularly large one, that the most sanguine man alive could hardly entertain a hope of being able to get through it at a sitting—to put entirely out of the question, a very thick coating of powder.

This was the handsome offer with which Mr. Grimwig backed and confirmed nearly every assertion he made; and it was the more singular in his case, because, even admitting for the sake of argument, the possibility of scientific improvements being brought to that pass which will enable a gentleman to eat his own head in the event of his being so disposed, Mr. Grimwig’s head was such a particularly large one, that the most sanguine man alive could hardly entertain a hope of being able to get through it at a sitting—to put entirely out of the question, a very thick coating of powder.
这是格里姆威格先生对他几乎每一个论断的背书和支持；在他身上更为奇特的是，即使为了争论的缘故承认科学进步有可能达到这样的程度，即如果某人有这样的意愿，他可以吃掉自己的头，格里姆威格先生的头是如此特别的大，以至于最乐观的人也很难指望能在一顿饭的时间里把它吃完——更不用说还有一层厚厚的粉末。

source: Now, the fact was, that in the inmost recesses of his own heart, Mr. Grimwig was strongly disposed to admit that Oliver’s appearance and manner were unusually prepossessing; but he had a strong appetite for contradiction, sharpened on this occasion by the finding of the orange-peel; and, inwardly determining that no man should dictate to him whether a boy was well-looking or not, he had resolved, from the first, to oppose his friend. When Mr. Brownlow admitted that on no one point of inquiry could he yet return a satisfactory answer; and that he had postponed any investigation into Oliver’s previous history until he thought the boy was strong enough to hear it; Mr. Grimwig chuckled maliciously. And he demanded, with a sneer, whether the housekeeper was in the habit of counting the plate at night; because if she didn’t find a table-spoon or two missing some sunshiny morning, why, he would be content to—and so forth.
domestication: 说来也怪，其实格里姆威格先生心里头，对奥利弗的长相和举止还是挺认可的，觉得这孩子确实讨人喜欢；但他就是爱唱反调，再加上地上捡到了橘子皮，这火气就更大了。他心里打定了主意，谁也不能左右他，说什么这孩子长得多好看，他偏不干。布朗洛先生坦白说，到现在为止，他对奥利弗过去的事一无所知，还特意等孩子身体好些了再细问。听到这儿，格里姆威格冷笑了几声。他还阴阳怪气地问，管家晚上数不数银器，要是哪天大清早发现少了几把勺子，那他可就心甘情愿地——后面的话就不提了。
foreignization: 现在，事实上，在格林威格先生自己内心深处，他非常倾向于承认奥利弗的外貌和举止非同寻常地讨人喜欢；但他有一种强烈的反驳欲望，这次因找到了橙皮而变得更加敏锐；并且，他内心决定不让任何人告诉他一个男孩是否英俊，因此从一开始他就决定要反对他的朋友。当布朗洛先生承认在调查的每一个问题上他都无法给出令人满意的答案，并且他认为奥利弗的身体已经足够强壮可以听这些事情之前，他推迟了对奥利弗过去历史的任何调查时，格林威格先生恶意地窃笑。他带着冷笑问道，管家是否习惯于在晚上数银器；因为如果她在某个阳光明媚的早晨没有发现少了一两个汤匙的话，那么，他就会心满意足地——如此这般。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Now, the fact was, that in the inmost recesses of his own heart, Mr. Grimwig was strongly disposed to admit that Oliver’s appearance and manner were unusually prepossessing; but he had a strong appetite for contradiction, sharpened on this occasion by the finding of the orange-peel; and, inwardly determining that no man should dictate to him whether a boy was well-looking or not, he had resolved, from the first, to oppose his friend. When Mr. Brownlow admitted that on no one point of inquiry could he yet return a satisfactory answer; and that he had postponed any investigation into Oliver’s previous history until he thought the boy was strong enough to hear it; Mr. Grimwig chuckled maliciously. And he demanded, with a sneer, whether the housekeeper was in the habit of counting the plate at night; because if she didn’t find a table-spoon or two missing some sunshiny morning, why, he would be content to—and so forth.

说来也怪，其实格里姆威格先生心里头，对奥利弗的长相和举止还是挺认可的，觉得这孩子确实讨人喜欢；但他就是爱唱反调，再加上地上捡到了橘子皮，这火气就更大了。他心里打定了主意，谁也不能左右他，说什么这孩子长得多好看，他偏不干。布朗洛先生坦白说，到现在为止，他对奥利弗过去的事一无所知，还特意等孩子身体好些了再细问。听到这儿，格里姆威格冷笑了几声。他还阴阳怪气地问，管家晚上数不数银器，要是哪天大清早发现少了几把勺子，那他可就心甘情愿地——后面的话就不提了。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Now, the fact was, that in the inmost recesses of his own heart, Mr. Grimwig was strongly disposed to admit that Oliver’s appearance and manner were unusually prepossessing; but he had a strong appetite for contradiction, sharpened on this occasion by the finding of the orange-peel; and, inwardly determining that no man should dictate to him whether a boy was well-looking or not, he had resolved, from the first, to oppose his friend. When Mr. Brownlow admitted that on no one point of inquiry could he yet return a satisfactory answer; and that he had postponed any investigation into Oliver’s previous history until he thought the boy was strong enough to hear it; Mr. Grimwig chuckled maliciously. And he demanded, with a sneer, whether the housekeeper was in the habit of counting the plate at night; because if she didn’t find a table-spoon or two missing some sunshiny morning, why, he would be content to—and so forth.

现在，事实上，在格林威格先生自己内心深处，他非常倾向于承认奥利弗的外貌和举止非同寻常地讨人喜欢；但他有一种强烈的反驳欲望，这次因找到了橙皮而变得更加敏锐；并且，他内心决定不让任何人告诉他一个男孩是否英俊，因此从一开始他就决定要反对他的朋友。当布朗洛先生承认在调查的每一个问题上他都无法给出令人满意的答案，并且他认为奥利弗的身体已经足够强壮可以听这些事情之前，他推迟了对奥利弗过去历史的任何调查时，格林威格先生恶意地窃笑。他带着冷笑问道，管家是否习惯于在晚上数银器；因为如果她在某个阳光明媚的早晨没有发现少了一两个汤匙的话，那么，他就会心满意足地——如此这般。

source: As they walked along, Oliver glanced from time to time with much interest and curiosity at the new comer. He seemed about five-and-twenty years of age, and was of the middle height; his countenance was frank and handsome; and his demeanor easy and prepossessing. Notwithstanding the difference between youth and age, he bore so strong a likeness to the old lady, that Oliver would have had no great difficulty in imagining their relationship, if he had not already spoken of her as his mother.
domestication: 他们边走边聊，奥利弗时不时地用好奇的目光打量着这位新朋友。这位年轻人约莫二十五六岁，中等个头，长了一副坦率帅气的面孔，举止自然又讨人喜欢。尽管年纪上有着差距，但他和那位老太太长得很像，要是奥利弗不知道老太太是他母亲的话，恐怕会以为他们是亲戚呢。
foreignization: 当他们沿着路走时，奥利弗不时以极大的兴趣和好奇心瞥视这位新来者。他看上去大约二十五岁左右，中等身材；他的面容坦率而英俊；举止轻松而讨人喜欢。尽管年纪有别，但他与老太太如此相像，如果奥利弗没有已经提到她是他的母亲的话，他也不会太难想象他们之间的关系。

Notwithstanding the difference between youth and age, he bore so strong a likeness to the old lady, that Oliver would have had no great difficulty in imagining their relationship, if he had not already spoken of her as his mother.
他们边走边聊，奥利弗时不时地用好奇的目光打量着这位新朋友。这位年轻人约莫二十五六岁，中等个头，长了一副坦率帅气的面孔，举止自然又讨人喜欢。尽管年纪上有着差距，但他和那位老太太长得很像，要是奥利弗不知道老太太是他母亲的话，恐怕会以为他们是亲戚呢。

Notwithstanding the difference between youth and age, he bore so strong a likeness to the old lady, that Oliver would have had no great difficulty in imagining their relationship, if he had not already spoken of her as his mother.

source: Oliver’s clothes had been torn in the beating he had received; his face was bruised and scratched; and his hair scattered over his forehead. The angry flush had not disappeared, however; and when he was pulled out of his prison, he scowled boldly on Noah, and looked quite undismayed.
domestication: 奥利弗的衣服在挨打时被撕破了，脸上青一块紫一块的，还有几道抓痕，头发散乱地披在额头上。不过，他脸上的怒火还没消，从牢房里被拽出来时，他瞪着诺亚，眼中满是不屈，看起来一点儿也不害怕。
foreignization: 奥利弗的衣服在他受到的殴打中被撕破了；他的脸上有瘀伤和抓痕；头发散落在额头上。然而，愤怒的红晕并没有消失；当他被从牢房里拉出来时，他大胆地怒视着诺亚，看起来一点也不害怕。

source: Among other public buildings in a certain town, which for many reasons it will be prudent to refrain from mentioning, and to which I will assign no fictitious name, there is one anciently common to most towns, great or small: to wit, a workhouse; and in this workhouse was born; on a day and date which I need not trouble myself to repeat, inasmuch as it can be of no possible consequence to the reader, in this stage of the business at all events; the item of mortality whose name is prefixed to the head of this chapter.
domestication: 在某个小镇上，出于种种原因，还是不要点名道姓的好，这里有一座几乎每个城镇，无论大小，都有的老建筑——济贫院。就在这个济贫院里，诞生了一个小生命，具体哪天哪月就不必细说了，反正对读者来说，现在知道这些也没什么用处。这个小生命的姓名，就写在这章的标题上了。
foreignization: 在某个镇上，出于多种原因明智地不提其名，也不给它安上任何虚构的名字，在那里有各种公共建筑中的一座，这种建筑自古以来几乎每个大小城镇都有，那就是济贫院；而在这个济贫院里出生了一个人；具体的日期和日子我就不必费心重复了，因为在事情的这个阶段，对读者来说这完全无关紧要；此人的名字被列在本章标题之下。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Among other public buildings in a certain town, which for many reasons it will be prudent to refrain from mentioning, and to which I will assign no fictitious name, there is one anciently common to most towns, great or small: to wit, a workhouse; and in this workhouse was born; on a day and date which I need not trouble myself to repeat, inasmuch as it can be of no possible consequence to the reader, in this stage of the business at all events; the item of mortality whose name is prefixed to the head of this chapter.
在某个小镇上，出于种种原因，还是不要点名道姓的好，这里有一座几乎每个城镇，无论大小，都有的老建筑——济贫院。就在这个济贫院里，诞生了一个小生命，具体哪天哪月就不必细说了，反正对读者来说，现在知道这些也没什么用处。这个小生命的姓名，就写在这章的标题上了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Among other public buildings in a certain town, which for many reasons it will be prudent to refrain from mentioning, and to which I will assign no fictitious name, there is one anciently common to most towns, great or small: to wit, a workhouse; and in this workhouse was born; on a day and date which I need not trouble myself to repeat, inasmuch as it can be of no possible consequence to the reader, in this stage of the business at all events; the item of mortality whose name is prefixed to the head of this chapter.
在某个镇上，出于多种原因明智地不提其名，也不给它安上任何虚构的名字，在那里有各种公共建筑中的一座，这种建筑自古以来几乎每个大小城镇都有，那就是济贫院；而在这个济贫院里出生了一个人；具体的日期和日子我就不必费心重复了，因为在事情的这个阶段，对读者来说这完全无关紧要；此人的名字被列在本章标题之下。

source: On pressed the people from the front—on, on, on, in a strong struggling current of angry faces, with here and there a glaring torch to lighten them up, and show them out in all their wrath and passion. The houses on the opposite side of the ditch had been entered by the mob; sashes were thrown up, or torn bodily out; there were tiers and tiers of faces in every window; cluster upon cluster of people clinging to every house-top. Each little bridge (and there were three in sight) bent beneath the weight of the crowd upon it. Still the current poured on to find some nook or hole from which to vent their shouts, and only for an instant see the wretch.
domestication: 人群被逼向前——往前，往前，往前，在愤怒的面孔汇成的汹涌人潮中，偶尔有火炬照亮，映出他们的怒火和激情。对面沟渠边的房子已被暴民占领，窗户被砸开或强行扯掉，每扇窗里都挤满了层层叠叠的脸庞，屋顶上也爬满了密密麻麻的人群。三座小桥都因人群的重压而弯曲变形。人潮依然不断涌动，寻找着每一个角落和缝隙，以便发出他们的怒吼，哪怕只看一眼那个罪人。
foreignization: 人们从前面蜂拥而上——向前，向前，向前，在愤怒的面孔汇成的强大斗争洪流中，不时有火炬闪亮照亮他们，使他们在愤怒和激情中显现出来。对面沟渠边的房子已经被暴民闯入；窗户被推开或撕裂；每个窗口都有层层叠叠的脸孔；每座屋顶都簇拥着人群。每一座小桥（眼前有三座）都在人群的重压下弯曲。然而，这股人流依然涌动，寻找某个角落或洞穴来发泄他们的呐喊，并仅仅在一瞬间看到那个可怜虫。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

On pressed the people from the front—on, on, on, in a strong struggling current of angry faces, with here and there a glaring torch to lighten them up, and show them out in all their wrath and passion. The houses on the opposite side of the ditch had been entered by the mob; sashes were thrown up, or torn bodily out; there were tiers and tiers of faces in every window; cluster upon cluster of people clinging to every house-top. Each little bridge (and there were three in sight) bent beneath the weight of the crowd upon it. Still the current poured on to find some nook or hole from which to vent their shouts, and only for an instant see the wretch.
