source: “Witness you three,” cried the boy shaking his clenched fist, and becoming more and more excited as he spoke. “Witness you three—I’m not afraid of him—if they come here after him, I’ll give him up; I will. I tell you out at once. He may kill me for it if he likes, or if he dares, but if I am here I’ll give him up. I’d give him up if he was to be boiled alive. Murder! Help! If there’s the pluck of a man among you three, you’ll help me. Murder! Help! Down with him!”
domestication: 你们三个给我作证，这小子握紧拳头，越说越激动。你们三个给我作证——我不怕他——要是他们来找他，我就把他交出去，一定交出去。我先跟你们说清楚。他要是敢，要是有胆子，就杀了我吧，只要我还活着，我就把他交出去。哪怕他会被活煮了，我也要交出去。杀人啦！救命啊！你们三个要是还有点男人的骨气，就帮帮我。杀人啦！救命啊！打倒他！
foreignization: “你们三个都给我作证，”男孩挥舞着紧握的拳头喊道，越说越激动。“你们三个都给我作证——我不怕他——如果他们来这里抓他，我会把他交出来；我会的。我一开始就告诉你们。他可以因为我这么做杀了我，如果他敢的话，但如果我还在这里，我会把他交出来。即使他会活活被煮死，我也会把他交出来。谋杀！救命！如果你们三个中还有一个人的勇气，你们会帮我的。谋杀！救命！打倒他！”

source: Oliver, who could hardly stand, made a shift to raise himself on his feet, and was at once lugged along the streets by the jacket-collar, at a rapid pace. The gentleman walked on with them by the officer’s side; and as many of the crowd as could achieve the feat, got a little ahead, and stared back at Oliver from time to time. The boys shouted in triumph; and on they went.
domestication: 奥利弗几乎站不稳，勉强撑着站起来，立刻就被抓着衣领，飞快地拖着沿街走去。那绅士跟在警官旁边一起走，人群中能挤到前面的人，时不时回头瞅瞅奥利弗。孩子们在一旁欢呼雀跃，一行人就这么往前去了。
foreignization: 奥利弗几乎站不起来，勉强挣扎着站起来，立刻被抓住衣领，以快速的步伐沿着街道拖行。那位绅士走在他们旁边，和警官并行；人群中尽可能做到这一点的人，稍微走到前面一些，时不时回头盯着奥利弗。男孩子们欢呼雀跃；他们继续前行。

The boys shouted in triumph; and on they went.

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver, who could hardly stand, made a shift to raise himself on his feet, and was at once lugged along the streets by the jacket-collar, at a rapid pace. The gentleman walked on with them by the officer’s side; and as many of the crowd as could achieve the feat, got a little ahead, and stared back at Oliver from time to time. The boys shouted in triumph; and on they went.
奥利弗几乎站不起来，勉强挣扎着站起来，立刻被抓住衣领，以快速的步伐沿着街道拖行。那位绅士走在他们旁边，和警官并行；人群中尽可能做到这一点的人，稍微走到前面一些，时不时回头盯着奥利弗。男孩子们欢呼雀跃；他们继续前行。

source: “Now, you must put your best leg foremost, old lady!” whispered Sowerberry in the old woman’s ear; “we are rather late; and it won’t do, to keep the clergyman waiting. Move on, my men,—as quick as you like!”
domestication: “老太太，现在你得打起精神来！”索伯瑞在老妇人耳边轻声说，“咱们已经有点晚了，可不能让牧师等太久。弟兄们，走吧——越快越好！”
foreignization: “现在，老太婆，你得把最好的脚往前迈！”索尔伯里在老太太耳边低语，“我们有点晚了；不能让牧师等着。前进，伙计们，——多快都行！”

source: The animal looked up into his master’s face while these preparations were making; whether his instinct apprehended something of their purpose, or the robber’s sidelong look at him was sterner than ordinary, he skulked a little farther in the rear than usual, and cowered as he came more slowly along. When his master halted at the brink of a pool, and looked round to call him, he stopped outright.
domestication: 那动物抬头望了望主人的脸，准备期间，不知是本能感觉到了什么，还是强盗斜眼看着它时比平时更严厉，它躲得比往常远了一些，走起来也慢吞吞的，显得有些畏畏缩缩。当主人在池塘边停下，回头叫它时，它索性停住了脚步。
foreignization: 动物在这些准备进行时抬头看着他的主人；不管是它的本能预感到了这些准备的目的，还是强盗斜眼看着它比平时更严厉，它比往常更加往后躲，走得更慢，显得畏缩。当主人在池塘边停下，环顾四周呼唤它时，它完全停了下来。

source: “Bolter, Bolter! Poor lad!” said Fagin, looking up with an expression of devilish anticipation, and speaking slowly and with marked emphasis. “He’s tired—tired with watching for her so long,—watching for her, Bill.”
domestication: “博尔特，博尔特！可怜的孩子！”费金抬头，脸上露出魔鬼般的期待，慢条斯理地说道，语气格外沉重。“他累了——为了她守了那么久，守得疲惫不堪了，比尔。”
foreignization: “博尔特，博尔特！可怜的孩子！”费金抬头说道，脸上带着一种魔鬼般的期待表情，说话缓慢且重音明显。“他累了——因为长时间等着她，等着她，比尔。”

source: Notwithstanding all this, when the hurry of the first half-hour was over, the same silence and constraint prevailed that had marked their journey down. Mr. Brownlow did not join them at dinner, but remained in a separate room. The two other gentlemen hurried in and out with anxious faces, and, during the short intervals when they were present, conversed apart. Once, Mrs. Maylie was called away, and after being absent for nearly an hour, returned with eyes swollen with weeping. All these things made Rose and Oliver, who were not in any new secrets, nervous and uncomfortable. They sat wondering, in silence; or, if they exchanged a few words, spoke in whispers, as if they were afraid to hear the sound of their own voices.
domestication: 尽管如此，半小时的忙乱过后，那种沉默和拘谨又回到了他们中间，就像他们来时一样。布朗洛先生没有和他们一起吃晚饭，而是留在了另一个房间。另外两位绅士则急匆匆地进进出出，脸上满是焦虑，在他们偶尔出现的短暂时间里，也是低声交谈。有一次，梅利太太被叫走了，差不多一个小时后才回来，眼睛哭得肿肿的。这一切都让不知情的罗斯和奥利弗感到紧张不安。他们默默地坐着，猜测着；即使偶尔说上几句话，也是轻声细语，仿佛害怕听到自己说话的声音。
foreignization: 尽管如此，当最初的半小时的忙乱过后，同样的沉默和拘束又像他们下楼时那样占据了主导。布朗洛先生没有和他们一起吃晚餐，而是留在了一个单独的房间里。另外两位绅士神情焦虑地匆匆进出，而在他们短暂在场的间隙里，则在一旁交谈。有一次，梅利太太被叫走了，差不多一个小时后才回来，眼睛因哭泣而肿胀。所有这些事情让罗斯和奥利弗感到紧张不安，因为他们并没有得到任何新的秘密。他们默默地坐着猜想；或者，即使互相交流几句，也是轻声细语，好像害怕听到自己的声音似的。

All these things made Rose and Oliver, who were not in any new secrets, nervous and uncomfortable. They sat wondering, in silence; or, if they exchanged a few words, spoke in whispers, as if they were afraid to hear the sound of their own voices.

source: Crackit went down to the door, and returned followed by a man with the lower part of his face buried in a handkerchief, and another tied over his head under his hat. He drew them slowly off. Blanched face, sunken eyes, hollow cheeks, beard of three days’ growth, wasted flesh, short thick breath; it was the very ghost of Sikes.
domestication: 克瑞基特走到门口，跟着进来一个男人，下半张脸埋在手帕里，头上还系着一条，藏在帽子下面。他慢慢解下手帕。脸色苍白如纸，眼窝深陷，两颊凹陷，三天没刮的胡子，瘦得皮包骨头，呼吸粗重短促；这简直就是西克斯的鬼魂重现。
foreignization: Crackit 走到门口，随后跟着一个男人回来了，这个男人的下半张脸埋在手帕里，另一块手帕系在他帽子下面的头上。他慢慢取下了它们。脸色苍白，眼睛凹陷，脸颊憔悴，三天没刮的胡子，消瘦的身体，粗重的呼吸；这就是西克斯的鬼魂。

source: Meanwhile, Oliver gradually throve and prospered under the united care of Mrs. Maylie, Rose, and the kind-hearted Mr. Losberne. If fervent prayers, gushing from hearts overcharged with gratitude, be heard in heaven—and if they be not, what prayers are!—the blessings which the orphan child called down upon them, sunk into their souls, diffusing peace and happiness.
domestication: 与此同时，奥利弗在梅利太太、罗斯和好心的洛斯伯恩先生的共同照料下，渐渐地茁壮成长起来。倘若诚挚的祷告，出自感恩满满的心灵，能在天堂被听见——若不然，那又何为祷告呢！——这孤儿所祈求的福泽，深深渗入他们的心田，带来无尽的安宁与幸福。
foreignization: 与此同时，奥利弗在梅利太太、罗斯和好心的洛斯伯恩先生的共同照顾下逐渐茁壮成长。如果发自充满感激之心的热烈祈祷能在天堂被听到——而如果它们不被听到，那还有什么祈祷能被听到呢！——这个孤儿孩子为他们祈求的祝福沉入了他们的灵魂，播撒了和平与幸福。

source: Bestowing something half-way between a smile and a frown upon his two companions, and again beckoning them to follow him, the man hastened across the apartment, which was of considerable extent, but low in the roof. He was preparing to ascend a steep staircase, or rather ladder, leading to another floor of warehouses above: when a bright flash of lightning streamed down the aperture, and a peal of thunder followed, which shook the crazy building to its centre.
domestication: 那人对着两个同伴露出一个似笑非笑、似怒非怒的表情，再次示意他们跟上，便急匆匆地穿过这间又大又矮的房间。他正准备攀上通往楼上仓库的陡峭梯子，这时一道闪电从天窗射进来，紧随其后的一声巨响，震得这破旧的建筑都摇晃起来。
foreignization: 给他的两个同伴报以介于微笑和皱眉之间的一种表情，并再次示意他们跟着他，那人急忙穿过这个相当宽敞但屋顶很低的房间。他正准备爬上一个陡峭的楼梯，或者说梯子，通向上面的另一层仓库：这时一道明亮的闪电透过缝隙照了进来，随之而来的一声雷鸣使这座摇摇欲坠的建筑震动到了核心。

source: She staggered and fell: nearly blinded with the blood that rained down from a deep gash in her forehead; but raising herself, with difficulty, on her knees, drew from her bosom a white handkerchief—Rose Maylie’s own—and holding it up, in her folded hands, as high towards Heaven as her feeble strength would allow, breathed one prayer for mercy to her Maker.
domestication: 她摇摇晃晃地跌倒了，前额上的一道深口子鲜血直流，几乎让她看不见路。但她费了好大力气，才勉强跪了起来，从怀里掏出一块白手帕——这可是罗丝·梅利的宝贝——双手合十举得高高的，用尽最后的力气向天祈祷，求上帝宽恕她。
foreignization: 她踉跄了一下，摔倒了：鲜血从她前额的一道深口中如雨般落下，几乎使她失明；但她挣扎着跪了起来，从怀里掏出一块白手帕——罗丝·梅利的那块——双手合十，将其举向天空，竭尽她微弱的力量所能及的高度，向她的造物主祈求了一次宽恕。

source: “I’ll take it clean out, sir,” replied the man, winking to the company, “before you can come across the room to get it. Gentlemen all, observe the dark stain upon this gentleman’s hat, no wider than a shilling, but thicker than a half-crown. Whether it is a wine-stain, fruit-stain, beer-stain, water-stain, paint-stain, pitch-stain, mud-stain, or blood-stain—”
domestication: “我保管把它弄干净，先生，”那人笑着对大家说，“您还没走到跟前，它就不见了。各位，瞧这位先生帽子上的黑点，这黑点不大，也就一先令那么宽，但厚度却有半克朗那么厚。这黑点嘛，可能是酒渍，也可能是果渍、啤酒渍、水渍、油漆渍、沥青渍、泥渍，或者是血渍——”
foreignization: “我会把它完全清除，先生，”那人回答说，向在场的人眨了眨眼，“在您穿过房间来取它之前。诸位先生，请注意这位先生帽子上的深色污渍，宽度不超过一先令，但厚度超过半克朗。无论是酒渍、果渍、啤酒渍、水渍、油漆渍、焦油渍、泥渍还是血渍——”

source: There were tears in the eyes of the gentle girl, as these words were spoken; and when one fell upon the flower over which she bent, and glistened brightly in its cup, making it more beautiful, it seemed as though the outpouring of her fresh young heart, claimed kindred naturally, with the loveliest things in nature.
domestication: 温柔的姑娘说着说着，眼眶里涌出了泪水；一滴泪珠落在她低头俯视的花上，晶莹闪亮，让花儿更加美丽动人，仿佛她那颗纯真年轻的心，自然地与世间最美好的事物产生了共鸣。
foreignization: 温柔的女孩在说这些话时眼中含着泪水；当一滴泪珠落在她俯身的花朵上，在花心中闪烁着明亮的光芒，使它更加美丽时，仿佛她那颗清新年轻的心的流露，自然而然地与自然界中最美好的事物产生了共鸣。

source: Persuaded, in part, by these representations, but overborne in a much greater degree by his fear of Fagin, Mr. Bolter at length consented, with a very bad grace, to undertake the expedition. By Fagin’s directions, he immediately substituted for his own attire, a waggoner’s frock, velveteen breeches, and leather leggings: all of which articles the Jew had at hand. He was likewise furnished with a felt hat well garnished with turnpike tickets; and a carter’s whip. Thus equipped, he was to saunter into the office, as some country fellow from Covent Garden market might be supposed to do for the gratification of his curiousity; and as he was as awkward, ungainly, and raw-boned a fellow as need be, Mr. Fagin had no fear but that he would look the part to perfection.
domestication: 半推半就地，博尔特先生最终还是答应了，虽然心里一百个不情愿。这多半是因为对费金的害怕，而不是因为别人的劝说。按照费金的指示，他立马换上了赶车人的行头：一件大围裙，一条绒布马裤，还有皮护腿，这些都是犹太老头儿早准备好了的。他还给了他一顶装饰着过路票的毡帽和一根赶车人的鞭子。打扮成这样，他就得像乡下来的伙计一样，假装是来科文特花园市场图个新鲜的，晃晃悠悠地走进办公室。博尔特先生本人长得又高又瘦，笨手笨脚，费金一点不担心他会演不像，反而觉得这样正合适，肯定能以假乱真。
foreignization: 在这些说辞的部分说服下，但更大程度上是被他对费金的恐惧所压倒，博尔特先生最终极不情愿地同意承担这次行动。按照费金的指示，他立即换上了马车夫的罩衣、绒裤和皮腿套：所有这些衣物犹太人都已准备妥当。他还得到了一顶装饰着许多通行费票据的毡帽和一根赶车人的鞭子。装备齐全后，他要悠哉游哉地走进办公室，就像某个从科文特花园市场来的乡下人出于好奇可能会做的那样；而由于他本人就是一个笨拙、丑陋、瘦长的家伙，费金先生毫不担心他会演得不够逼真。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: “That’s two doubles and the rub,” said Mr. Chitling, with a very long face, as he drew half-a-crown from his waistcoat-pocket. “I never see such a feller as you, Jack; you win everything. Even when we’ve good cards, Charley and I can’t make nothing of ’em.”
domestication: “两倍赌注加利息，”奇林先生长着一张苦瓜脸，从背心口袋里掏出半克朗说道，“杰克啊，我从来没见过像你这样的人；你赢了所有的东西。哪怕我们手里的牌好，查理和我也玩不出个名堂来。”
foreignization: “那是两个双打和一个单打，”奇特林先生说，他拉长了脸，从背心口袋里掏出半克朗。“我从来没见过像你这样的人，杰克；你赢了一切。即使我们有好牌，查理和我也无法从中获利。”

source: Mr. Giles was apparently considering the propriety of indulging in a respectful smile himself, when a gig drove up to the garden-gate: out of which there jumped a fat gentleman, who ran straight up to the door: and who, getting quickly into the house by some mysterious process, burst into the room, and nearly overturned Mr. Giles and the breakfast-table together.
domestication: 吉尔斯先生正琢磨着要不要也礼貌地笑笑，这时一辆轻便马车停在了花园门口。一个胖 gentlemen 从车上跳了下来，直奔大门，不知怎么三两步就进了屋，猛地闯进房间，差点把吉尔斯先生和早餐桌一起撞翻。
foreignization: 吉尔斯先生显然正在考虑是否适合自己露出一个恭敬的微笑，这时一辆轻便马车停在了花园门口：从中跳出一个胖 gentlemen，他径直跑向大门：并且通过某种神秘的方式迅速进入屋内，冲进房间，差点将吉尔斯先生和早餐桌一起撞倒。

吉尔斯先生显然正在考虑是否适合自己露出一个恭敬的微笑，这时一辆轻便马车停在了花园门口：从中跳出一个胖 gentlemen，他径直跑向大门：并且通过某种神秘的方式迅速进入屋内，冲进房间，差点将吉尔斯先生和早餐桌一起撞倒。

source: “Well,” said the Jew, inspecting them closely; “they’re very good ones, very. You haven’t marked them well, though, Charley; so the marks shall be picked out with a needle, and we’ll teach Oliver how to do it. Shall us, Oliver, eh? Ha! ha! ha!”
domestication: 这犹太人仔细打量了它们一番，说道：“还真不错，真不错。不过，查理，你刻的记号可不怎么好；我们得用针把这些记号挑出来，还得教教奥利弗怎么干这活。怎么样，奥利弗，咱们一起干吧？哈！哈！哈！”
foreignization: “呣，”那个犹太人仔细检查了一下；“它们非常好，非常。不过，查理，你没有把它们刻得很清楚；所以要用针把这些记号挑出来，我们得教奥利弗怎么做。是不是，奥利弗，嗯？哈！哈！哈！”

“Well,” said the Jew, inspecting them closely; “they’re very good ones, very. You haven’t marked them well, though, Charley; so the marks shall be picked out with a needle, and we’ll teach Oliver how to do it. Shall us, Oliver, eh? Ha! ha! ha!”

source: These arrangements completed, he was informed of the necessary signs and tokens by which to recognise the Artful Dodger, and was conveyed by Master Bates through dark and winding ways to within a very short distance of Bow Street. Having described the precise situation of the office, and accompanied it with copious directions how he was to walk straight up the passage, and when he got into the side, and pull off his hat as he went into the room, Charley Bates bade him hurry on alone, and promised to bide his return on the spot of their parting.
domestication: 这些安排妥当后，他被告知了一些暗号和标记，以便认出机灵鬼。随后，在贝茨先生的带领下，他穿过一条条昏暗曲折的小巷，来到了离鲍街不远的地方。贝茨仔仔细细地描述了办公室的具体位置，还告诉他怎么径直走过走廊，进了侧门后要摘下帽子再进房间。说完，查利·贝茨催他快点独自上路，自己则答应在分手的地方等他回来。
foreignization: 这些安排完成后，他被告知必要的暗号和标志，以便认出机灵鬼。然后由贝茨先生带他穿过黑暗曲折的小路，来到距离鲍街非常近的地方。详细描述了办公室的确切位置，并附上了详细的指示，告诉他如何直接走过通道，进入侧门后摘下帽子走进房间。查理·贝茨让他独自赶紧前往，并承诺会在他们分手的地方等他回来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

These arrangements completed, he was informed of the necessary signs and tokens by which to recognise the Artful Dodger, and was conveyed by Master Bates through dark and winding ways to within a very short distance of Bow Street. Having described the precise situation of the office, and accompanied it with copious directions how he was to walk straight up the passage, and when he got into the side, and pull off his hat as he went into the room, Charley Bates bade him hurry on alone, and promised to bide his return on the spot of their parting.
这些安排妥当后，他被告知了一些暗号和标记，以便认出机灵鬼。随后，在贝茨先生的带领下，他穿过一条条昏暗曲折的小巷，来到了离鲍街不远的地方。贝茨仔仔细细地描述了办公室的具体位置，还告诉他怎么径直走过走廊，进了侧门后要摘下帽子再进房间。说完，查利·贝茨催他快点独自上路，自己则答应在分手的地方等他回来。

Having described the precise situation of the office, and accompanied it with copious directions how he was to walk straight up the passage, and when he got into the side, and pull off his hat as he went into the room, Charley Bates bade him hurry on alone, and promised to bide his return on the spot of their parting.
这些安排完成后，他被告知必要的暗号和标志，以便认出机灵鬼。然后由贝茨先生带他穿过黑暗曲折的小路，来到距离鲍街非常近的地方。详细描述了办公室的确切位置，并附上了详细的指示，告诉他如何直接走过通道，进入侧门后摘下帽子走进房间。查理·贝茨让他独自赶紧前往，并承诺会在他们分手的地方等他回来。

source: Having eased his mind by this discovery; and having accomplished his twofold object of imparting to the girl what he had, that night, heard, and of ascertaining, with his own eyes, that Sikes had not returned, Mr. Fagin again turned his face homeward: leaving his young friend asleep, with her head upon the table.
domestication: 发现这个情况后，他心里踏实了许多；不仅把当晚听到的事告诉了女孩，还亲眼确认了赛克斯没回来，菲金这才放心地往回走，留下那女孩头枕着桌子睡着了。
foreignization: 通过这一发现使他的心安定了下来；并且实现了他双重的目的：一是将他当夜所听到的告诉了女孩，二是亲眼确认赛克斯没有回来，费金先生再次转身回家：留下他的年轻朋友睡着了，头枕在桌子上。

Having eased his mind by this discovery; and having accomplished his twofold object of imparting to the girl what he had, that night, heard, and of ascertaining, with his own eyes, that Sikes had not returned, Mr. Fagin again turned his face homeward: leaving his young friend asleep, with her head upon the table.
发现这个情况后，他心里踏实了许多；不仅把当晚听到的事告诉了女孩，还亲眼确认了赛克斯没回来，菲金这才放心地往回走，留下那女孩头枕着桌子睡着了。

Having eased his mind by this discovery; and having accomplished his twofold object of imparting to the girl what he had, that night, heard, and of ascertaining, with his own eyes, that Sikes had not returned, Mr. Fagin again turned his face homeward: leaving his young friend asleep, with her head upon the table.
通过这一发现使他的心安定了下来；并且实现了他双重的目的：一是将他当夜所听到的告诉了女孩，二是亲眼确认赛克斯没有回来，费金先生再次转身回家：留下他的年轻朋友睡着了，头枕在桌子上。

source: “How are you, Faguey?” said this worthy, nodding to the Jew. “Pop that shawl away in my castor, Dodger, so that I may know where to find it when I cut; that’s the time of day! You’ll be a fine young cracksman afore the old file now.”
domestication: “法格伊，你好吗？”这好人冲犹太人点了点头。“多杰，把那披肩收进我的大衣里，这样我等会儿好找。就是现在！你这小子在老手面前得学着点，很快就能成为一流的扒手了。”

source: “He is tall,” said the girl, “and a strongly made man, but not stout; he has a lurking walk; and as he walks, constantly looks over his shoulder, first on one side, and then on the other. Don’t forget that, for his eyes are sunk in his head so much deeper than any other man’s, that you might almost tell him by that alone. His face is dark, like his hair and eyes; and, although he can’t be more than six or eight and twenty, withered and haggard. His lips are often discoloured and disfigured with the marks of teeth; for he has desperate fits, and sometimes even bites his hands and covers them with wounds—why did you start?” said the girl, stopping suddenly.
domestication: 女孩说：“他个子高高的，身子骨也结实，但并不胖。走路时总是蹑手蹑脚的，还时不时回头张望，先看看这边，再瞧瞧那边。你可别忘了这点，因为他的眼睛比别人的深陷得多，几乎单凭这一点就能认出他来。他的脸黑黑的，头发和眼睛也是黑的。虽然年纪不过二十六七岁，但看起来苍老憔悴。嘴唇经常被牙齿咬得又青又紫，因为他有时候会发狂，甚至咬自己的手，弄得满手都是伤——你怎么突然这么紧张？”女孩突然停了下来，问道。
foreignization: “他很高，”女孩说，“是个结实的男人，但并不胖；他走路时脚步轻浮；而且走的时候，经常回头看，先是向一边，然后又向另一边看。别忘了这一点，因为他的眼睛比其他人的深陷得多，几乎单凭这一点就能认出他来。他的脸很黑，和他的头发和眼睛一样；虽然他不会超过二十六八岁，但面容憔悴、苍老。他的嘴唇经常因牙齿的痕迹而变色和变形；因为他有时会发狂，甚至咬自己的手，弄得满是伤痕——你为什么突然一惊？”女孩突然停下来说。

“He is tall,” said the girl, “and a strongly made man, but not stout; he has a lurking walk; and as he walks, constantly looks over his shoulder, first on one side, and then on the other. Don’t forget that, for his eyes are sunk in his head so much deeper than any other man’s, that you might almost tell him by that alone. His face is dark, like his hair and eyes; and, although he can’t be more than six or eight and twenty, withered and haggard. “他很高，”女孩说，“是个结实的男人，但并不胖；他走路时脚步轻浮；而且走的时候，经常回头看，先是向一边，然后又向另一边看。别忘了这一点，因为他的眼睛比其他人的深陷得多，几乎单凭这一点就能认出他来。他的脸很黑，和他的头发和眼睛一样；虽然他不会超过二十六八岁，但面容憔悴、苍老。他的嘴唇经常因牙齿的痕迹而变色和变形；因为他有时会发狂，甚至咬自己的手，弄得满是伤痕——你为什么突然一惊？”女孩突然停下来说。

source: “Honour, eh?” said the doctor; “well, I don’t know; perhaps it’s as honourable to hit a thief in a back kitchen, as to hit your man at twelve paces. Fancy that he fired in the air, and you’ve fought a duel, Giles.”
domestication: “荣誉，嗯？”医生说，“我说不好；也许在后厨打一个小偷和在十二步外打一个人一样光荣。你就当他是朝天开枪了，吉尔斯，你这架也就算打完了。”
foreignization: “荣誉，是吗？”医生说；“嗯，我不知道；也许在后厨房打一个小偷和在十二步外打倒你的对手一样光荣。想象他朝空中开枪了，你已经决斗过了，吉尔斯。”

“Honour, eh?” said the doctor; “well, I don’t know; perhaps it’s as honourable to hit a thief in a back kitchen, as to hit your man at twelve paces. Fancy that he fired in the air, and you’ve fought a duel, Giles.”

“Honour, eh?” said the doctor; “well, I don’t know; perhaps it’s as honourable to hit a thief in a back kitchen, as to hit your man at twelve paces. Fancy that he fired in the air, and you’ve fought a duel, Giles.”
“荣誉，是吗？”医生说；“嗯，我不知道；也许在后厨房打一个小偷和在十二步外打倒你的对手一样光荣。想象他朝空中开枪了，你已经决斗过了，吉尔斯。”

source: The twilight was beginning to close in, when Mr. Brownlow alighted from a hackney-coach at his own door, and knocked softly. The door being opened, a sturdy man got out of the coach and stationed himself on one side of the steps, while another man, who had been seated on the box, dismounted too, and stood upon the other side. At a sign from Mr. Brownlow, they helped out a third man, and taking him between them, hurried him into the house. This man was Monks.
domestication: 天色渐渐暗了下来，布朗洛先生从一辆出租马车上下来，轻轻敲了敲自家的门。门一开，一个壮汉从车里钻了出来，站在台阶的一边；而刚才坐在车夫位置上的另一个男人也跳了下来，站在另一边。布朗洛先生打了个手势，两人便扶出第三个人，架着他匆匆进了屋。这人就是蒙克斯。
foreignization: 暮色开始降临，布朗洛先生从一辆出租马车里下车，在自家门口轻轻敲门。门开了后，一个健壮的男人从马车里出来，站在台阶的一侧，而另一个坐在车夫座位上的男人也下来了，站在另一侧。在布朗洛先生的示意下，他们扶出第三个人，架着他匆匆进了屋。这个人就是蒙克斯。

暮色开始降临，布朗洛先生从一辆出租马车里下车，在自家门口轻轻敲门。门开了后，一个健壮的男人从马车里出来，站在台阶的一侧，而另一个坐在车夫座位上的男人也下来了，站在另一侧。在布朗洛先生的示意下，他们扶出第三个人，架着他匆匆进了屋。这个人就是蒙克斯。

source: With the next morning, there came a rumour, that two men and a boy were in the cage at Kingston, who had been apprehended over night under suspicious circumstances; and to Kingston Messrs. Blathers and Duff journeyed accordingly. The suspicious circumstances, however, resolving themselves, on investigation, into the one fact, that they had been discovered sleeping under a haystack; which, although a great crime, is only punishable by imprisonment, and is, in the merciful eye of the English law, and its comprehensive love of all the King’s subjects, held to be no satisfactory proof, in the absence of all other evidence, that the sleeper, or sleepers, have committed burglary accompanied with violence, and have therefore rendered themselves liable to the punishment of death; Messrs. Blathers and Duff came back again, as wise as they went.
domestication: 第二天一早，风声传来，说在金斯顿的牢笼里关着两个男人和一个男孩，他们是头天晚上因可疑行为被逮的。于是，布莱瑟先生和达夫先生就去了金斯顿。不过，一番调查下来，所谓的可疑行为，不过是他们在干草堆下睡觉被发现了。虽说这罪不轻，但也就判个监禁，按英国法律那宽厚仁慈的心肠，加上对国王所有臣民的关怀，若无其他证据，单凭这一点，可不能证明睡在干草堆下的人犯了暴力盗窃罪，更别提判死刑了。所以，布莱瑟先生和达夫先生这一趟，去时聪明，回时也一样聪明。
foreignization: 随着第二天早晨，传来一个传言，说有两个人和一个男孩因涉嫌可疑情况在夜间被捕，关进了金斯顿的囚笼；于是，布莱瑟先生和达夫先生相应地前往了金斯顿。然而，经过调查，这些可疑的情况最终归结为一个事实，即他们被发现睡在一个干草堆下；尽管这是一个严重的罪行，但只处以监禁，而且根据英国法律的仁慈精神及其对所有国王臣民的广泛关爱，在没有其他证据的情况下，这并不能作为令人满意的证明，表明睡眠者或睡眠者们犯有暴力入室盗窃罪，因此不应受到死刑的惩罚；于是，布莱瑟先生和达夫先生又回来了，和他们去时一样明智。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Blathers and Duff journeyed accordingly. The suspicious circumstances, however, resolving themselves, on investigation, into the one fact, that they had been discovered sleeping under a haystack; which, although a great crime, is only punishable by imprisonment, and is, in the merciful eye of the English law, and its comprehensive love of all the King’s subjects, held to be no satisfactory proof, in the absence of all other evidence, that the sleeper, or sleepers, have committed burglary accompanied with violence, and have therefore rendered themselves liable to the punishment of death; Messrs. Blathers and Duff came back again, as wise as they went.

With the next morning, there came a rumour, that two men and a boy were in the cage at Kingston, who had been apprehended over night under suspicious circumstances; and to Kingston Messrs. The suspicious circumstances, however, resolving themselves, on investigation, into the one fact, that they had been discovered sleeping under a haystack; which, although a great crime, is only punishable by imprisonment, and is, in the merciful eye of the English law, and its comprehensive love of all the King’s subjects, held to be no satisfactory proof, in the absence of all other evidence, that the sleeper, or sleepers, have committed burglary accompanied with violence, and have therefore rendered themselves liable to the punishment of death; Messrs. 随着第二天早晨，传来一个传言，说有两个人和一个男孩因涉嫌可疑情况在夜间被捕，关进了金斯顿的囚笼；于是，布莱瑟先生和达夫先生相应地前往了金斯顿。然而，经过调查，这些可疑的情况最终归结为一个事实，即他们被发现睡在一个干草堆下；尽管这是一个严重的罪行，但只处以监禁，而且根据英国法律的仁慈精神及其对所有国王臣民的广泛关爱，在没有其他证据的情况下，这并不能作为令人满意的证明，表明睡眠者或睡眠者们犯有暴力入室盗窃罪，因此不应受到死刑的惩罚；于是，布莱瑟先生和达夫先生又回来了，和他们去时一样明智。

source: “It’s no fault of mine if he is!” interposed the other man, with a look of terror, and clasping the Jew’s arm with trembling hands. “Mind that. Fagin! I had no hand in it. Anything but his death, I told you from the first. I won’t shed blood; it’s always found out, and haunts a man besides. If they shot him dead, I was not the cause; do you hear me? Fire this infernal den! What’s that?”
domestication: “这可不能怪我！”那人吓得脸色苍白，战战兢兢地抓住犹太人的胳膊说，“你听好了，费金！这事和我没一丁点儿关系。我一开始就和你说过，我不想让他死。我可不想沾上血，那东西迟早会露馅，还会缠着你。如果他们真把他打死了，也和我没半毛钱关系，你听清楚了吗？快把这个鬼地方点着了！什么声音？”
foreignization: “这又不是我的错！”另一个人插话道，脸上带着恐惧的神情，用颤抖的手抓住犹太人的胳膊。“你可要记住了，费金！我可没参与这事。从一开始我就告诉过你，除了他死我什么都干。我不会流血的；那总是会被发现，而且还会纠缠人。如果他们把他打死了，我可不是原因；你听明白了吗？放火烧了这个该死的地方！那是什么？”

source: The room remained deserted for a quarter of an hour or more; the girl glided back with the same unearthly tread; and, immediately afterwards, the two men were heard descending. Monks went at once into the street; and the Jew crawled upstairs again for the money. When he returned, the girl was adjusting her shawl and bonnet, as if preparing to be gone.
domestication: 房间里空荡荡的，过了好一阵子，那姑娘又飘了回来，脚步轻得跟鬼魂似的。紧跟着，就听到那两个男人下楼的声音。一眨眼工夫，蒙克斯就消失在街上；那犹太老头则爬回楼上取钱去了。等他再回来时，只见那姑娘正整理着披肩和帽子，一副要出门的样子。
foreignization: 这房间空荡荡的持续了十五分钟或更久；那女孩以同样的鬼魅般的步态滑了回来；紧接着，就听到那两个人下楼的声音。蒙克斯立刻走上街头；而犹太人则爬回楼上取钱。当他回来时，女孩正在整理她的披肩和帽子，仿佛准备离开。

这房间空荡荡的持续了十五分钟或更久；那女孩以同样的鬼魅般的步态滑了回来；紧接着，就听到那两个人下楼的声音。蒙克斯立刻走上街头；而犹太人则爬回楼上取钱。当他回来时，女孩正在整理她的披肩和帽子，仿佛准备离开。

source: “I can’t help it,” said Charley, “I can’t help it! To see him splitting away at that pace, and cutting round the corners, and knocking up again’ the posts, and starting on again as if he was made of iron as well as them, and me with the wipe in my pocket, singing out arter him—oh, my eye!” The vivid imagination of Master Bates presented the scene before him in too strong colours. As he arrived at this apostrophe, he again rolled upon the door-step, and laughed louder than before.
domestication: “我控制不住自己，”查理说，“我实在忍不住！看到他那样飞快地跑，转角时还那么利索，撞到柱子上又马上站起来，像是铁打的一样，而我却在后面追着他，口袋里还揣着那块布，喊得我嗓子都快冒烟了——哎哟，我的老天！”巴茨少爷的想象力太丰富了，描绘得活灵活现。说到这儿，他又在地上打了个滚，笑得比刚才还要大声。
foreignization: “我忍不住了，”查利说，“我忍不住了！看到他以那么快的速度跑，绕过那些弯道，撞到柱子上又重新站起来，好像他是铁打的一样，而我口袋里有手帕，还冲着他喊——哦，天哪！”小贝茨的生动想象力将这一幕展现得太过强烈。当他说到这儿时，他又一次滚倒在门阶上，笑得比之前更响亮。

source: “Of course not,” replied the doctor, with a nervous glance at the two ladies. “I know his whole history: but we can talk about that presently. You would like, first, to see the place where the thieves made their attempt, I suppose?”
domestication: “当然不是。”医生紧张地瞥了那两位女士一眼，答道。“他的事我全都知道，不过咱们待会儿再细说。你肯定先想看看贼人们下手的地方吧？”
foreignization: “当然不，”医生回答说，同时紧张地 glanced at the two ladies。我知道他所有的历史：但我们待会儿再谈这个。你是不是想先看看小偷行窃的地方？”

source: Along this same footpath, Oliver well-remembered he had trotted beside Mr. Bumble, when he first carried him to the workhouse from the farm. His way lay directly in front of the cottage. His heart beat quickly when he bethought himself of this; and he half resolved to turn back. He had come a long way though, and should lose a great deal of time by doing so. Besides, it was so early that there was very little fear of his being seen; so he walked on.
domestication: 沿着这条小路，奥利弗记得他当初被班布尔先生从农场带到济贫院时，就是在这条路上跑着的。一想到这，他的心就跳得快了起来，差点儿打算掉头回去。可他已经走了这么远，如果现在回去，就要浪费很多时间了。再说，这么早，几乎不可能被人看见。于是，他就继续往前走。
foreignization: 沿着同一条小路，奥利弗清楚地记得他曾跟在邦布尔先生身边，当他第一次把他从农场带到济贫院时。他的路正好经过那座小屋的前面。想到这一点，他心里一阵狂跳；他一半想转身回去。然而他已经走了很远的路，如果回头将会浪费很多时间。此外，时间还这么早，被看到的可能性很小；所以他继续往前走。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Along this same footpath, Oliver well-remembered he had trotted beside Mr. Bumble, when he first carried him to the workhouse from the farm. His way lay directly in front of the cottage. Besides, it was so early that there was very little fear of his being seen; so he walked on.

source: Sikes dismounted with great precipitation, holding Oliver by the hand all the while; and lifting him down directly, bestowed a furious look upon him, and rapped the side-pocket with his fist, in a significant manner.
domestication: 赛克斯急匆匆下了马，一路拉着奥利弗；一落地，他就狠狠瞪了奥利弗一眼，还用拳头敲了敲侧兜，那意思再明白不过了。
foreignization: 锡克斯急忙下马，一直牵着奥利弗的手；然后立即把他扶下来，恶狠狠地瞪了他一眼，并以有意义的方式用拳头敲了敲侧袋。

source: “We stay in town, of course,” said Mrs. Maylie, “while there remains the slightest prospect of prosecuting this inquiry with a chance of success. I will spare neither trouble nor expense in behalf of the object in which we are all so deeply interested, and I am content to remain here, if it be for twelve months, so long as you assure me that any hope remains.”
domestication: “我们当然要留在镇上，”梅利太太说，“只要有一线希望可以继续调查下去，找到线索。为了我们大家都这么关心的事情，我什么辛苦都不怕，多少钱都愿意花。哪怕要等上一年，只要您还告诉我有希望，我就心满意足地等着。”
foreignization: “我们当然留在镇上，”梅利太太说，“只要还有一线希望能让这项调查取得成功。我将不遗余力，不惜一切代价来帮助我们共同关心的目标，我愿意在这里待上十二个月，只要您能向我保证还有任何希望。”

“We stay in town, of course,” said Mrs. Maylie, “while there remains the slightest prospect of prosecuting this inquiry with a chance of success. I will spare neither trouble nor expense in behalf of the object in which we are all so deeply interested, and I am content to remain here, if it be for twelve months, so long as you assure me that any hope remains.”

“We stay in town, of course,” said Mrs. Maylie, “while there remains the slightest prospect of prosecuting this inquiry with a chance of success. I will spare neither trouble nor expense in behalf of the object in which we are all so deeply interested, and I am content to remain here, if it be for twelve months, so long as you assure me that any hope remains.”
“我们当然留在镇上，”梅利太太说，“只要还有一线希望能让这项调查取得成功。我将不遗余力，不惜一切代价来帮助我们共同关心的目标，我愿意在这里待上十二个月，只要您能向我保证还有任何希望。”

source: “Not a living leg,” answered Mr. Crackit, pulling up his collar; “it’s been as dull as swipes. You ought to stand something handsome, Fagin, to recompense me for keeping house so long. Damme, I’m as flat as a juryman; and should have gone to sleep, as fast as Newgate, if I hadn’t had the good natur’ to amuse this youngster. Horrid dull, I’m blessed if I an’t!”
domestication: “一条活路都没有，”克拉基特先生说着，拉了拉衣领；“这日子过得跟刷油漆一样乏味。费金，你得意思意思，给我点儿好处，补偿我这么久以来的辛劳。天杀的，我现在无聊得像陪审团的成员一样，要不是我好心陪这个小孩儿解闷，我早就睡着了，比纽盖特监狱的大门关得还快。真是无聊透顶，我敢说！”
foreignization: “一条活腿也没有，”克瑞基特先生拉起衣领回答说；“这比擦皮鞋还无聊。你该给我点像样的东西，费金，来补偿我这么久的守家。见鬼，我比陪审团成员还无精打采；要不是我好心逗这个小孩玩，我早就和纽盖特一样睡着了。真他妈的无聊，我发誓！”

source: At this intelligence, the worthy Mrs. Corney muttered a variety of invectives against old women who couldn’t even die without purposely annoying their betters; and, muffling herself in a thick shawl which she hastily caught up, briefly requested Mr. Bumble to stay till she came back, lest anything particular should occur. Bidding the messenger walk fast, and not be all night hobbling up the stairs, she followed her from the room with a very ill grace, scolding all the way.
domestication: 听到这个消息，科尼太太嘟囔着骂了一通老太婆，说她们连死都要故意给比自己地位高的人添麻烦；接着她匆匆披上一件厚斗篷，随口对邦布尔先生说，让他等着自己回来，免得有什么特别的事情发生。她命令送信的快点走，别磨蹭得跟上楼梯似的，一边很不情愿地跟着那人出了房间，一路上还唠唠叨叨地抱怨个不停。
foreignization: 听到这个消息，可敬的科尼太太对着那些连死都要故意惹怒比她们身份高的人的老女人咒骂了一通；然后，她急忙抓起一件厚厚的披肩裹在身上，简短地要求邦布尔先生等她回来，以免发生什么特别的事情。她命令送信人快点走，别在楼梯上磨蹭一整晚，然后很不情愿地跟着她出了房间，一路上都在骂骂咧咧。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

At this intelligence, the worthy Mrs. Corney muttered a variety of invectives against old women who couldn’t even die without purposely annoying their betters; and, muffling herself in a thick shawl which she hastily caught up, briefly requested Mr. Bumble to stay till she came back, lest anything particular should occur. Bidding the messenger walk fast, and not be all night hobbling up the stairs, she followed her from the room with a very ill grace, scolding all the way.
听到这个消息，科尼太太嘟囔着骂了一通老太婆，说她们连死都要故意给比自己地位高的人添麻烦；接着她匆匆披上一件厚斗篷，随口对邦布尔先生说，让他等着自己回来，免得有什么特别的事情发生。她命令送信的快点走，别磨蹭得跟上楼梯似的，一边很不情愿地跟着那人出了房间，一路上还唠唠叨叨地抱怨个不停。

At this intelligence, the worthy Mrs. Corney muttered a variety of invectives against old women who couldn’t even die without purposely annoying their betters; and, muffling herself in a thick shawl which she hastily caught up, briefly requested Mr. Bumble to stay till she came back, lest anything particular should occur. Bidding the messenger walk fast, and not be all night hobbling up the stairs, she followed her from the room with a very ill grace, scolding all the way.
听到这个消息，可敬的科尼太太对着那些连死都要故意惹怒比她们身份高的人的老女人咒骂了一通；然后，她急忙抓起一件厚厚的披肩裹在身上，简短地要求邦布尔先生等她回来，以免发生什么特别的事情。她命令送信人快点走，别在楼梯上磨蹭一整晚，然后很不情愿地跟着她出了房间，一路上都在骂骂咧咧。

source: “Sitch a rabbit pie, Bill,” exclaimed that young gentleman, disclosing to view a huge pasty; “sitch delicate creeturs, with sitch tender limbs, Bill, that the wery bones melt in your mouth, and there’s no occasion to pick ’em; half a pound of seven and six-penny green, so precious strong that if you mix it with biling water, it’ll go nigh to blow the lid of the tea-pot off; a pound and a half of moist sugar that the niggers didn’t work at all at, afore they got it up to sitch a pitch of goodness,—oh no! Two half-quartern brans; pound of best fresh; piece of double Glo’ster; and, to wind up all, some of the richest sort you ever lushed!”
domestication: “比尔，来做个兔子派吧，”那位年轻先生喊道，露出一个大馅饼，“这些小家伙，肉嫩得骨头都能在嘴里化掉，根本不用吐骨头；还有半磅七先令六便士的绿茶，那茶可劲儿浓了，一冲开水就能差点把茶壶盖给顶起来；一磅半的糖浆，黑奴们都没怎么费劲就弄到这么好——可不是嘛！两个麦麸面包，一磅上好的新鲜面包，一块双层格洛斯特奶酪；最后，最最美味的甜点，你绝对没尝过这么好的！”
foreignization: “比尔，尝尝这个兔子派，”那位年轻的绅士叫道，展示出一个巨大的馅饼；“如此娇嫩的生物，有着如此柔软的四肢，比尔，骨头在嘴里几乎都化了，根本不需要剔骨；半磅七先令六便士的绿茶，如此之浓烈，如果你用沸水冲泡，差点儿就把茶壶盖给吹飞了；一磅半的软糖，黑人完全没有加工过，直到达到了如此高的品质——哦不！两个半夸脱的麸皮面包；一磅最好的新鲜面包；一块双格洛斯特奶酪；最后，最上等的那种你从未品尝过的！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Sitch a rabbit pie, Bill,” exclaimed that young gentleman, disclosing to view a huge pasty; “sitch delicate creeturs, with sitch tender limbs, Bill, that the wery bones melt in your mouth, and there’s no occasion to pick ’em; half a pound of seven and six-penny green, so precious strong that if you mix it with biling water, it’ll go nigh to blow the lid of the tea-pot off; a pound and a half of moist sugar that the niggers didn’t work at all at, afore they got it up to sitch a pitch of goodness,—oh no! Two half-quartern brans; pound of best fresh; piece of double Glo’ster; and, to wind up all, some of the richest sort you ever lushed!”
“比尔，来做个兔子派吧，”那位年轻先生喊道，露出一个大馅饼，“这些小家伙，肉嫩得骨头都能在嘴里化掉，根本不用吐骨头；还有半磅七先令六便士的绿茶，那茶可劲儿浓了，一冲开水就能差点把茶壶盖给顶起来；一磅半的糖浆，黑奴们都没怎么费劲就弄到这么好——可不是嘛！两个麦麸面包，一磅上好的新鲜面包，一块双层格洛斯特奶酪；最后，最最美味的甜点，你绝对没尝过这么好的！”

“Sitch a rabbit pie, Bill,” exclaimed that young gentleman, disclosing to view a huge pasty; “sitch delicate creeturs, with sitch tender limbs, Bill, that the wery bones melt in your mouth, and there’s no occasion to pick ’em; half a pound of seven and six-penny green, so precious strong that if you mix it with biling water, it’ll go nigh to blow the lid of the tea-pot off; a pound and a half of moist sugar that the niggers didn’t work at all at, afore they got it up to sitch a pitch of goodness,—oh no! Two half-quartern brans; pound of best fresh; piece of double Glo’ster; and, to wind up all, some of the richest sort you ever lushed!”
“比尔，尝尝这个兔子派，”那位年轻的绅士叫道，展示出一个巨大的馅饼；“如此娇嫩的生物，有着如此柔软的四肢，比尔，骨头在嘴里几乎都化了，根本不需要剔骨；半磅七先令六便士的绿茶，如此之浓烈，如果你用沸水冲泡，差点儿就把茶壶盖给吹飞了；一磅半的软糖，黑人完全没有加工过，直到达到了如此高的品质——哦不！两个半夸脱的麸皮面包；一磅最好的新鲜面包；一块双格洛斯特奶酪；最后，最上等的那种你从未品尝过的！”

source: In pursuance of this cautious plan, Mr. Claypole went on, without halting, until he arrived at the Angel at Islington, where he wisely judged, from the crowd of passengers and numbers of vehicles, that London began in earnest. Just pausing to observe which appeared the most crowded streets, and consequently the most to be avoided, he crossed into Saint John’s Road, and was soon deep in the obscurity of the intricate and dirty ways, which, lying between Gray’s Inn Lane and Smithfield, render that part of the town one of the lowest and worst that improvement has left in the midst of London.
domestication: 按照这个谨慎的计划，克莱波尔先生一路没停，直到来到伊斯灵顿的天使旅馆。他从这里的人流车马中判断，伦敦算是真正开始了。他稍作停留，观察了下哪些街道最拥挤，自然也最该避开，然后拐进了圣约翰路，很快就钻进了格雷旅店巷和史密斯菲尔德之间那些错综复杂、又脏又乱的小道里。这一带是伦敦最底层、最糟糕的地方之一，改造也没能改变这里的面貌。
foreignization: 根据这个谨慎的计划，克莱普尔先生不停地走着，直到他到达伊斯灵顿的天使旅馆，在那里他明智地判断，从众多的乘客和车辆来看，伦敦才真正开始。他只是稍作停留，观察哪条街道显得最拥挤，因此是最需要避免的，然后他穿过到圣约翰路，并很快就深入到了那些复杂而肮脏的小巷中，这些小巷位于格雷旅店巷和史密斯菲尔德之间，使这一带成为改良后仍留在伦敦中心的最低劣和最糟糕的地方之一。

source: With these words, the old gentleman gave his hand to Mrs. Maylie, and escorted her into the supper-room. Mr. Losberne followed, leading Rose; and the council was, for the present, effectually broken up.
domestication: 老先生说完这话，就伸出手来扶着梅利太太进了餐室。洛斯伯恩先生跟着后面，牵着罗斯；这样一来，会议也就暂时散了。
foreignization: 说着，老先生把手伸给了梅利太太，并陪她进入了餐室。洛斯伯恩先生跟着，带着罗斯；于是会议暂时有效地解散了。

source: After a great deal of haggling and squabbling, Fagin beat down the amount of the required advance from five pounds to three pounds four and sixpence: protesting with many solemn asseverations that that would only leave him eighteen-pence to keep house with; Mr. Sikes sullenly remarking that if he couldn’t get any more he must accompany him home; with the Dodger and Master Bates put the eatables in the cupboard. The Jew then, taking leave of his affectionate friend, returned homeward, attended by Nancy and the boys: Mr. Sikes, meanwhile, flinging himself on the bed, and composing himself to sleep away the time until the young lady’s return.
domestication: 一番讨价还价，磨磨蹭蹭之后，费金终于把预付款从五镑压到了三镑四先令六便士。费金信誓旦旦地说，这下他只剩十八便士过日子了；赛克斯则阴沉着脸说，如果再拿不到钱，他就得逼着费金跟他回家。这时，杰克和查理把吃的放进了橱柜。费金随后告别了这位“亲密”的朋友，由南希和两个小子陪着回了家；而赛克斯则一头栽倒在床上，打算睡到那位小姐回来再说。
foreignization: 经过一番激烈的讨价还价和争吵之后，费金把预付款从五英镑压低到三英镑四先令六便士：他以许多庄严的誓言抗议说，这只会让他剩下十八便士来维持家用；西克斯先生阴沉地说道，如果他拿不到更多的钱，他就必须陪他回家；与此同时，机灵鬼和贝茨把食物放进了橱柜。然后，这个犹太人向他的亲密朋友告别，由南希和孩子们陪同回家：西克斯先生则扑倒在床上，准备睡到那位年轻女士回来的时候。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

After a great deal of haggling and squabbling, Fagin beat down the amount of the required advance from five pounds to three pounds four and sixpence: protesting with many solemn asseverations that that would only leave him eighteen-pence to keep house with; Mr. Sikes sullenly remarking that if he couldn’t get any more he must accompany him home; with the Dodger and Master Bates put the eatables in the cupboard. The Jew then, taking leave of his affectionate friend, returned homeward, attended by Nancy and the boys: Mr. Sikes, meanwhile, flinging himself on the bed, and composing himself to sleep away the time until the young lady’s return.

source: As she uttered these words, and indicated, with her hand, the direction in which she wished them to proceed, the countryman looked round, and roughly asking what they took up the whole pavement for, passed on.
domestication: 她说完这话，手一挥，指了指她想让他们走的方向。这时，乡下人回头一看，粗声粗气地问他们为啥占了整条路，然后就走了。
foreignization: 她说着这些话，并用手指示了她希望他们前进的方向，这时，乡下人转过身来，粗鲁地问他们为什么要占整条人行道，然后走了过去。

messages_domestication.content:

她说完这话，手一挥，指了指她想让他们走的方向。这时，乡下人回头一看，粗声粗气地问他们为啥占了整条路，然后就走了。

source: Mr. Blathers made a bow. Being desired to sit down, he put his hat on the floor, and taking a chair, motioned to Duff to do the same. The latter gentleman, who did not appear quite so much accustomed to good society, or quite so much at his ease in it—one of the two—seated himself, after undergoing several muscular affections of the limbs, and the head of his stick into his mouth, with some embarrassment.
domestication: 布拉瑟先生鞠了一躬。被请坐下后，他把帽子放在地上，自己找了个椅子坐下，还示意达夫也这么做。后者似乎不太习惯上流社会的场合，或者是在这种场合下不太自在——可能是这两点之一——他先是手脚不知往哪儿放，还把拐杖头塞进了嘴里，显得有些手足无措，最终才坐了下来。
foreignization: 布菜瑟先生鞠了一躬。被邀请坐下后，他把帽子放在地板上，拿起一把椅子，示意达夫也这么做。后者看来不太习惯上流社会的场合，或者在其中不太自在——两者之一——在经历了几番肢体上的肌肉活动，以及手杖的头部塞进嘴里之后，显得有些尴尬地坐了下来。

The latter gentleman, who did not appear quite so much accustomed to good society, or quite so much at his ease in it—one of the two—seated himself, after undergoing several muscular affections of the limbs, and the head of his stick into his mouth, with some embarrassment.

source: Fagin was about to translate these mysterious expressions into the vulgar tongue; and, being interpreted, Mr. Bolter would have been informed that they represented that combination of words, “transportation for life,” when the dialogue was cut short by the entry of Master Bates, with his hands in his breeches-pockets, and his face twisted into a look of semi-comical woe.
domestication: 费金正准备把这些神秘的词句翻译成大白话；要是翻译出来，波尔特先生就会知道这些词句的意思是“终身流放”。这时，巴茨少爷进来了，两手插在裤子口袋里，脸上一副半开玩笑半愁苦的表情，打断了他们的对话。
foreignization: 费金正要把这些神秘的表达翻译成通俗的语言；而且，如果被解释的话，博尔特先生将会被告知它们代表了“终身流放”这个词组，这时对话被贝茨少爷的进入打断了，他把手插在马裤口袋里，脸上扭曲出一种半滑稽的悲哀表情。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Fagin was about to translate these mysterious expressions into the vulgar tongue; and, being interpreted, Mr. Bolter would have been informed that they represented that combination of words, “transportation for life,” when the dialogue was cut short by the entry of Master Bates, with his hands in his breeches-pockets, and his face twisted into a look of semi-comical woe.
费金正准备把这些神秘的词句翻译成大白话；要是翻译出来，波尔特先生就会知道这些词句的意思是“终身流放”。这时，巴茨少爷进来了，两手插在裤子口袋里，脸上一副半开玩笑半愁苦的表情，打断了他们的对话。

费金正要把这些神秘的表达翻译成通俗的语言；而且，如果被解释的话，博尔特先生将会被告知它们代表了“终身流放”这个词组，这时对话被贝茨少爷的进入打断了，他把手插在马裤口袋里，脸上扭曲出一种半滑稽的悲哀表情。

source: As I purpose to show in the sequel whether the white waistcoated gentleman was right or not, I should perhaps mar the interest of this narrative (supposing it to possess any at all), if I ventured to hint just yet, whether the life of Oliver Twist had this violent termination or no.
domestication: 我打算在下文中说明那位穿白背心的先生说得对不对，要是我现在就透露奥利弗·特威斯特的人生是否以这种方式戛然而止，恐怕会破坏这个故事的悬念（当然，这故事要是还有点吸引力的话）。
foreignization: 正如我打算在下文中表明那位穿白色背心的先生是否正确，如果我现在就暗示奥利弗·特威斯特的生活是否有如此暴力的结局，也许会破坏这个叙述的兴趣（假设它有任何兴趣的话）。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As I purpose to show in the sequel whether the white waistcoated gentleman was right or not, I should perhaps mar the interest of this narrative (supposing it to possess any at all), if I ventured to hint just yet, whether the life of Oliver Twist had this violent termination or no.
我打算在下文中说明那位穿白背心的先生说得对不对，要是我现在就透露奥利弗·特威斯特的人生是否以这种方式戛然而止，恐怕会破坏这个故事的悬念（当然，这故事要是还有点吸引力的话）。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As I purpose to show in the sequel whether the white waistcoated gentleman was right or not, I should perhaps mar the interest of this narrative (supposing it to possess any at all), if I ventured to hint just yet, whether the life of Oliver Twist had this violent termination or no.
正如我打算在下文中表明那位穿白色背心的先生是否正确，如果我现在就暗示奥利弗·特威斯特的生活是否有如此暴力的结局，也许会破坏这个叙述的兴趣（假设它有任何兴趣的话）。

source: A minute ago, the boy had looked the quiet child, mild, dejected creature that harsh treatment had made him. But his spirit was roused at last; the cruel insult to his dead mother had set his blood on fire. His breast heaved; his attitude was erect; his eye bright and vivid; his whole person changed, as he stood glaring over the cowardly tormentor who now lay crouching at his feet; and defied him with an energy he had never known before.
domestication: 刚才，这男孩还像是个温顺的孩子，一个因长期受虐而变得消沉的小生命。可现在，他终于被激怒了；母亲死后遭受的残酷侮辱点燃了他的怒火。他胸膛起伏，挺直了身子，眼中闪烁着光芒；整个人仿佛焕然一新。他站在那儿，瞪着那个现在蜷缩在他脚下的懦弱的折磨者，用从未有过的勇气挑战着他。
foreignization: 一分钟前，这个男孩看起来还是那个安静的孩子，一个温和、沮丧的生物，严酷的对待让他变得如此。但他的精神终于被激发了；对已故母亲的残忍侮辱激起了他的怒火。他的胸膛起伏；他的姿态挺拔；他的眼睛明亮而生动；他的整个身体都发生了变化，他站在那里，瞪着现在蜷缩在他脚下的懦弱折磨者，并以他从未有过的力量向他挑衅。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A minute ago, the boy had looked the quiet child, mild, dejected creature that harsh treatment had made him. But his spirit was roused at last; the cruel insult to his dead mother had set his blood on fire. His breast heaved; his attitude was erect; his eye bright and vivid; his whole person changed, as he stood glaring over the cowardly tormentor who now lay crouching at his feet; and defied him with an energy he had never known before.

But his spirit was roused at last; the cruel insult to his dead mother had set his blood on fire. His breast heaved; his attitude was erect; his eye bright and vivid; his whole person changed, as he stood glaring over the cowardly tormentor who now lay crouching at his feet; and defied him with an energy he had never known before.
一分钟前，这个男孩看起来还是那个安静的孩子，一个温和、沮丧的生物，严酷的对待让他变得如此。但他的精神终于被激发了；对已故母亲的残忍侮辱激起了他的怒火。他的胸膛起伏；他的姿态挺拔；他的眼睛明亮而生动；他的整个身体都发生了变化，他站在那里，瞪着现在蜷缩在他脚下的懦弱折磨者，并以他从未有过的力量向他挑衅。

source: It chanced one morning, while Oliver’s affairs were in this auspicious and comfortable state, that Mr. Gamfield, chimney-sweep, went his way down the High Street, deeply cogitating in his mind his ways and means of paying certain arrears of rent, for which his landlord had become rather pressing. Mr. Gamfield’s most sanguine estimate of his finances could not raise them within full five pounds of the desired amount; and, in a species of arithmetical desperation, he was alternately cudgelling his brains and his donkey, when passing the workhouse, his eyes encountered the bill on the gate.
domestication: 有那么一天早晨，奥利弗的境况正是一片大好，春风得意。恰巧这当儿，扫烟囱的甘菲尔德先生走在大街上，满脑子盘算着怎么凑齐房租欠款，房东最近催得紧。甘菲尔德先生算来算去，最乐观的估计也还差整整五英镑。他急得团团转，一会儿拍脑袋，一会儿打毛驴，就在这时，路过济贫院，突然瞥见大门上贴着告示。
foreignization: 碰巧有一天天早晨，奥利弗的事情正处于这样一种吉祥而舒适的状态，甘菲尔德烟囱清洁工在高街上行走，深思熟虑着如何支付房东已变得相当紧迫的某些租金欠款。甘菲尔德先生最乐观的财务估计也无法使资金达到所需数额还差整整五英镑；在一种算术上的绝望中，他交替地使劲思考和鞭打他的驴子，当路过济贫院时，他的目光遇到了大门上的告示。

Mr. Gamfield’s most sanguine estimate of his finances could not raise them within full five pounds of the desired amount; and, in a species of arithmetical desperation, he was alternately cudgelling his brains and his donkey, when passing the workhouse, his eyes encountered the bill on the gate.

source: She was busily engaged in the little offices of the table. Chancing to raise her eyes as the elder lady was regarding her, she playfully put back her hair, which was simply braided on her forehead; and threw into her beaming look, such an expression of affection and artless loveliness, that blessed spirits might have smiled to look upon her.
domestication: 她正忙着张罗桌上的事情。恰好抬头看见老太太在打量她，便调皮地拨了拨额前简单的发辫；她那充满爱意和纯真美好的眼神，连天上的仙人看了也会露出笑容。
foreignization: 她正忙着在桌旁做一些小事。偶然间抬眼看到那位年长的女士正在注视她，她调皮地把简单编在额头上的头发往后一拨；并在她闪亮的目光中投入了如此一种充满爱意和天真可爱的表情，以至于天上的精灵看了也会微笑。

source: The Dodger nodded in the affirmative, and, shading the flame of the candle with his hand, gave Charley Bates a private intimation, in dumb show, that he had better not be funny just then. Having performed this friendly office, he fixed his eyes on the Jew’s face, and awaited his directions.
domestication: 躲格点了点头，用手遮住烛光，给查理·贝茨使了个眼色，意思是让他别在这时候搞笑。做完这贴心的事后，他盯着犹太人的脸，等着听吩咐。
foreignization: 多德杰点头表示同意，然后用手遮住蜡烛的火焰，给查利·贝茨做了个哑剧式的暗示，表示他最好这时候不要搞笑。完成了这项友好举动后，他盯着犹太人的脸，等待他的指示。

source: Noah was a charity-boy, but not a workhouse orphan. No chance-child was he, for he could trace his genealogy all the way back to his parents, who lived hard by; his mother being a washerwoman, and his father a drunken soldier, discharged with a wooden leg, and a diurnal pension of twopence-halfpenny and an unstateable fraction. The shop-boys in the neighbourhood had long been in the habit of branding Noah in the public streets, with the ignominious epithets of “leathers,” “charity,” and the like; and Noah had borne them without reply. But, now that fortune had cast in his way a nameless orphan, at whom even the meanest could point the finger of scorn, he retorted on him with interest. This affords charming food for contemplation. It shows us what a beautiful thing human nature may be made to be; and how impartially the same amiable qualities are developed in the finest lord and the dirtiest charity-boy.
domestication: 诺亚是个慈善孩子，但不是济贫院的孤儿。他可不是个无父无母的苦命孩子，因为他的家谱可以追溯到还在附近的父母。他妈妈是个洗衣妇，爸爸则是个酗酒的退伍军人，一条木腿，每天领着两便士半的退休金和一些说不出口的小恩小惠。附近店铺的孩子们长期以来都在大街上给诺亚贴上“穷皮”、“慈善”的标签，诺亚一直忍气吞声。可如今，命运让他遇到了一个连名字都没有的孤儿，连最底层的人都能对他指指点点，诺亚便开始反过来欺负这孩子，而且变本加厉。这事儿真让人感慨万千。它告诉我们，人性可以被塑造得多么美好；无论是高贵的勋爵，还是最脏的慈善孩子，身上都能展现出同样迷人的品质。
foreignization: 诺亚是个慈善生，但不是济贫院的孤儿。他并非命中注定无依无靠的孩子，因为他可以追溯自己的家谱一直回到他的父母，他们就住在附近；他的母亲是个洗衣妇，父亲则是个酗酒的士兵，因伤退伍，装了一条木腿，每天领取两便士半和一个无法描述的零头作为退休金。附近店铺的小厮们长期以来习惯在公共街道上给诺亚贴上“皮革”，“慈善”等耻辱性的绰号，而诺亚对此并未作出回应。但是，现在命运让他遇到了一个无名的孤儿，连最卑微的人都可以对他投以鄙视的目光，于是诺亚便对他进行了报复，并且变本加厉。这提供了一个令人愉快的思考题材。它向我们展示了人性可以被塑造得多么美好；以及同样的可亲品质如何公正地在最显赫的贵族和最肮脏的慈善生身上得到发展。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Noah was a charity-boy, but not a workhouse orphan. No chance-child was he, for he could trace his genealogy all the way back to his parents, who lived hard by; his mother being a washerwoman, and his father a drunken soldier, discharged with a wooden leg, and a diurnal pension of twopence-halfpenny and an unstateable fraction. The shop-boys in the neighbourhood had long been in the habit of branding Noah in the public streets, with the ignominious epithets of “leathers,” “charity,” and the like; and Noah had borne them without reply. But, now that fortune had cast in his way a nameless orphan, at whom even the meanest could point the finger of scorn, he retorted on him with interest. This affords charming food for contemplation. It shows us what a beautiful thing human nature may be made to be; and how impartially the same amiable qualities are developed in the finest lord and the dirtiest charity-boy.

source: Not having a very clearly defined notion of what a live board was, Oliver was rather astounded by this intelligence, and was not quite certain whether he ought to laugh or cry. He had no time to think about the matter, however; for Mr. Bumble gave him a tap on the head, with his cane, to wake him up: and another on the back to make him lively: and bidding him to follow, conducted him into a large white-washed room, where eight or ten fat gentlemen were sitting round a table. At the top of the table, seated in an arm-chair rather higher than the rest, was a particularly fat gentleman with a very round, red face.
domestication: 奥利弗对“寄养所”这个概念不太清楚，所以听到这消息时，他相当吃惊，一时半会儿不知道该笑还是该哭。可他没时间细想，因为邦布尔先生用拐杖在他头上轻轻敲了一下，把他从愣神中唤醒；又在他背上敲了一下，让他振作精神。邦布尔先生吩咐奥利弗跟上，带他进了一个大大的刷成白色的房间。房间里围着桌子坐着八九位胖绅士。桌子的主位上，坐着一位特别胖的绅士，坐在一把比其他椅子稍高的扶手椅里，圆圆的脸红扑扑的。
foreignization: 对活板究竟是怎么回事没有一个非常明确的概念，奥利弗对这一消息感到相当惊讶，不确定自己该笑还是该哭。他没有时间考虑这个问题，因为邦布尔先生用拐杖在他头上轻轻敲了一下，让他清醒过来：又在他背上敲了一下，让他精神振奋起来：然后吩咐他跟上，把他带进了一个大白粉刷的房间，那里有八九位胖绅士正围坐在一张桌子旁。在桌子的上首，坐在一把比其他椅子稍高的扶手椅里的是一个特别胖的绅士，长着一张非常圆而红润的脸。

source: Talking all the way, he followed Mr. Giles upstairs; and while he is going upstairs, the reader may be informed, that Mr. Losberne, a surgeon in the neighbourhood, known through a circuit of ten miles round as “the doctor,” had grown fat, more from good-humour than from good living: and was as kind and hearty, and withal as eccentric an old bachelor, as will be found in five times that space, by any explorer alive.
domestication: 一路上说个不停，他跟着吉尔斯先生上了楼。读者可能还不知道，洛斯伯恩先生是附近的一位外科医生，在方圆十里地内大家都叫他“医生”。他发福了，主要是因为心宽体胖，并不是吃得好。他是个和蔼可亲、热情洋溢的老光棍，这种人你就算走遍五十里路也难找一个。
foreignization: 一路 talking，他跟着吉尔斯先生上楼；而在他上楼的时候，读者可以了解到，洛斯伯恩先生，一位在附近行医、方圆十里以内人称“医生”的人士，已经发福了，这更多是因为他的好脾气而不是因为生活优越：他是一位和蔼可亲、心地善良的老人，同时也是一个怪癖的老单身汉，在五倍于此的地方也难找到像他这样的人，无论哪个探险家都难以发现。

Talking all the way, he followed Mr. Giles upstairs; and while he is going upstairs, the reader may be informed, that Mr. Losberne, a surgeon in the neighbourhood, known through a circuit of ten miles round as “the doctor,” had grown fat, more from good-humour than from good living: and was as kind and hearty, and withal as eccentric an old bachelor, as will be found in five times that space, by any explorer alive.
一路 talking，他跟着吉尔斯先生上楼；而在他上楼的时候，读者可以了解到，洛斯伯恩先生，一位在附近行医、方圆十里以内人称“医生”的人士，已经发福了，这更多是因为他的好脾气而不是因为生活优越：他是一位和蔼可亲、心地善良的老人，同时也是一个怪癖的老单身汉，在五倍于此的地方也难找到像他这样的人，无论哪个探险家都难以发现。

source: The two old crones, chiming in together, began pouring out many piteous lamentations that the poor dear was too far gone to know her best friends; and were uttering sundry protestations that they would never leave her, when the superior pushed them from the room, closed the door, and returned to the bedside. On being excluded, the old ladies changed their tone, and cried through the keyhole that old Sally was drunk; which, indeed, was not unlikely; since, in addition to a moderate dose of opium prescribed by the apothecary, she was labouring under the effects of a final taste of gin-and-water which had been privily administered, in the openness of their hearts, by the worthy old ladies themselves.
domestication: 两个老太婆一唱一和，开始大倒苦水，说可怜的宝贝儿已经认不出最好的朋友了；还信誓旦旦地说她们决不会离开她。这时，修道院院长把她们推出了门外，关上房门，又回到床边。被赶出去后，这两个老太太立刻变了腔调，对着门缝大喊老萨莉喝醉了。这事儿还真有可能，因为除了药剂师开的适量鸦片外，她还偷偷地喝了两位好心的老太太给她的最后一口杜松子酒加水。
foreignization: 那两个老妇人异口同声地开始倾诉许多可怜的哀叹，说可怜的亲爱的已经病入膏肓，不认识她最好的朋友了；并且发誓她们永远不会离开她，这时院长把她们推出了房间，关上门，回到了床边。被拒之门外后，老妇人们改变了语气，对着锁眼大喊老萨莉喝醉了；这确实很有可能；因为除了药剂师开的适量鸦片外，她还在经受最后尝一口由这两位可敬的老妇人出于一片好心私下里给她的杜松子水的影响。

source: The man went to the door, and returned to announce that all was quiet. The two robbers issued forth with Oliver between them. Barney, having made all fast, rolled himself up as before, and was soon asleep again.
domestication: 那人去门口看了看，回来报告说外面一片安静。两个强盗便带着奥利弗走了出去。巴尼锁好门窗，又像之前一样裹成一团，很快就又睡着了。
foreignization: 那人走到门口，回来宣布一切都很安静。两个强盗带着奥利弗走了出来。巴尼在确保一切安全后，像之前一样把自己卷起来，很快就又睡着了。

source: “Three Cripples,” repeated Noah, “and a very good sign too. Now, then! Keep close at my heels, and come along.” With these injunctions, he pushed the rattling door with his shoulder, and entered the house, followed by his companion.

source: Noah Claypole’s mind might have been at ease after this assurance, but his body certainly was not; for he shuffled and writhed about, into various uncouth positions: eyeing his new friend meanwhile with mingled fear and suspicion.
domestication: 诺亚·克雷波尔听了这话，心里或许安定了些，可身体却一点也不消停；他扭来扭去，摆出各种古怪的姿势，眼睛还时不时地盯着新朋友，满是惧怕和猜疑。
foreignization: 诺亚·克劳普尔的脑海在得到这个保证后或许安心了，但他的身体肯定没有；因为他扭动着身子，摆出各种古怪的姿势：同时用混杂着恐惧和怀疑的眼神打量着他的新朋友。

source: I wish some well-fed philosopher, whose meat and drink turn to gall within him; whose blood is ice, whose heart is iron; could have seen Oliver Twist clutching at the dainty viands that the dog had neglected. I wish he could have witnessed the horrible avidity with which Oliver tore the bits asunder with all the ferocity of famine. There is only one thing I should like better; and that would be to see the Philosopher making the same sort of meal himself, with the same relish.
domestication: 真希望那些吃得脑满肠肥的哲学家，他们的吃喝在肚子里变成了苦胆，他们的血冷得像冰，心硬得像铁，能够看到奥利弗·特威斯特抓起那只狗没吃的东西。真希望他们能亲眼看看奥利弗饿得两眼发直，撕扯着那点儿残羹冷炙，狼吞虎咽的样子。要说还有比这更让人解气的事，那就是看到这些哲学家自己也这样大快朵颐，吃得津津有味。
foreignization: 我希望某个养尊处优的哲学家，他的肉食和饮料在他体内变成胆汁；他的血是冰，他的心是铁；能够看到奥利弗·退斯特抓住那只狗所忽略的精致食品。我希望他能目睹奥利弗以那种可怕的贪婪撕扯食物，带着饥饿的野性。只有一件事我更想看到；那就是看到这位哲学家自己也以同样的方式享用这顿饭，同样的津津有味。

There is only one thing I should like better; and that would be to see the Philosopher making the same sort of meal himself, with the same relish.

source: “Well,” rejoined Harry Maylie, smiling, “you can do as you like. Let him go on with the luggage, if you wish it, and do you follow with us. Only first exchange that nightcap for some more appropriate covering, or we shall be taken for madmen.”
domestication: 哈里·梅利笑了笑，说道：“好啦，随你的便。要是你愿意，就让他先带着行李走，你跟我们随后。不过，先把那顶睡帽换个像样的帽子吧，不然咱们会被当疯子的。”
foreignization: “好啦，”哈利·梅利微笑着接话说，“你爱怎么就怎么吧。如果你愿意，就让他继续去搬行李，而你跟我们一起来。不过首先把那顶睡帽换成更适合的帽子，不然我们会被人当作疯子的。”

“Well,” rejoined Harry Maylie, smiling, “you can do as you like. Let him go on with the luggage, if you wish it, and do you follow with us. Only first exchange that nightcap for some more appropriate covering, or we shall be taken for madmen.”
哈里·梅利笑了笑，说道：“好啦，随你的便。要是你愿意，就让他先带着行李走，你跟我们随后。不过，先把那顶睡帽换个像样的帽子吧，不然咱们会被当疯子的。”

“Well,” rejoined Harry Maylie, smiling, “you can do as you like. Let him go on with the luggage, if you wish it, and do you follow with us. Only first exchange that nightcap for some more appropriate covering, or we shall be taken for madmen.”
“好啦，”哈利·梅利微笑着接话说，“你爱怎么就怎么吧。如果你愿意，就让他继续去搬行李，而你跟我们一起来。不过首先把那顶睡帽换成更适合的帽子，不然我们会被人当作疯子的。”

source: It happened that about this time, Mr. Giles, Brittles, and the tinker, were recruiting themselves, after the fatigues and terrors of the night, with tea and sundries, in the kitchen. Not that it was Mr. Giles’s habit to admit to too great familiarity the humbler servants: towards whom it was rather his wont to deport himself with a lofty affability, which, while it gratified, could not fail to remind them of his superior position in society. But, death, fires, and burglary, make all men equals; so Mr. Giles sat with his legs stretched out before the kitchen fender, leaning his left arm on the table, while, with his right, he illustrated a circumstantial and minute account of the robbery, to which his hearers (but especially the cook and housemaid, who were of the party) listened with breathless interest.
domestication: 恰好这个时候，吉尔斯先生、布里特尔斯和那个补锅匠正坐在厨房里，一边喝茶吃点心，一边恢复着前一晚的疲劳和惊吓。吉尔斯先生平时可不会跟下人们走得太近，对这些地位低下的仆人，他总是摆出一副高高在上的姿态，既让人感到亲切，又时刻不忘提醒他们自己的社会地位比他们高。可是，死亡、火灾和盗窃，这些事情能把所有人都拉到同一个水平线上。所以这会儿，吉尔斯先生也大大方方地伸直了腿，靠在壁炉边的脚凳上，左手撑在桌子上，右手比划着，详细地讲述着那场盗窃案的来龙去脉。听众们个个听得入了迷，特别是厨娘和女佣，更是屏住呼吸，生怕漏掉一个细节。
foreignization: 碰巧在这时，吉尔斯先生、布里特尔斯和补锅匠正在厨房里用茶和一些小吃来恢复他们在那个恐怖夜晚的体力。并不是说吉尔斯先生习惯于与低下的仆人们过于亲密：他对他们更多的是以一种高高在上的亲切态度相处，这种态度虽然让他们感到满意，但也不断提醒着他们他在社会中的优越地位。但是，死亡、火灾和盗窃使得所有人平等；因此，吉尔斯先生伸直双腿坐在厨房的炉挡前，左臂撑在桌上，而用右手指画着详细地叙述了那场盗窃的情况，听众们（特别是参加进来的厨师和女仆）都屏息凝神地听着。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It happened that about this time, Mr. Giles, Brittles, and the tinker, were recruiting themselves, after the fatigues and terrors of the night, with tea and sundries, in the kitchen. Not that it was Mr. Giles’s habit to admit to too great familiarity the humbler servants: towards whom it was rather his wont to deport himself with a lofty affability, which, while it gratified, could not fail to remind them of his superior position in society. But, death, fires, and burglary, make all men equals; so Mr. Giles sat with his legs stretched out before the kitchen fender, leaning his left arm on the table, while, with his right, he illustrated a circumstantial and minute account of the robbery, to which his hearers (but especially the cook and housemaid, who were of the party) listened with breathless interest.

source: During the silence, the Jew looked restlessly about the room, as if to assure himself that there were no appearances of Sikes having covertly returned. Apparently satisfied with his inspection, he coughed twice or thrice, and made as many efforts to open a conversation; but the girl heeded him no more than if he had been made of stone. At length he made another attempt; and rubbing his hands together, said, in his most conciliatory tone,
domestication: 沉默中，这犹太人不安地在房间里四处张望，像是要确认赛克斯没有偷偷回来。看了一圈，似乎觉得没什么问题，他就咳嗽了两声，又试了几次想开口说话；但那女孩就像他是一块石头似的，根本没理他。最后，他又试着说了一次，搓着手，用最和气的语气说道：
foreignization: 在这沉默期间，那个犹太人不安地环顾着房间，仿佛要确保西克斯没有偷偷地回来。显然对他的检查感到满意后，他咳嗽了两三声，并做了同样多次的努力试图打开话题；但那女孩对他毫不理会，就好像他是石头做的似的。最后他又做了一次尝试；搓着手，用他最和解的语气说道，

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

During the silence, the Jew looked restlessly about the room, as if to assure himself that there were no appearances of Sikes having covertly returned. Apparently satisfied with his inspection, he coughed twice or thrice, and made as many efforts to open a conversation; but the girl heeded him no more than if he had been made of stone. At length he made another attempt; and rubbing his hands together, said, in his most conciliatory tone,
沉默中，这犹太人不安地在房间里四处张望，像是要确认赛克斯没有偷偷回来。看了一圈，似乎觉得没什么问题，他就咳嗽了两声，又试了几次想开口说话；但那女孩就像他是一块石头似的，根本没理他。最后，他又试着说了一次，搓着手，用最和气的语气说道：

source: “Look here! do you see this! Isn’t it a most wonderful and extraordinary thing that I can’t call at a man’s house but I find a piece of this poor surgeon’s friend on the staircase? I’ve been lamed with orange-peel once, and I know orange-peel will be my death, or I’ll be content to eat my own head, sir!”
domestication: 你瞧！你看这！我到人家家里去，竟然在楼梯上发现了这位可怜外科医生朋友的遗物，这不是太离奇了吗？我以前就被橘子皮滑倒过一次，我知道这橘子皮早晚要了我的命，不然我情愿把自己的头吃掉！
foreignization: “你看！你看见这个了吗！难道不是一件最奇妙、最不可思议的事情吗，我一到人家的门口，就能在楼梯上找到这位可怜的外科医生朋友的一块东西？我曾经被橙皮绊倒过一次，我知道橙皮会要了我的命，否则我就甘愿吃掉自己的脑袋，先生！”

source: “You old women never believe anything but quack-doctors, and lying story-books,” growled Mr. Grimwig. “I knew it all along. Why didn’t you take my advice in the beginning; you would if he hadn’t had a fever, I suppose, eh? He was interesting, wasn’t he? Interesting! Bah!” And Mr. Grimwig poked the fire with a flourish.
domestication: 你们这些老太婆，只信那些江湖郎中和骗人的故事书，”格林威格先生嘟囔道。“我早料到了。一开始就该听我的，要不是他发了烧，你们肯定就听了，对吧？他还挺有意思的，是吧？有意思！哼！”说着，格林威格先生用力捅了捅火。
foreignization: “你们这些老太婆从来只相信江湖郎中和撒谎的故事书，”格里姆威格先生咕哝道。“我早知道了。为什么一开始不听我的建议；如果他没有发烧的话，你大概会听的吧，是吗？他很有趣，不是吗？有趣！呸！”格里姆威格先生挥舞着拨火棍戳了戳火。

“You old women never believe anything but quack-doctors, and lying story-books,” growled Mr. Grimwig. “I knew it all along. Why didn’t you take my advice in the beginning; you would if he hadn’t had a fever, I suppose, eh? He was interesting, wasn’t he? Interesting! Bah!” And Mr. Grimwig poked the fire with a flourish.

source: Still, the same quiet life went on at the little cottage, and the same cheerful serenity prevailed among its inmates. Oliver had long since grown stout and healthy; but health or sickness made no difference in his warm feelings of a great many people. He was still the same gentle, attached, affectionate creature that he had been when pain and suffering had wasted his strength, and when he was dependent for every slight attention, and comfort on those who tended him.
domestication: 小屋里的生活依旧平静如初，屋里的人也依然和乐融融。奥利弗早已经长得壮实健康了，但无论是身体好还是不好，他对许多人的深厚感情始终未变。他依然是那个温柔、依恋、充满爱意的孩子，就像当初病痛折磨得他虚弱不堪，完全依赖别人照料时一样。
foreignization: 然而，小屋里的生活依旧平静，屋子里的人们依然保持着同样的愉快和安宁。奥利弗早已变得健壮健康；但无论是健康还是疾病，都未曾改变他对许多人的深厚感情。他仍然是那个温柔、依恋、充满爱意的生物，就像在他因痛苦和折磨而体力衰竭时，以及他依赖照顾他的人给予每一点关怀和安慰时一样。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Still, the same quiet life went on at the little cottage, and the same cheerful serenity prevailed among its inmates. Oliver had long since grown stout and healthy; but health or sickness made no difference in his warm feelings of a great many people. He was still the same gentle, attached, affectionate creature that he had been when pain and suffering had wasted his strength, and when he was dependent for every slight attention, and comfort on those who tended him.
小屋里的生活依旧平静如初，屋里的人也依然和乐融融。奥利弗早已经长得壮实健康了，但无论是身体好还是不好，他对许多人的深厚感情始终未变。他依然是那个温柔、依恋、充满爱意的孩子，就像当初病痛折磨得他虚弱不堪，完全依赖别人照料时一样。

Still, the same quiet life went on at the little cottage, and the same cheerful serenity prevailed among its inmates. Oliver had long since grown stout and healthy; but health or sickness made no difference in his warm feelings of a great many people. He was still the same gentle, attached, affectionate creature that he had been when pain and suffering had wasted his strength, and when he was dependent for every slight attention, and comfort on those who tended him.
然而，小屋里的生活依旧平静，屋子里的人们依然保持着同样的愉快和安宁。奥利弗早已变得健壮健康；但无论是健康还是疾病，都未曾改变他对许多人的深厚感情。他仍然是那个温柔、依恋、充满爱意的生物，就像在他因痛苦和折磨而体力衰竭时，以及他依赖照顾他的人给予每一点关怀和安慰时一样。

source: The tears stood in Oliver’s eyes as he recalled the scene which was the beginning of so much happiness; and the gentleman turned his face away, and remained silent, for some minutes. Oliver thought he heard him sob, more than once; but he feared to interrupt him by any fresh remark—for he could well guess what his feelings were—and so stood apart, feigning to be occupied with his nosegay.
domestication: 奥利弗回忆起那一幕，那是幸福的起点，泪水涌上了他的眼眶；那位绅士转过脸去，好几分钟都没有说话。奥利弗觉得好像听到他抽泣了几声，但又不敢多嘴打扰——因为他能理解对方此刻的心情——于是站在一旁，假装在整理手里的花束。
foreignization: 奥利弗想起那个带来如此多幸福的场景时，眼泪涌上了他的眼眶；那位先生转过脸去，沉默了好几分钟。奥利弗觉得他不止一次听到了先生的抽泣声；但他害怕用任何新的评论打扰他——因为他完全可以猜到他的感受——于是站在一旁，假装在摆弄他的花束。

messages_domestication.content:

The tears stood in Oliver’s eyes as he recalled the scene which was the beginning of so much happiness; and the gentleman turned his face away, and remained silent, for some minutes. Oliver thought he heard him sob, more than once; but he feared to interrupt him by any fresh remark—for he could well guess what his feelings were—and so stood apart, feigning to be occupied with his nosegay.
奥利弗回忆起那一幕，那是幸福的起点，泪水涌上了他的眼眶；那位绅士转过脸去，好几分钟都没有说话。奥利弗觉得好像听到他抽泣了几声，但又不敢多嘴打扰——因为他能理解对方此刻的心情——于是站在一旁，假装在整理手里的花束。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The tears stood in Oliver’s eyes as he recalled the scene which was the beginning of so much happiness; and the gentleman turned his face away, and remained silent, for some minutes. Oliver thought he heard him sob, more than once; but he feared to interrupt him by any fresh remark—for he could well guess what his feelings were—and so stood apart, feigning to be occupied with his nosegay.
奥利弗想起那个带来如此多幸福的场景时，眼泪涌上了他的眼眶；那位先生转过脸去，沉默了好几分钟。奥利弗觉得他不止一次听到了先生的抽泣声；但他害怕用任何新的评论打扰他——因为他完全可以猜到他的感受——于是站在一旁，假装在摆弄他的花束。

source: Accordingly, with a clean white apron tied over her gown, and her curl-papers tucked up under a straw bonnet,—both articles of dress being provided from the Jew’s inexhaustible stock,—Miss Nancy prepared to issue forth on her errand.
domestication: 于是，南希小姐系上了一条洁白的围裙，戴上了草帽，卷发纸也塞进了帽子下面——这两样东西都是从犹太人的无尽存货中拿来的——便准备出门办事了。
foreignization: 于是，南希小姐系上一条干净的白围裙，把她卷发的纸塞进一顶草帽里——这两样都是从犹太人的取之不尽的存货中提供的——准备出门办事。

messages_domestication.content:

Accordingly, with a clean white apron tied over her gown, and her curl-papers tucked up under a straw bonnet,—both articles of dress being provided from the Jew’s inexhaustible stock,—Miss Nancy prepared to issue forth on her errand.
于是，南希小姐系上了一条洁白的围裙，戴上了草帽，卷发纸也塞进了帽子下面——这两样东西都是从犹太人的无尽存货中拿来的——便准备出门办事了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Accordingly, with a clean white apron tied over her gown, and her curl-papers tucked up under a straw bonnet,—both articles of dress being provided from the Jew’s inexhaustible stock,—Miss Nancy prepared to issue forth on her errand.
于是，南希小姐系上一条干净的白围裙，把她卷发的纸塞进一顶草帽里——这两样都是从犹太人的取之不尽的存货中提供的——准备出门办事。

source: “Promptness, indeed!” replied the beadle. “But what’s the consequence; what’s the ungrateful behaviour of these rebels, sir? Why, the husband sends back word that the medicine won’t suit his wife’s complaint, and so she shan’t take it—says she shan’t take it, sir! Good, strong, wholesome medicine, as was given with great success to two Irish labourers and a coal-heaver, only a week before—sent ’em for nothing, with a blackin’-bottle in,—and he sends back word that she shan’t take it, sir!”
domestication: “真是神速！”差役回答道，“可是先生，这些叛逆之徒后来怎么样了？有多忘恩负义啊！那丈夫回话说这药不合他老婆的病症，她不吃了——就是不吃！好好的药，强身健体的，一周前还免费给了两个爱尔兰劳工和一个运煤的，还送了个黑漆瓶，结果他就敢回话不让吃！真是岂有此理！”
foreignization: “真是及时！”执事回答道。“但是有什么后果；这些叛逆者的忘恩负义的行为是什么呢，先生？为什么，丈夫回话说这药不适合他妻子的病，所以她不吃了——说她不吃了，先生！好得很，强壮，健康的药，在一周前还成功地给了两个爱尔兰劳工和一个煤工——免费送给他们，还带了一个黑鞋油瓶子，——而他回话说她不吃了，先生！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Promptness, indeed!” replied the beadle. “But what’s the consequence; what’s the ungrateful behaviour of these rebels, sir? Why, the husband sends back word that the medicine won’t suit his wife’s complaint, and so she shan’t take it—says she shan’t take it, sir! Good, strong, wholesome medicine, as was given with great success to two Irish labourers and a coal-heaver, only a week before—sent ’em for nothing, with a blackin’-bottle in,—and he sends back word that she shan’t take it, sir!”
“真是神速！”差役回答道，“可是先生，这些叛逆之徒后来怎么样了？有多忘恩负义啊！那丈夫回话说这药不合他老婆的病症，她不吃了——就是不吃！好好的药，强身健体的，一周前还免费给了两个爱尔兰劳工和一个运煤的，还送了个黑漆瓶，结果他就敢回话不让吃！真是岂有此理！”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Promptness, indeed!” replied the beadle. “But what’s the consequence; what’s the ungrateful behaviour of these rebels, sir? Why, the husband sends back word that the medicine won’t suit his wife’s complaint, and so she shan’t take it—says she shan’t take it, sir! Good, strong, wholesome medicine, as was given with great success to two Irish labourers and a coal-heaver, only a week before—sent ’em for nothing, with a blackin’-bottle in,—and he sends back word that she shan’t take it, sir!”
“真是及时！”执事回答道。“但是有什么后果；这些叛逆者的忘恩负义的行为是什么呢，先生？为什么，丈夫回话说这药不适合他妻子的病，所以她不吃了——说她不吃了，先生！好得很，强壮，健康的药，在一周前还成功地给了两个爱尔兰劳工和一个煤工——免费送给他们，还带了一个黑鞋油瓶子，——而他回话说她不吃了，先生！”

source: After a time she arose, and with feeble and tottering steps ascended the street. The astonished listener remained motionless on his post for some minutes afterwards, and having ascertained, with many cautious glances round him, that he was again alone, crept slowly from his hiding-place, and returned, stealthily and in the shade of the wall, in the same manner as he had descended.
domestication: 过了一会儿，她起身了，迈着虚弱蹒跚的步子往街上走去。那吃惊的听众在原地愣了好几分钟，四处张望，确定四周无人后，才慢慢从藏身之处探出头来，像来时那样，贴着墙根，悄悄地原路返回。
foreignization: 过了一会儿，她站了起来，用虚弱蹒跚的步伐走上街道。惊讶的听众在原地呆立了几分钟后，在多次谨慎地环顾四周后，确认自己再次独处，便慢慢从藏身之处爬了出来，并像他下来时那样，沿着墙的阴影悄悄地返回。

source: Meanwhile, Oliver Twist, little dreaming that he was within so very short a distance of the merry old gentleman, was on his way to the book-stall. When he got into Clerkenwell, he accidently turned down a by-street which was not exactly in his way; but not discovering his mistake until he had got half-way down it, and knowing it must lead in the right direction, he did not think it worth while to turn back; and so marched on, as quickly as he could, with the books under his arm.
domestication: 与此同时，奥利弗·退斯特做梦也没想到，自己离那位快乐的老绅士竟如此之近，正往书摊的方向走去。到了克拉肯威尔，他无意中拐进了一条小街，这路其实并不顺路；但等他发现时已经走了一半了，心想这条路总归是通向目的地的，就懒得再回头，于是加快脚步，夹着书继续往前走。
foreignization: 与此同时，奥利弗·特威斯特做梦也没有想到自己离那位快乐的老先生如此之近，正往书店走去。当他走进克拉肯韦尔时，无意中拐进了一条旁街，那条路并不完全在他的必经之路上；但直到他走了一半才意识到自己的错误，而且知道这条路最终也会通往正确的方向，他觉得不值得回头；于是他就尽可能快地往前走，胳膊下夹着书。

与此同时，奥利弗·退斯特做梦也没想到，自己离那位快乐的老绅士竟如此之近，正往书摊的方向走去。到了克拉肯威尔，他无意中拐进了一条小街，这路其实并不顺路；但等他发现时已经走了一半了，心想这条路总归是通向目的地的，就懒得再回头，于是加快脚步，夹着书继续往前走。

source: The visitors stopped a long time. Spirits were produced, in consequence of one of the young ladies complaining of a coldness in her inside; and the conversation took a very convivial and improving turn. At length, Charley Bates expressed his opinion that it was time to pad the hoof. This, it occurred to Oliver, must be French for going out; for directly afterwards, the Dodger, and Charley, and the two young ladies, went away together, having been kindly furnished by the amiable old Jew with money to spend.
domestication: 客人待了好一会儿。因为一位小姐抱怨觉得肚子里冷，大家就喝了点酒壮壮胆；聊着聊着，气氛渐渐热闹起来，话题也变得既欢乐又有益。最后，查理·贝茨说该“出去走走了”。奥利弗琢磨着，“出去走走”应该是法语里的“出门”；果然，紧接着，杰克·道奇、查理和那两位年轻小姐一起出门了，临行前，那位慈眉善目的老犹太人还大方地给了他们钱花。
foreignization: 来访者停留了很长时间。由于一位年轻女士抱怨她感到内脏发冷，于是拿出了烈酒；谈话变得非常愉快和有益。最后，查理·贝茨表达了他认为是时候垫脚跟了。这使奥利弗想到，这一定是法语中的出门；因为紧接着，小偷、查理、两位年轻女士一起离开了，他们由那位和蔼的老犹太人友好地提供了花钱的钱。

This, it occurred to Oliver, must be French for going out; for directly afterwards, the Dodger, and Charley, and the two young ladies, went away together, having been kindly furnished by the amiable old Jew with money to spend.

source: The donkey was in a state of profound abstraction: wondering, probably, whether he was destined to be regaled with a cabbage-stalk or two when he had disposed of the two sacks of soot with which the little cart was laden; so, without noticing the word of command, he jogged onward.
domestication: 驴子心思重重，八成在盘算着卸完车上的两袋黑煤灰后，能不能美美地啃上几根菜梗。于是，它压根没听见主人的命令，自顾自地慢悠悠往前走。
foreignization: 驴子处于一种深刻的出神状态：可能在想，当他卸下小车上的两袋烟灰后，是否会被款待吃上一两根白菜梗；因此，他没有注意到命令，继续慢悠悠地向前走。

source: The building rang with a tremendous shout, and another, and another, and then it echoed loud groans, that gathered strength as they swelled out, like angry thunder. It was a peal of joy from the populace outside, greeting the news that he would die on Monday.
domestication: 大楼里响起了震天的呐喊声，一声接一声，随后是震耳欲聋的哀号，声音越来越大，仿佛愤怒的雷鸣。这是外面民众欢腾的欢呼，庆祝他将在周一被处决的消息。
foreignization: 大楼里响起了一声巨大的呐喊，接着又是另一声，再一声，然后是响亮的呻吟声，这些声音在膨胀中逐渐增强，如同愤怒的雷鸣。这是外面民众的欢呼声，迎接他将于周一被处死的消息。

source: “Thank you, sir,” said Mr. Giles. “Misses wished some ale to be given out, sir; and as I felt no ways inclined for my own little room, sir, and was disposed for company, I am taking mine among ’em here.”
domestication: “谢谢您，先生。”吉尔斯先生说道。“小姐想让人分发些麦芽酒，先生；再说我也不想待在自己那小屋子里，先生，反而想找个伴儿，所以就来这儿和大家一块儿喝了。”
foreignization: “谢谢你，先生，”吉尔斯先生说。“小姐希望分发一些麦芽酒，先生；因为我一点也不想去我自己的小房间，先生，而且我想和他们在一起，所以我在这里和他们一起喝。”

source: Not that, all this time, his mind was, for an instant, free from one oppressive overwhelming sense of the grave that opened at his feet; it was ever present to him, but in a vague and general way, and he could not fix his thoughts upon it. Thus, even while he trembled, and turned burning hot at the idea of speedy death, he fell to counting the iron spikes before him, and wondering how the head of one had been broken off, and whether they would mend it, or leave it as it was. Then, he thought of all the horrors of the gallows and the scaffold—and stopped to watch a man sprinkling the floor to cool it—and then went on to think again.
domestication: 倒不是说这段时间他有片刻忘记脚下随时可能张开的坟墓，这念头一直萦绕在他心头，但却是模糊而笼统的，他无法将心思定在上面。因此，即便他一想到即将面临的死亡就浑身发抖，满脸通红，却还能数起眼前的铁刺，心里琢磨着其中一根是怎么断的，会不会修好，还是就这样算了。接着，他又想起绞架和断头台的各种恐怖——却又停下来看一个男人洒水冷却地面——然后又继续胡思乱想。
foreignization: 并不是说，这段时间里，他心中有一刻摆脱了脚下打开的坟墓那令人压抑的重负感；这种感觉一直伴随着他，但却是模糊而笼统的，他无法将思绪集中在上面。因此，即使他在死亡临近的想法中颤抖，感到炽热难耐，他还是开始数起面前的铁钉，疑惑其中一个钉头是如何断裂的，他们是否会修理它，还是就这样留下。随后，他想到了绞刑架和断头台的所有恐怖——却停下来观看一个男人洒水以冷却地面——然后又继续思考。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Not that, all this time, his mind was, for an instant, free from one oppressive overwhelming sense of the grave that opened at his feet; it was ever present to him, but in a vague and general way, and he could not fix his thoughts upon it. Thus, even while he trembled, and turned burning hot at the idea of speedy death, he fell to counting the iron spikes before him, and wondering how the head of one had been broken off, and whether they would mend it, or leave it as it was. Then, he thought of all the horrors of the gallows and the scaffold—and stopped to watch a man sprinkling the floor to cool it—and then went on to think again.
倒不是说这段时间他有片刻忘记脚下随时可能张开的坟墓，这念头一直萦绕在他心头，但却是模糊而笼统的，他无法将心思定在上面。因此，即便他一想到即将面临的死亡就浑身发抖，满脸通红，却还能数起眼前的铁刺，心里琢磨着其中一根是怎么断的，会不会修好，还是就这样算了。接着，他又想起绞架和断头台的各种恐怖——却又停下来看一个男人洒水冷却地面——然后又继续胡思乱想。

source: “If you don’t take pocket-handkechers and watches,” said the Dodger, reducing his conversation to the level of Oliver’s capacity, “some other cove will; so that the coves that lose ’em will be all the worse, and you’ll be all the worse, too, and nobody half a ha’p’orth the better, except the chaps wot gets them—and you’ve just as good a right to them as they have.”
domestication: “你要不偷手帕和表，”机灵鬼把话说到小奥利弗能听懂的水平，“别人也会去偷。这样，丢了东西的人更倒霉，你也一样吃亏，谁也得不到半点好处，除了那些得手的小偷——你和他们有同样的权利拿这些东西。”
foreignization: “如果你不拿手帕和手表，”杰克说，把谈话降低到奥利弗能理解的水平，“其他家伙也会拿；这样一来，丢了东西的那些人会更糟，你也一样更糟，而没有人会因此好上半点，除了拿到这些东西的家伙——你对他们这些东西有和他们一样的权利。”

source: With these last words, the Dodger suffered himself to be led off by the collar; threatening, till he got into the yard, to make a parliamentary business of it; and then grinning in the officer’s face, with great glee and self-approval.
domestication: 说完这番话，机灵鬼儿任由别人拽着衣领把他带走；一路上还扬言要把这事闹到议会去；可一到了院子，他就冲着警官嘻嘻哈哈地笑了，那得意劲儿简直没法说。
foreignization: 说了这最后几句话后，多德杰让人抓住衣领带走了；直到被带到院子里，他还威胁要把这件事弄成一个议会问题；然后他对着警官的脸露出了非常高兴和自鸣得意的笑容。

source: “I fear it is all too true,” said the old gentleman sorrowfully, after looking over the papers. “This is not much for your intelligence; but I would gladly have given you treble the money, if it had been favourable to the boy.”
domestication: 老先生看完文件后，悲伤地说：“我怕这是真的。”他又叹了口气，说：“虽然这消息对你来说没啥用，但要是对那孩子有利，我就是出三倍的钱也心甘情愿。”
foreignization: “我恐怕这都是真的，”老先生悲伤地看完文件后说，“这个消息对你来说没什么价值；但如果这对那个男孩有利的话，我愿意给你三倍的钱。”

source: “Ay, that he shall,” replied Fagin, “and we’ll have a big-wig, Charley: one that’s got the greatest gift of the gab: to carry on his defence; and he shall make a speech for himself too, if he likes; and we’ll read it all in the papers—‘Artful Dodger—shrieks of laughter—here the court was convulsed’—eh, Charley, eh?”
domestication: “那当然，”费金回答说，“我们得给他找个大律师，查理，找一个嘴皮子最厉害的；他还想自己辩护呢，要是他乐意的话；到时候咱们可以在报纸上看到——‘机灵鬼——哄堂大笑——法庭一片混乱’——对吧，查理，对吧？”
foreignization: “嗯，他会的，”费金回答，“我们还会给他找个大律师，查理：一个口才最好的；让他来为他辩护；如果他愿意，他自己也可以发表演讲；我们会在报纸上看到这一切——‘机灵鬼——哄堂大笑——法庭一片混乱’——是吧，查理，是吧？”

source: “Wide,” replied Toby, after peeping in to satisfy himself. “The game of that is, that they always leave it open with a catch, so that the dog, who’s got a bed in here, may walk up and down the passage when he feels wakeful. Ha! ha! Barney ’ticed him away tonight. So neat!”
domestication: 托比探头看了一眼，确认后答道：“挺宽敞的。他们总是留着门不关，但又卡住一点，这样狗晚上睡不着时，就能在这条过道里走来走去。哈哈！巴尼今晚引开它了，真机灵！”
foreignization: “宽，”托比窥视了一眼以确认后回答道。“这个游戏的妙处在于，他们总是开着门，但会用一个门扣固定住，这样狗就可以在感到清醒时进出通道。哈！哈！巴尼今晚引诱他走开了。真巧妙！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: Disturbed by these different reflections; inclining now to one course and then to another, and again recoiling from all, as each successive consideration presented itself to her mind; Rose passed a sleepless and anxious night. After more communing with herself next day, she arrived at the desperate conclusion of consulting Harry.
domestication: 这些纷乱的思绪搅得罗斯心神不宁，一会儿倾向于这样做，一会儿又想那样做，每想到一个新问题又马上退缩，就这样翻来覆去，一夜都没合眼。第二天，她左思右想，最后咬牙决定去找哈里商量。
foreignization: 受到这些不同想法的困扰；现在倾向于这一条路，然后又倾向于另一条，再次从所有选择中退缩，因为每一个相继出现的想法呈现在她脑海中；罗丝度过了一个不眠而焦虑的夜晚。在第二天与自己更多的交流之后，她得出了一个绝望的结论，那就是去咨询哈里。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

After more communing with herself next day, she arrived at the desperate conclusion of consulting Harry.

source: Weak, and thin, and pallid, he awoke at last from what seemed to have been a long and troubled dream. Feebly raising himself in the bed, with his head resting on his trembling arm, he looked anxiously around.
domestication: 他终于从一场漫长而纷乱的梦中醒来，虚弱、消瘦、脸色苍白。他有气无力地在床上撑起身子，头靠在发抖的胳膊上，焦急地环顾四周。
foreignization: 虚弱、消瘦、面色苍白，他终于从似乎是一个漫长而不安的梦中醒来。无力地在床上撑起身体，头靠在颤抖的胳膊上，他焦急地环顾四周。

source: All this time, Mr. Giles, with the white nightcap on, had been sitting on the steps of the chaise, supporting an elbow on each knee, and wiping his eyes with a blue cotton pocket-handkerchief dotted with white spots. That the honest fellow had not been feigning emotion, was abundantly demonstrated by the very red eyes with which he regarded the young gentleman, when he turned round and addressed him.
domestication: 这工夫，吉尔斯先生戴着白睡帽，坐在马车的台阶上，两肘支在膝盖上，用一条白点蓝棉手帕抹着眼泪。他可不是装模作样，当年轻人转过身来跟他说话时，那双红得厉害的眼睛就是明证。
foreignization: 所有这段时间，吉尔斯先生戴着白色的睡帽，一直坐在四轮马车的台阶上，每只膝盖上支着一只胳膊肘，用一块点缀着白色斑点的蓝色棉手帕擦着眼睛。当他转过身来对那位年轻人说话时，那双非常红的眼睛充分证明了这位诚实的伙计并非假装动情。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

All this time, Mr. Giles, with the white nightcap on, had been sitting on the steps of the chaise, supporting an elbow on each knee, and wiping his eyes with a blue cotton pocket-handkerchief dotted with white spots. That the honest fellow had not been feigning emotion, was abundantly demonstrated by the very red eyes with which he regarded the young gentleman, when he turned round and addressed him.

That the honest fellow had not been feigning emotion, was abundantly demonstrated by the very red eyes with which he regarded the young gentleman, when he turned round and addressed him.

source: “It!” replied the woman, laying her hand over the other’s mouth. “The only thing she had. She wanted clothes to keep her warm, and food to eat; but she had kept it safe, and had it in her bosom. It was gold, I tell you! Rich gold, that might have saved her life!”
domestication: “就是这个！”女人说着，一手捂住了对方的嘴，“这是她唯一拥有的东西。她想要衣服保暖，想有吃的果腹；但她一直小心保管着，贴身藏着。是金子，我跟你说！是值钱的金子，能救她一命的金子！”
foreignization: “它！”那女人回答，用手捂住了另一个人的嘴。“她唯一拥有的东西。她想要衣服保暖，想要食物充饥；但她一直保存着它，藏在她的怀里。那是金子，我告诉你！是能救她命的黄金！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“It!” replied the woman, laying her hand over the other’s mouth. “The only thing she had. She wanted clothes to keep her warm, and food to eat; but she had kept it safe, and had it in her bosom. It was gold, I tell you! Rich gold, that might have saved her life!”
“就是这个！”女人说着，一手捂住了对方的嘴，“这是她唯一拥有的东西。她想要衣服保暖，想有吃的果腹；但她一直小心保管着，贴身藏着。是金子，我跟你说！是值钱的金子，能救她一命的金子！”

“它！”那女人回答，用手捂住了另一个人的嘴。“她唯一拥有的东西。她想要衣服保暖，想要食物充饥；但她一直保存着它，藏在她的怀里。那是金子，我告诉你！是能救她命的黄金！”

source: Oliver dozed off again, soon after this; when he awoke, it was nearly twelve o’clock. The old lady tenderly bade him good-night shortly afterwards, and left him in charge of a fat old woman who had just come: bringing with her, in a little bundle, a small Prayer Book and a large nightcap. Putting the latter on her head and the former on the table, the old woman, after telling Oliver that she had come to sit up with him, drew her chair close to the fire and went off into a series of short naps, chequered at frequent intervals with sundry tumblings forward, and divers moans and chokings. These, however, had no worse effect than causing her to rub her nose very hard, and then fall asleep again.
domestication: 奥利弗打了个盹，没过多久又睡着了；等他醒来时，已经快十二点了。老太太随后温柔地跟他道了晚安，就把他交给了一位刚来的胖老太照看。这位胖老太带了个小包裹，里面有一本小小的祈祷书和一个大大的睡帽。她把睡帽往头上一戴，把祈祷书往桌上一放，告诉奥利弗她是来陪他的，然后就把椅子挪到火炉边，开始打起一连串的小盹。其间，她不时往前一栽，发出阵阵呻吟和哽咽声。不过这些都没什么大碍，顶多是让她用力揉揉鼻子，然后又继续睡去。
foreignization: 奥利弗在这之后不久又打起了盹；当他醒来时，已经快到十二点了。老夫人随后不久亲切地与他道了晚安，并把他交给了一位刚来的胖老妇人照看：她带来了一个小包裹，里面有一本小祈祷书和一个大睡帽。老妇人把睡帽戴在头上，把祈祷书放在桌子上，告诉奥利弗她来陪他过夜后，便把椅子拉近火炉旁，开始进入了一系列短暂的小憩，期间频繁地向前倾倒，伴随着各种呻吟和窒息声。然而，这些情况的最坏结果也只是让她用力揉搓鼻子，然后再次入睡。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver dozed off again, soon after this; when he awoke, it was nearly twelve o’clock. The old lady tenderly bade him good-night shortly afterwards, and left him in charge of a fat old woman who had just come: bringing with her, in a little bundle, a small Prayer Book and a large nightcap. Putting the latter on her head and the former on the table, the old woman, after telling Oliver that she had come to sit up with him, drew her chair close to the fire and went off into a series of short naps, chequered at frequent intervals with sundry tumblings forward, and divers moans and chokings. These, however, had no worse effect than causing her to rub her nose very hard, and then fall asleep again.
